-- | The winner of a <a>race</a>: <a>LeftWon</a> means the left task
--   finished first, <a>RightWon</a> means the right task finished first.
data RaceWinner a b
LeftWon :: a -> RaceWinner a b
RightWon :: b -> RaceWinner a b
run :: Show err => Task err result -> Task err (AsyncTask err result)
waitFor :: Show err => AsyncTask err result -> Task err result

-- | Wait for an async task to complete without re-throwing its exceptions.
--   
--   Unlike <a>waitFor</a> which re-throws the task's exception into the
--   calling thread, <a>waitCatch</a> returns the outcome as a
--   <a>Result</a>. This is essential when waiting on tasks that were
--   intentionally cancelled (e.g., during shutdown), where re-throwing
--   <tt>AsyncCancelled</tt> would be incorrect.
waitCatch :: Show err => AsyncTask err result -> Task err2 (Result Text result)

-- | Cancel a running async task.
--   
--   This forcefully terminates the task. Use with caution as it may leave
--   resources in an inconsistent state if the task doesn't handle
--   cancellation.
cancel :: AsyncTask err result -> Task Text Unit
sleep :: Int -> Task w Unit
process :: Show err => Task err a -> (AsyncTask err a -> Task err b) -> Task err b

-- | Run two Tasks concurrently; return the result of whichever finishes
--   first. The loser is cancelled. Errors in the winner propagate; errors
--   in the loser are ignored. Mirrors the semantics of <a>race</a> but
--   stays inside the Task error channel.
race :: Show err => Task err a -> Task err b -> Task err (RaceWinner a b)
runConcurrently :: Show err => (Task err a, Task err b) -> Task err (a, b)

-- | Execute multiple tasks in parallel, like Promise.all() in
--   JavaScript/TypeScript.
--   
--   This function takes an array of tasks and runs them all at the same
--   time (concurrently), rather than waiting for each one to finish before
--   starting the next (sequentially).
--   
--   Think of it like this: - Sequential (one after another): Total time =
--   task1 time + task2 time + task3 time - Concurrent (all at once): Total
--   time ≈ longest task time
--   
--   Note: If any task fails, this function continues executing the other
--   tasks and ignores the failure. This is useful for scenarios like
--   dispatching events to multiple listeners where one listener failing
--   shouldn't prevent others from receiving the event.
runAllIgnoringErrors :: Show err => Array (Task err a) -> Task w Unit
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq a, GHC.Classes.Eq b) => GHC.Classes.Eq (AsyncTask.RaceWinner a b)
instance (GHC.Show.Show a, GHC.Show.Show b) => GHC.Show.Show (AsyncTask.RaceWinner a b)

module Accumulator

-- | <a>Accumulator</a> is a type that allows to accumulate values in a
--   monadic way. As long as the content of the accumulator implements the
--   <tt>Combinable</tt> and <tt>HasDefault</tt> traits, it is possible to
--   use the `do` notation to accumulate values.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell foo :: Array Int foo = Accumulator.accumulate do
--   Accumulator.push [1] Accumulator.push [2] Accumulator.push [3] ```
type Accumulator value = AccumulatorDsl value ()
data AccumulatorDsl someType result
AccumulatorDsl :: State someType result -> AccumulatorDsl someType result
[value] :: AccumulatorDsl someType result -> State someType result

-- | Pushes a value into the accumulator.
push :: Appendable value => value -> Accumulator value
accumulate :: Default value => Accumulator value -> value

-- | Updates the accumulator with a callback.
update :: (value -> value) -> Accumulator value
andThen :: (input -> AccumulatorDsl someType output) -> AccumulatorDsl someType input -> AccumulatorDsl someType output
yield :: value -> AccumulatorDsl someType value

module Map

-- | A Map from keys <tt>k</tt> to values <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   The <a>Semigroup</a> operation for <a>Map</a> is <a>union</a>, which
--   prefers values from the left operand. If <tt>m1</tt> maps a key
--   <tt>k</tt> to a value <tt>a1</tt>, and <tt>m2</tt> maps the same key
--   to a different value <tt>a2</tt>, then their union <tt>m1 &lt;&gt;
--   m2</tt> maps <tt>k</tt> to <tt>a1</tt>.
data Map k a

-- | A builder API like this can be easily implemented through the usage of
--   the <a>Accumulator</a> module. <a>--&gt;</a> is an operator that will
--   push a tuple into the accumulator list. After that, the <a>build</a>
--   function will convert the accumulator list into a <a>Map</a>.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell Map.build do "a" --&gt; 1 "b" --&gt; 2 "c" --&gt; 3 ```
build :: Accumulator (Map key value) -> Map key value

-- | &lt;math&gt;. The empty map.
--   
--   <pre>
--   empty      == fromList []
--   size empty == 0
--   </pre>
empty :: Map k a

-- | Creates a Map from an Array of key-value tuples.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   ```haskell Map.fromArray [("a", 1), ("b", 2), ("c", 3)] ```
fromArray :: (Eq key, Ord key) => Array (key, value) -> Map key value

-- | Accumulator operator to build a <a>Map</a>
(-->) :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Accumulator (Map key value)

-- | Set a value in a <a>Map</a>.
set :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Get the value from a <a>Map</a>.
get :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> Map key value -> Maybe value

-- | Reduce a <a>Map</a>.
reduce :: acc -> (key -> value -> acc -> acc) -> Map key value -> acc

-- | Merge two <a>Map</a>s.
merge :: (Eq key, Ord key) => Map key value -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Converts a map to an array of tuples
entries :: Map key value -> Array (key, value)

-- | Checks if a key exists in a map
contains :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> Map key value -> Bool

-- | Removes an element from the map
remove :: Ord key => key -> Map key value -> Map key value

-- | Get the value from a <a>Map</a>, defaulting to a value
getOrElse :: (Eq key, Ord key) => key -> value -> Map key value -> value

-- | Returns the length of the map
length :: Map key value -> Int

-- | Gets all the values in a map
values :: Map key value -> Array value

-- | Gets all the keys in a map
keys :: Map key value -> Array key

-- | Maps a function over the values in a map
mapValues :: (valueA -> valueB) -> Map key valueA -> Map key valueB

module Log
data Level
Debug :: Level
Info :: Level
Warn :: Level
Error :: Level
debug :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
info :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
warn :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
critical :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task w Unit
withScope :: Array (Text, Text) -> Task err value -> Task err value
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Log.Level
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Log.Level
instance GHC.Show.Show Log.Level


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.StateToken
--   
--   HMAC-signed state tokens for OAuth2 CSRF protection.
--   
--   State tokens are used in the OAuth2 Authorization Code flow to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Prevent CSRF attacks (cryptographically random nonce)</li>
--   <li>Prevent replay attacks (one-time use enforced by
--   TransactionStore)</li>
--   <li>Prevent mix-up attacks (provider name in payload)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>Security Properties</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>HMAC-SHA256 signature prevents tampering</li>
--   <li>Constant-time comparison prevents timing attacks</li>
--   <li>TTL enforcement prevents stale tokens</li>
--   <li>Clock skew tolerance (60s) handles minor time drift</li>
--   <li>NO PII in token (userId stored server-side in
--   TransactionStore)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- At <i>connect</i>{provider}
--   key &lt;- getHmacKey  -- From environment/config (MUST be persistent!)
--   nonce &lt;- StateToken.generateNonce
--   let payload = StatePayload
--         { provider = "oura"
--         , nonce = nonce
--         , issuedAt = now
--         , expiresAt = now + 300  -- 5 minutes
--         }
--   stateToken &lt;- StateToken.encodeStateToken key payload
--   -- Store userId in TransactionStore alongside PKCE verifier
--   
--   -- At <i>callback</i>{provider}
--   payload &lt;- StateToken.decodeStateToken key currentTime stateToken
--   -- Verify payload.provider matches route
--   -- Retrieve userId from TransactionStore
--   </pre>
module Auth.OAuth2.StateToken

-- | HMAC key for signing state tokens.
--   
--   Must be at least 32 bytes for HMAC-SHA256 security. Show instance is
--   redacted to prevent key leakage.
data HmacKey

-- | Payload carried in the state token.
--   
--   All fields are validated on decode to ensure token integrity.
--   
--   SECURITY: This payload is signed but NOT encrypted - it can be read by
--   anyone who intercepts it (including the OAuth provider). Therefore, NO
--   PII (like userId) should be stored here. Store userId server-side in
--   the TransactionStore instead.
data StatePayload
StatePayload :: Text -> Text -> Int -> Int -> StatePayload

-- | OAuth2 provider name (e.g., "oura", "github") Used to prevent mix-up
--   attacks
[provider] :: StatePayload -> Text

-- | Cryptographically random nonce for CSRF protection Generated via
--   <a>generateNonce</a>, at least 16 bytes of entropy
[nonce] :: StatePayload -> Text

-- | Unix timestamp when token was issued
[issuedAt] :: StatePayload -> Int

-- | Unix timestamp when token expires
[expiresAt] :: StatePayload -> Int

-- | Errors that can occur during state token operations.
data StateTokenError

-- | HMAC signature does not match (tampering detected)
SignatureInvalid :: StateTokenError

-- | Token has expired (expiresAt &lt; currentTime)
TokenExpired :: StateTokenError

-- | Token issuedAt is too far in the future (clock skew &gt; 60s)
TokenNotYetValid :: StateTokenError

-- | Token format is invalid (not base64, too short, bad JSON)
MalformedToken :: StateTokenError

-- | Token timestamps are invalid (expiresAt &lt; issuedAt)
TokenInvalidTimestamps :: StateTokenError

-- | Create an HMAC key from a secret.
--   
--   The secret must be at least 32 bytes (256 bits) for security. In
--   production, load this from an environment variable or secrets manager.
--   
--   <pre>
--   key &lt;- case mkHmacKey (Environment.get <a>OAUTH2_STATE_KEY</a>) of
--     Err err -&gt; panic err
--     Ok k -&gt; pure k
--   </pre>
mkHmacKey :: Text -> Result Text HmacKey

-- | Generate a cryptographically secure random HMAC key.
--   
--   Creates a 32-byte (256-bit) key suitable for HMAC-SHA256.
--   
--   WARNING: In production, load a persistent key from environment/config
--   using <a>mkHmacKey</a> instead. Generating a new key at runtime means
--   all in-flight OAuth flows will break on restart.
--   
--   <pre>
--   key &lt;- StateToken.generateKey
--   </pre>
generateKey :: Task err HmacKey

-- | Generate a cryptographically secure random nonce.
--   
--   Creates a 16-byte (128-bit) random value encoded as base64url. This
--   provides sufficient entropy for CSRF protection.
--   
--   <pre>
--   nonce &lt;- StateToken.generateNonce
--   </pre>
generateNonce :: Task err Text

-- | Encode a state payload into a signed token.
--   
--   Format: base64url(json_payload || hmac_sha256(json_payload))
--   
--   The token is URL-safe and can be passed as a query parameter.
encodeStateToken :: HmacKey -> StatePayload -> Task error Text

-- | Decode and verify a signed state token.
--   
--   Performs the following validations:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Base64url decoding</li>
--   <li>HMAC-SHA256 signature verification (constant-time)</li>
--   <li>JSON payload parsing</li>
--   <li>TTL validation (expiresAt &gt;= currentTime)</li>
--   <li>Clock skew validation (issuedAt &lt;= currentTime + 60s)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   Returns the decoded payload if all validations pass.
decodeStateToken :: HmacKey -> Int -> Text -> Task StateTokenError StatePayload

-- | Constant-time comparison for Text values.
--   
--   SECURITY: {-# INLINE constEq #-} is MANDATORY — without inlining, GHC
--   may reorder the comparison operations, defeating the constant-time
--   guarantee. This prevents timing oracle attacks where an attacker
--   measures response time to infer information about the compared values.
--   
--   Uses <a>constEq</a> from the <tt>memory</tt> package, which performs
--   byte-by-byte comparison without short-circuiting.
constEq :: Text -> Text -> Bool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.HmacKey
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StatePayload
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StateTokenError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StatePayload
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StatePayload
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StateTokenError
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.HmacKey
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StatePayload
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StateTokenError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.StateToken.StatePayload


-- | Core OAuth2 types for client-side OAuth2 flows.
--   
--   These types support the Authorization Code flow for connecting to
--   external APIs (Oura, GitHub, Google, etc.).
--   
--   <h1>Security Notes</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Tokens should be stored in <tt>SecretStore</tt>, NOT in the event
--   store</li>
--   <li><a>State</a> parameter is mandatory for CSRF protection</li>
--   <li><a>ClientSecret</a> should never be logged or serialized to
--   events</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.OAuth2.Types

-- | OAuth2 provider configuration.
--   
--   Contains the endpoints needed for the Authorization Code flow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   ouraProvider :: Provider
--   ouraProvider = Provider
--     { name = "oura"
--     , authorizeEndpoint = "<a>https://cloud.ouraring.com/oauth/authorize"</a>
--     , tokenEndpoint = "<a>https://api.ouraring.com/oauth/token"</a>
--     }
--   </pre>
data Provider
Provider :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Provider
[name] :: Provider -> Text
[authorizeEndpoint] :: Provider -> Text
[tokenEndpoint] :: Provider -> Text

-- | A provider whose endpoints have been validated.
--   
--   This type proves that both the authorize and token endpoints have
--   passed SSRF validation (HTTPS required, no private IPs, DNS checked).
--   Use <tt>validateProvider</tt> to construct a ValidatedProvider.
--   
--   SECURITY: Once validated at startup, token requests skip redundant
--   validation, improving performance at scale while maintaining security
--   guarantees.
--   
--   Constructor is intentionally not exported - use
--   <tt>validateProvider</tt> in Client.hs.
data ValidatedProvider

-- | Get the underlying Provider from a ValidatedProvider. Use this when
--   you need access to the provider's name or endpoints.
getValidatedProvider :: ValidatedProvider -> Provider

-- | INTERNAL: Construct a ValidatedProvider without validation.
--   
--   WARNING: This function bypasses SSRF protection. Only use in:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>validateProvider</a> (after performing validation)</li>
--   <li>Test code (for creating test fixtures with mock providers)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   NEVER use this in application code. Always use
--   <tt>validateProvider</tt> instead.
--   
--   Using this function incorrectly can expose your application to SSRF
--   attacks against internal infrastructure (AWS metadata service,
--   Kubernetes API, etc.)
unsafeValidatedProvider :: Provider -> ValidatedProvider

-- | OAuth2 client identifier.
newtype ClientId
ClientId :: Text -> ClientId

-- | OAuth2 client secret.
--   
--   WARNING: Never log or serialize this to events. Show instance is
--   redacted for security. No JSON instances - use explicit conversion if
--   needed. Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkClientSecret</a> and
--   <a>unwrapClientSecret</a>.
data ClientSecret

-- | Create a ClientSecret from raw text. Use with caution - the secret
--   should come from secure configuration.
mkClientSecret :: Text -> ClientSecret

-- | Extract the raw secret text. Use with caution. Only use this when you
--   need to send the secret to the OAuth2 provider.
unwrapClientSecret :: ClientSecret -> Text

-- | OAuth2 redirect URI (callback URL).
--   
--   SECURITY: Must use HTTPS in production (except localhost for
--   development). Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkRedirectUri</a> and
--   <a>unwrapRedirectUri</a>.
data RedirectUri

-- | Create a RedirectUri with HTTPS validation.
--   
--   SECURITY: Redirect URIs MUST use HTTPS to prevent authorization code
--   theft. The only exception is localhost (http:/<i>localhost,
--   http:</i><i>127.0.0.1, http:</i>/[::1]) which is allowed for
--   development/testing.
--   
--   Returns <a>InvalidRedirectUri</a> error if validation fails.
mkRedirectUri :: Text -> Result OAuth2Error RedirectUri

-- | Extract the raw redirect URI text.
unwrapRedirectUri :: RedirectUri -> Text

-- | Authorization code received from the OAuth2 provider.
--   
--   This is exchanged for tokens via <a>exchangeCode</a>. Show instance is
--   redacted for security (codes are secrets). No ToJSON instance -
--   prevent accidental serialization. Constructor is hidden - use
--   <a>mkAuthorizationCode</a> and <a>unwrapAuthorizationCode</a>.
data AuthorizationCode

-- | Create an AuthorizationCode from raw text. Typically from the OAuth2
--   callback query parameter.
mkAuthorizationCode :: Text -> AuthorizationCode

-- | Extract the raw authorization code text. Only use this when exchanging
--   for tokens.
unwrapAuthorizationCode :: AuthorizationCode -> Text

-- | State parameter for CSRF protection.
--   
--   SECURITY: Must be: * Cryptographically random * Stored in session
--   before redirect * Validated on callback before token exchange *
--   Single-use (rejected if reused)
--   
--   Show instance is redacted for security (state tokens are secrets). No
--   ToJSON instance - prevent accidental serialization. Constructor is
--   hidden - use <a>mkState</a> and <a>unwrapState</a>.
data State

-- | Create a State from raw text. The text should be cryptographically
--   random.
mkState :: Text -> State

-- | Extract the raw state text. Only use this for URL building or
--   validation.
unwrapState :: State -> Text

-- | OAuth2 scope(s) being requested.
newtype Scope
Scope :: Text -> Scope

-- | PKCE code verifier (RFC 7636).
--   
--   A cryptographically random string used as the secret in PKCE. Must be
--   43-128 characters from [A-Z], [a-z], [0-9], "-", ".", "_", "~". Show
--   instance is redacted for security. No ToJSON instance - prevent
--   accidental serialization. Constructor is hidden - use
--   <a>mkCodeVerifier</a> and <a>unwrapCodeVerifier</a>.
data CodeVerifier

-- | Create a CodeVerifier from raw text with validation (RFC 7636).
--   
--   Validates: * Length is 43-128 characters * All characters are from the
--   unreserved set: [A-Z], [a-z], [0-9], "-", ".", "_", "~"
--   
--   Returns <a>InvalidPkceVerifier</a> error if validation fails.
mkCodeVerifier :: Text -> Result OAuth2Error CodeVerifier

-- | Create a CodeVerifier without validation (UNSAFE).
--   
--   Only use this when you trust the source (e.g., from
--   <tt>generateCodeVerifier</tt>). For user input, use
--   <a>mkCodeVerifier</a> which validates.
mkCodeVerifierUnsafe :: Text -> CodeVerifier

-- | Extract the raw code verifier text. Only use this when sending to the
--   token endpoint.
unwrapCodeVerifier :: CodeVerifier -> Text

-- | PKCE code challenge (RFC 7636).
--   
--   Derived from CodeVerifier using SHA256 + base64url encoding. Sent to
--   authorization endpoint; verifier is sent to token endpoint.
newtype CodeChallenge
CodeChallenge :: Text -> CodeChallenge

-- | PKCE code challenge method. S256 is recommended; Plain should only be
--   used when SHA256 is unavailable.
data CodeChallengeMethod

-- | SHA256 hash of verifier (recommended)
S256 :: CodeChallengeMethod

-- | Plain text verifier (not recommended)
Plain :: CodeChallengeMethod

-- | OAuth2 access token.
--   
--   Used in Authorization header: <tt>Authorization: Bearer
--   &lt;token&gt;</tt> Show instance is redacted for security. No JSON
--   instances - use explicit conversion if needed. Constructor is hidden -
--   use <a>mkAccessToken</a> and <a>unwrapAccessToken</a>.
data AccessToken

-- | Create an AccessToken from raw text. Typically from an OAuth2 token
--   response.
mkAccessToken :: Text -> AccessToken

-- | Extract the raw access token text. Only use this when making
--   authenticated API requests.
unwrapAccessToken :: AccessToken -> Text

-- | OAuth2 refresh token.
--   
--   Used to obtain new access tokens without user interaction. Some
--   providers rotate refresh tokens on each use. Show instance is redacted
--   for security. No JSON instances - use explicit conversion if needed.
--   Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkRefreshToken</a> and
--   <a>unwrapRefreshToken</a>.
data RefreshToken

-- | Create a RefreshToken from raw text. Typically from an OAuth2 token
--   response.
mkRefreshToken :: Text -> RefreshToken

-- | Extract the raw refresh token text. Only use this when refreshing
--   tokens with the OAuth2 provider.
unwrapRefreshToken :: RefreshToken -> Text

-- | Complete set of tokens from an OAuth2 token response.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case maybeTokenSet of
--     Just tokens -&gt; do
--       let (AccessToken bearer) = tokens.accessToken
--       Http.request
--         |&gt; Http.addHeader <a>Authorization</a> [fmt|Bearer #{bearer}|]
--         |&gt; Http.get
--     Nothing -&gt; Task.throw "Not connected"
--   </pre>
--   
--   Show instance is redacted (contains secrets). No JSON instances -
--   tokens should be stored via SecretStore, not serialized.
data TokenSet
TokenSet :: AccessToken -> Maybe RefreshToken -> Maybe Int -> TokenSet
[accessToken] :: TokenSet -> AccessToken
[refreshToken] :: TokenSet -> Maybe RefreshToken

-- | Duration in seconds until access token expires (from OAuth2
--   expires_in). This is NOT a unix timestamp - it's a duration from the
--   time of issuance. To get expiry time: currentTime + expiresInSeconds
[expiresInSeconds] :: TokenSet -> Maybe Int

-- | Errors that can occur during OAuth2 operations.
data OAuth2Error

-- | Token request failed (network or HTTP error)
TokenRequestFailed :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Invalid grant (code expired, already used, etc.)
InvalidGrant :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Invalid client credentials
InvalidClient :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Requested scope was denied
ScopeDenied :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Token response was malformed
MalformedResponse :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Network error during token exchange
NetworkError :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Endpoint URL failed security validation (HTTPS/SSRF)
EndpointValidationFailed :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | State parameter validation failed (CSRF protection)
InvalidState :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | PKCE verifier validation failed
InvalidPkceVerifier :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Redirect URI validation failed
InvalidRedirectUri :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Validate that the returned state matches the expected state. SECURITY:
--   This is critical for CSRF protection. Always validate state before
--   exchanging the authorization code for tokens.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case validateState expectedState returnedState of
--     Ok () -&gt; OAuth2.exchangeCode provider clientId secret redirectUri code
--     Err err -&gt; Task.throw err
--   </pre>
validateState :: State -> State -> Result OAuth2Error ()

-- | Validate that a redirect URI matches the expected registered URI.
--   SECURITY: Redirect URI validation is critical to prevent authorization
--   code theft.
--   
--   Validates: 1. Exact string match (no normalization that could allow
--   bypasses) 2. No fragment component (fragments are forbidden in
--   redirect URIs per RFC 6749) 3. Uses HTTPS (except localhost for
--   development)
--   
--   <pre>
--   case validateRedirectUri registeredUri receivedUri of
--     Ok () -&gt; -- proceed with token exchange
--     Err err -&gt; -- reject the request
--   </pre>
validateRedirectUri :: RedirectUri -> RedirectUri -> Result OAuth2Error ()
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.AccessToken
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.AuthorizationCode
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientId
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientSecret
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallenge
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallengeMethod
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeVerifier
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.OAuth2Error
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.Provider
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.RedirectUri
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.RefreshToken
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.Scope
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.State
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.TokenSet
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Types.ValidatedProvider
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.AuthorizationCode
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientId
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallenge
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallengeMethod
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeVerifier
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.OAuth2Error
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.Provider
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.RedirectUri
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.Scope
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.State
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.AccessToken
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.AuthorizationCode
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientId
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientSecret
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallenge
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallengeMethod
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeVerifier
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.OAuth2Error
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.Provider
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.RedirectUri
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.RefreshToken
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.Scope
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.State
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Types.TokenSet
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.AccessToken
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.AuthorizationCode
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientId
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientSecret
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallenge
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallengeMethod
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeVerifier
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.OAuth2Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.Provider
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.RedirectUri
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.RefreshToken
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.Scope
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.State
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.TokenSet
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Types.ValidatedProvider
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.ClientId
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallenge
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.CodeChallengeMethod
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.OAuth2Error
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.Provider
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.RedirectUri
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.OAuth2.Types.Scope


-- | Secure storage interface for OAuth2 tokens.
--   
--   This module provides an interface for storing sensitive credentials
--   like OAuth2 tokens. Tokens should NEVER be stored in the event store.
--   
--   <h1>Why SecretStore?</h1>
--   
--   Event stores are append-only and often replicated to logs/backups.
--   Storing tokens there means:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Cannot truly delete tokens on revocation</li>
--   <li>Tokens end up in backups and debug logs</li>
--   <li>Replay tooling becomes a secret-exfiltration path</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   SecretStore provides proper secret lifecycle (create, update, delete).
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Create a store (in-memory for development)
--   store &lt;- SecretStore.InMemory.new
--   
--   -- Store tokens after OAuth2 exchange
--   let key = TokenKey [fmt|oauth2:oura:#{userId}|]
--   store.put key tokens
--   
--   -- Retrieve tokens
--   maybeTokens &lt;- store.get key
--   
--   -- Delete on disconnect
--   store.delete key
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Implementations</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>InMemory</a> - Development only, single process</li>
--   <li>(Future) Postgres, Vault, etc.</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.SecretStore

-- | Key for storing tokens.
--   
--   Typically includes provider and user/entity identifier:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let key = TokenKey [fmt|oauth2:{userId}|]
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: Show instance is redacted to prevent PII leakage in logs.
--   Keys often contain user IDs or other identifying information.
newtype TokenKey
TokenKey :: Text -> TokenKey

-- | Interface for secure token storage.
--   
--   Implementations must ensure:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Tokens are not logged</li>
--   <li>Tokens can be deleted (not append-only)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   data SecretStore = SecretStore
--     { get :: TokenKey -&gt; Task Text (Maybe TokenSet)
--     , put :: TokenKey -&gt; TokenSet -&gt; Task Text Unit
--     , delete :: TokenKey -&gt; Task Text Unit
--     }
--   </pre>
data SecretStore
SecretStore :: (TokenKey -> Task Text (Maybe TokenSet)) -> (TokenKey -> TokenSet -> Task Text Unit) -> (TokenKey -> Task Text Unit) -> (TokenKey -> (Maybe TokenSet -> Maybe TokenSet) -> Task Text Unit) -> (forall result. () => TokenKey -> (Maybe TokenSet -> Task Text (Maybe TokenSet, result)) -> Task Text result) -> SecretStore

-- | Retrieve tokens by key. Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if no tokens are
--   stored for this key.
[get] :: SecretStore -> TokenKey -> Task Text (Maybe TokenSet)

-- | Store tokens for a key. Overwrites any existing tokens.
[put] :: SecretStore -> TokenKey -> TokenSet -> Task Text Unit

-- | Delete tokens for a key. No-op if key doesn't exist.
[delete] :: SecretStore -> TokenKey -> Task Text Unit

-- | Atomically modify tokens for a key. The transformation function
--   receives the current value (or <tt>Nothing</tt>) and returns the new
--   value (or <tt>Nothing</tt> to delete). Enables read-modify-write and
--   compare-and-swap patterns.
[atomicModify] :: SecretStore -> TokenKey -> (Maybe TokenSet -> Maybe TokenSet) -> Task Text Unit

-- | Atomically modify tokens and return a result. The transformation
--   function receives the current value (or <tt>Nothing</tt>) and returns
--   the new value (or <tt>Nothing</tt> to delete) AND a result value.
--   Enables atomic check-and-refresh patterns without TOCTOU races.
[atomicModifyReturning] :: SecretStore -> forall result. () => TokenKey -> (Maybe TokenSet -> Task Text (Maybe TokenSet, result)) -> Task Text result
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.SecretStore.TokenKey
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.SecretStore.TokenKey
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Auth.SecretStore.TokenKey
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Auth.SecretStore.TokenKey
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.SecretStore.TokenKey


-- | In-memory SecretStore implementation.
--   
--   WARNING: This implementation is for DEVELOPMENT ONLY.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Data is lost on process restart</li>
--   <li>Not suitable for multi-process deployments</li>
--   <li>Not encrypted at rest</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   For production, use a persistent implementation (Postgres, Vault,
--   etc.).
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   store &lt;- SecretStore.InMemory.new
--   
--   let key = TokenKey "oauth2:oura:user-123"
--   store.put key tokens
--   maybeTokens &lt;- store.get key
--   </pre>
module Auth.SecretStore.InMemory

-- | Create a new in-memory SecretStore.
--   
--   WARNING: Development only. See module documentation.
--   
--   <pre>
--   store &lt;- SecretStore.InMemory.new
--   </pre>
new :: Task Text SecretStore


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore
--   
--   Short-lived storage for OAuth2 flow transactions.
--   
--   During the OAuth2 Authorization Code flow, we need to store temporary
--   data between the `<i>connect` and `</i>callback` requests:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>PKCE code verifier (needed to exchange the authorization
--   code)</li>
--   <li>Expiry time (for cleanup and validation)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Key Properties</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Short-lived**: Transactions expire after 5 minutes
--   (configurable)</li>
--   <li>**One-time use**: <a>consume</a> atomically retrieves and
--   deletes</li>
--   <li>**Replay protection**: Concurrent consume attempts - exactly one
--   succeeds</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Implementations</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>InMemory</a> - Development only (single instance)</li>
--   <li>Redis (future) - Production multi-instance deployments</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- At /connect (store verifier)
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   let tx = Transaction { verifier = pkceVerifier, expiresAt = now + 300 }
--   store.put key tx
--   
--   -- At /callback (consume verifier - one-time use)
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   maybeTx &lt;- store.consume key
--   case maybeTx of
--     Nothing -&gt; -- Replay attack or expired
--     Just tx -&gt; -- Use tx.verifier for token exchange
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Security Notes</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Keys are hashed via TransactionKey to prevent raw state token
--   storage</li>
--   <li>The store does NOT validate expiry - caller must check
--   <a>expiresAt</a></li>
--   <li>In-memory store breaks one-time use across multiple instances</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore

-- | A short-lived OAuth2 transaction.
--   
--   Stored between `<i>connect` and `</i>callback` requests. Contains all
--   data needed for the callback that should NOT be in the state token.
data Transaction
Transaction :: CodeVerifier -> Text -> Int -> Transaction

-- | PKCE code verifier for token exchange
[verifier] :: Transaction -> CodeVerifier

-- | User ID from JWT (stored server-side for GDPR compliance) This is NOT
--   in the state token to avoid PII exposure
[userId] :: Transaction -> Text

-- | Unix timestamp when this transaction expires Caller is responsible for
--   checking this
[expiresAt] :: Transaction -> Int

-- | Interface for OAuth2 transaction storage.
--   
--   Implementations must guarantee atomic <a>consume</a> semantics:
--   concurrent calls with the same key must return <tt>Just</tt> at most
--   once.
data TransactionStore
TransactionStore :: (TransactionKey -> Transaction -> Task Text Unit) -> (TransactionKey -> Task Text (Maybe Transaction)) -> (TransactionKey -> Task Text (Maybe Transaction)) -> (TransactionKey -> Task Text Unit) -> TransactionStore

-- | Store a transaction.
--   
--   If a transaction with the same key exists, it is overwritten.
[put] :: TransactionStore -> TransactionKey -> Transaction -> Task Text Unit

-- | Retrieve a transaction without consuming it.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if key doesn't exist. Does NOT check expiry -
--   caller must validate <a>expiresAt</a>.
[get] :: TransactionStore -> TransactionKey -> Task Text (Maybe Transaction)

-- | Atomically retrieve and delete a transaction.
--   
--   This is the critical operation for one-time use: - Returns `Just tx`
--   and deletes the entry atomically - Subsequent calls with same key
--   return <tt>Nothing</tt> - Concurrent calls: exactly one returns
--   <tt>Just</tt>
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if key doesn't exist (already consumed or
--   never stored).
[consume] :: TransactionStore -> TransactionKey -> Task Text (Maybe Transaction)

-- | Delete a transaction.
--   
--   No-op if key doesn't exist.
[delete] :: TransactionStore -> TransactionKey -> Task Text Unit

-- | A type-safe key for transaction storage.
--   
--   Keys are derived from state tokens via SHA256 hashing. The Show
--   instance is redacted to prevent key leakage in logs.
data TransactionKey

-- | Create a TransactionKey from a state token.
--   
--   The input is hashed using SHA256 and base64url-encoded to produce a
--   consistent, fixed-size key suitable for storage.
--   
--   <pre>
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   </pre>
fromText :: Text -> TransactionKey

-- | Get the underlying Text representation of the key.
--   
--   This returns the hashed value, not the original input. Useful for
--   storage backends that require Text keys.
toText :: TransactionKey -> Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.Transaction
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.Transaction
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.Transaction


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.Provider
--   
--   OAuth2 Provider configuration for integrating external OAuth2
--   providers (like Oura, GitHub, Google) into a NeoHaskell application.
--   
--   <h1>Overview</h1>
--   
--   This module provides the configuration types needed to set up OAuth2
--   provider integration. The actual route handlers are in <a>Routes</a>.
--   
--   <h1>Token Storage</h1>
--   
--   <b>Tokens are automatically stored</b> in <tt>SecretStore</tt> by the
--   framework. Your <a>onSuccess</a> callback receives a <a>TokenKey</a>
--   for later retrieval, NOT the raw tokens. This prevents accidental
--   token leakage.
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- STEP 1: Define callback command types
--   -- The TokenKey is provided by the framework - tokens are already stored!
--   
--   data OuraConnected = OuraConnected
--     { ouraUserId :: Text
--     , ouraTokenKey :: TokenKey  -- Framework provides this
--     }
--     deriving (Generic)
--   
--   instance Json.ToJSON OuraConnected
--   type instance NameOf OuraConnected = <a>OuraConnected</a>
--   
--   data OuraFailed = OuraFailed { userId :: Text, errorMessage :: Text }
--     deriving (Generic)
--   
--   instance Json.ToJSON OuraFailed
--   type instance NameOf OuraFailed = <a>OuraFailed</a>
--   
--   -- STEP 2: Configure the OAuth2 provider
--   ouraConfig :: OAuth2ProviderConfig
--   ouraConfig = OAuth2ProviderConfig
--     { provider = Provider
--         { name = "oura"
--         , authorizeEndpoint = "<a>https://cloud.ouraring.com/oauth/authorize"</a>
--         , tokenEndpoint = "<a>https://api.ouraring.com/oauth/token"</a>
--         }
--     , clientId = ClientId "your-client-id"
--     , clientSecret = mkClientSecret "your-client-secret"
--     , redirectUri = myRedirectUri  -- use Result.unwrap or handle Result from mkRedirectUri
--     , scopes = Array.fromLinkedList [Scope "personal"]
--     , onSuccess = \userId tokenKey -&gt;
--         -- tokenKey is already stored at "oauth:oura:{userId}"
--         Integration.encodeCommand (OuraConnected userId tokenKey)
--     , onFailure = \userId err -&gt; do
--         let errMsg = show err |&gt; Text.fromLinkedList
--         Integration.encodeCommand (OuraFailed userId errMsg)
--     , onDisconnect = \userId -&gt;
--         -- Tokens are auto-deleted from SecretStore by the framework
--         Integration.encodeCommand (OuraDisconnected userId)
--     , successRedirectUrl = "<a>https://yourapp.com/settings?connected=oura"</a>
--     , failureRedirectUrl = "<a>https://yourapp.com/settings?error=oauth"</a>
--     }
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Callback Format</h1>
--   
--   The callback functions (<a>onSuccess</a>, <a>onFailure</a>,
--   <a>onDisconnect</a>) must return JSON text in <a>CommandPayload</a>
--   format. Use <a>encodeCommand</a> to create this automatically from
--   your command types.
--   
--   The JSON format is: <tt>{ "commandType": <a>YourCommand</a>,
--   "commandData": {...} }</tt>
--   
--   <h1>Token Security</h1>
--   
--   Tokens are handled securely by the framework:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Tokens are <b>auto-stored</b> in <tt>SecretStore</tt> at key
--   <tt>oauth:{provider}:{userId}</tt></li>
--   <li>Your callback receives <a>TokenKey</a>, never raw
--   <tt>TokenSet</tt></li>
--   <li>Tokens are <b>auto-deleted</b> from SecretStore on disconnect</li>
--   <li><tt>TokenSet</tt> has no <tt>ToJSON</tt> instance (compile-time
--   protection)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Retrieving Tokens</h1>
--   
--   In your integrations, use <a>get</a> with the <a>TokenKey</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   maybeTokens &lt;- secretStore.get tokenKey
--   case maybeTokens of
--     Just tokens -&gt; do
--       let bearer = unwrapAccessToken tokens.accessToken
--       -- Use bearer token for API calls
--     Nothing -&gt; -- Token expired or revoked
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Security</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>All routes require JWT authentication (user must be logged
--   in)</li>
--   <li>State tokens are HMAC-signed to prevent tampering</li>
--   <li>PKCE is used for authorization code flow</li>
--   <li>One-time state consumption prevents replay attacks</li>
--   <li>Token types have no ToJSON (compile-time serialization
--   protection)</li>
--   <li>Tokens auto-stored in pluggable SecretStore (default:
--   in-memory)</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.OAuth2.Provider

-- | Configuration for an OAuth2 provider integration.
--   
--   This configures both the OAuth2 flow parameters and the
--   application-level callbacks for success/failure handling.
--   
--   The callbacks must return JSON in <a>CommandPayload</a> format. Use
--   <a>encodeCommand</a> to encode your command types correctly.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: Tokens are automatically stored in <tt>SecretStore</tt>
--   by the framework. Your <a>onSuccess</a> callback receives a
--   <a>TokenKey</a> for later retrieval, not raw tokens.
data OAuth2ProviderConfig
OAuth2ProviderConfig :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> (Text -> TokenKey -> Text) -> (Text -> OAuth2Error -> Text) -> (Text -> Text) -> Text -> Text -> OAuth2ProviderConfig

-- | OAuth2 provider endpoints
[provider] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Provider

-- | OAuth2 client ID (from provider dashboard)
[clientId] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> ClientId

-- | OAuth2 client secret (from provider dashboard, keep secure!)
[clientSecret] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> ClientSecret

-- | Callback URL (must match provider dashboard configuration)
[redirectUri] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> RedirectUri

-- | Scopes to request from the provider
[scopes] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Array Scope

-- | Called on successful token exchange. First arg is userId (from JWT),
--   second is the TokenKey for SecretStore lookup. Tokens are auto-stored
--   by the framework at key <tt>oauth:{provider}:{userId}</tt>. Returns
--   JSON via <a>encodeCommand</a>.
[onSuccess] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> TokenKey -> Text

-- | Called when OAuth2 flow fails. First arg is userId (from JWT), second
--   is the error. Returns JSON via <a>encodeCommand</a>.
[onFailure] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> OAuth2Error -> Text

-- | Called when user disconnects the provider. Arg is userId (from JWT).
--   Tokens are auto-deleted from SecretStore by the framework. Returns
--   JSON via <a>encodeCommand</a>.
[onDisconnect] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> Text

-- | URL to redirect user after successful connection
[successRedirectUrl] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text

-- | URL to redirect user after failed connection
[failureRedirectUrl] :: OAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text

-- | Runtime-validated OAuth2 provider configuration.
--   
--   This is created by validating an <a>OAuth2ProviderConfig</a> at
--   application startup. The <a>ValidatedProvider</a> field guarantees
--   that endpoint URLs have been validated for HTTPS and SSRF protection.
--   
--   This type is used internally by Routes to enable high-throughput token
--   operations (50k+ req/s) without repeated endpoint validation.
data ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig
ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig :: Provider -> ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> (Text -> TokenKey -> Text) -> (Text -> OAuth2Error -> Text) -> (Text -> Text) -> Text -> Text -> ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig

-- | The original unvalidated provider (for authorize URL generation)
[provider] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Provider

-- | Pre-validated provider for token operations
[validatedProvider] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> ValidatedProvider

-- | OAuth2 client ID
[clientId] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> ClientId

-- | OAuth2 client secret
[clientSecret] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> ClientSecret

-- | Callback URL
[redirectUri] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> RedirectUri

-- | Scopes to request
[scopes] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Array Scope

-- | Success callback - receives TokenKey (tokens auto-stored in
--   SecretStore)
[onSuccess] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> TokenKey -> Text

-- | Failure callback
[onFailure] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> OAuth2Error -> Text

-- | Disconnect callback
[onDisconnect] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text -> Text

-- | Success redirect URL
[successRedirectUrl] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text

-- | Failure redirect URL
[failureRedirectUrl] :: ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> Text

-- | Action returned by OAuth2 callbacks to be dispatched by the
--   application.
--   
--   These actions are type-erased commands that the application can
--   dispatch to its command handlers. This allows OAuth2 integration
--   without coupling to specific domain types.
data OAuth2Action

-- | Success action with JSON-encoded command
SuccessAction :: Text -> OAuth2Action

-- | Failure action with JSON-encoded command
FailureAction :: Text -> OAuth2Action

-- | Disconnect action with JSON-encoded command
DisconnectAction :: Text -> OAuth2Action
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Provider.OAuth2Action
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Provider.OAuth2Action
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Provider.OAuth2Action

module Http.Client
data Request
Request :: Maybe Text -> Map Text Text -> Maybe Int -> Int -> !Maybe Int -> Request
[url] :: Request -> Maybe Text
[headers] :: Request -> Map Text Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds (default: 10 seconds)
[timeoutSeconds] :: Request -> Maybe Int

-- | Maximum redirects to follow (default: 0 for SSRF protection)
[maxRedirects] :: Request -> Int

-- | Maximum response body size in bytes (default: 10MB). Enforced in
--   <a>getSecure</a> only. Other methods (<a>get</a>, <a>post</a>, etc.)
--   do not check this limit. The check is post-read (full body is fetched
--   first, then size is validated). For true OOM prevention, use a
--   streaming HTTP client. Set to Nothing to disable the limit (not
--   recommended for external APIs).
[maxResponseBytes] :: Request -> !Maybe Int
request :: Request
withUrl :: Text -> Request -> Request
addHeader :: Text -> Text -> Request -> Request

-- | Set request timeout in seconds. SECURITY: Always set timeouts for
--   external requests to prevent hangs. FAILS FAST: Raises error if
--   seconds &lt;= 0 to catch misuse at call site.
withTimeout :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | Set maximum number of redirects to follow. SECURITY: Default is 0 (no
--   redirects) to prevent SSRF bypass attacks. Only enable redirects for
--   trusted endpoints where redirect targets are known-safe. FAILS FAST:
--   Raises error if count &lt; 0 to catch misuse at call site.
withRedirects :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | Set the maximum response body size in bytes. Default is 10MB. Use this
--   to override for specific endpoints.
withMaxResponseSize :: Int -> Request -> Request

-- | HTTP response with status code, headers, and parsed body.
--   
--   All HTTP methods return this type, allowing access to response
--   metadata.
--   
--   <pre>
--   response &lt;- Http.get request
--   if response.statusCode == 200
--     then process response.body
--     else handleError response.statusCode
--   </pre>
data Response body
Response :: Int -> Array (Text, Text) -> body -> Response body

-- | HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 201, 404)
[statusCode] :: Response body -> Int

-- | Response headers as key-value pairs
[headers] :: Response body -> Array (Text, Text)

-- | Parsed response body
[body] :: Response body -> body
get :: FromJSON response => Request -> Task Error (Response response)
getSecure :: Request -> Task Error (Response Bytes)

-- | Performs a secure GET request, enforcing HTTPS and TLS 1.2+ minimum.
--   Rejects any URL that does not start with '<a>https://'</a> with an
--   <a>InvalidUrl</a> error. The error message sanitizes the URL via
--   <a>sanitizeUrlText</a> to prevent secret leakage. Uses a TLS manager
--   pinned to TLS 1.2+ for defense-in-depth. Use <a>getRaw</a> for trusted
--   localhost callers (e.g., OAuth2 discovery).
secureTlsSupportedVersions :: [Version]
checkContentLengthHeaderLimit :: Maybe Int -> [(CI ByteString, ByteString)] -> Maybe Int

-- | Performs a POST request with JSON body.
post :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a POST request with form-urlencoded body.
--   
--   Used for OAuth2 token endpoints which require
--   <tt>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/oauth/token"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.postForm
--         [ ("grant_type", "authorization_code")
--         , ("code", authCode)
--         , ("redirect_uri", redirectUri)
--         ]
--   </pre>
postForm :: FromJSON response => Request -> Array (Text, Text) -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a POST request with raw body.
--   
--   Used for XML, plain text, or other non-JSON content types.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/orders"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.postRaw "application<i>xml" "<a>order</a><a>id</a>123&lt;</i>id&gt;<a>/order</a>"
--   </pre>
postRaw :: FromJSON response => Request -> Text -> Text -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a PUT request with JSON body.
--   
--   PUT replaces the entire resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/users/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.put { name = "New Name", email = "new@example.com" }
--   </pre>
put :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a PATCH request with JSON body.
--   
--   PATCH partially updates the resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/orders/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.patch { status = "shipped" }
--   </pre>
patch :: (FromJSON response, ToJSON requestBody) => Request -> requestBody -> Task Error (Response response)

-- | Performs a DELETE request.
--   
--   DELETE removes the resource at the target URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.request
--     |&gt; Http.withUrl "<a>https://api.example.com/subscriptions/123"</a>
--     |&gt; Http.delete
--   </pre>
delete :: FromJSON response => Request -> Task Error (Response response)
data Error
Error :: Text -> Error

-- | The URL does not use HTTPS. Use getSecure only with https:// URLs. The
--   URL is sanitized (query params stripped) to prevent secret leakage in
--   logs.
InvalidUrl :: Text -> Error

-- | Response body exceeded the configured size limit. The Int is the limit
--   in bytes that was exceeded.
ResponseTooLarge :: Int -> Error
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Http.Client.Request
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Http.Client.Response body)
instance GHC.Show.Show Http.Client.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Http.Client.Request
instance GHC.Show.Show body => GHC.Show.Show (Http.Client.Response body)

module Http.Client.Internal

-- | Performs a GET request without JSON decoding, returning raw Bytes.
--   Unlike <a>get</a>, this does NOT throw on non-2xx status codes because
--   it uses setRequestIgnoreStatus to disable http-conduit's default
--   status-code exception behavior. Use this when you need to inspect
--   status codes before decoding (e.g., for 401/429 handling).
--   
--   WARNING: Does NOT enforce HTTPS. For external APIs, use
--   <a>getSecure</a> instead. This function exists for trusted internal
--   callers (e.g., localhost OAuth2 discovery).
getRaw :: Request -> Task Error (Response Bytes)

module Http


-- | OAuth2 client operations for Authorization Code flow.
--   
--   This module provides the core functions needed to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Build authorization URLs (redirect user to provider)</li>
--   <li>Exchange authorization codes for tokens</li>
--   <li>Refresh access tokens</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- 1. Generate PKCE verifier and redirect user to authorize
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   -- Store verifier in session, redirect user to authUrl...
--   
--   -- 2. On callback, validate state and exchange code for tokens
--   case OAuth2.validateState expectedState returnedState of
--     Err err -&gt; -- handle CSRF attack
--     Ok () -&gt; do
--       tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkce provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--       -- Store tokens in SecretStore...
--   
--   -- 3. Later, refresh the token
--   newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshToken provider clientId clientSecret tokens.refreshToken
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Security</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Always use PKCE (<a>authorizeUrlWithPkce</a>,
--   <a>exchangeCodeWithPkce</a>) when possible</li>
--   <li>Always validate the <a>State</a> parameter on callback using
--   <tt>validateState</tt> (CSRF protection)</li>
--   <li>Always validate redirect URIs using <tt>validateRedirectUri</tt>
--   before token exchange</li>
--   <li>Store tokens in <a>SecretStore</a>, NOT in event store</li>
--   <li>Never log full authorization URLs (they contain state
--   parameter)</li>
--   <li>Encrypt tokens at rest</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Deployment Requirements</h1>
--   
--   <b>CRITICAL</b>: Disable HTTP proxy environment variables in
--   production:
--   
--   <pre>
--   unset HTTP_PROXY HTTPS_PROXY http_proxy https_proxy
--   </pre>
--   
--   Proxies can bypass SSRF protections by routing validated URLs through
--   attacker-controlled infrastructure. Even with URL validation, a
--   malicious proxy can redirect requests to internal IPs.
--   
--   Alternatively, configure your deployment to block outbound proxy usage
--   at the network level for OAuth2 token endpoints.
module Auth.OAuth2.Client

-- | Validate a provider's endpoints once, returning a
--   <a>ValidatedProvider</a>.
--   
--   Use this at application startup to pre-validate OAuth2 providers. Then
--   use the <a>exchangeCodeValidated</a>,
--   <a>exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated</a>, and <a>refreshTokenValidated</a>
--   functions for high-throughput token operations without repeated
--   endpoint validation.
--   
--   SECURITY: Validates both authorize and token endpoints for:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>HTTPS requirement (tokens contain secrets)</li>
--   <li>No private/loopback IPs (SSRF protection)</li>
--   <li>DNS resolution check (rebinding attack prevention)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- At application startup
--   validatedProvider &lt;- OAuth2.validateProvider ouraProvider
--   
--   -- Later, in request handlers (50k req/s without validation overhead)
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   </pre>
--   
--   NOTE: Provider endpoint validation happens ONCE here, not on every
--   token request. If provider endpoints change (rare), restart the
--   application to re-validate.
validateProvider :: Provider -> Task OAuth2Error ValidatedProvider

-- | Build an OAuth2 authorization URL.
--   
--   The user should be redirected to this URL to begin the OAuth2 flow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   let state = State "random-csrf-token"
--   let scopes = [Scope "read", Scope "write"]
--   let url = OAuth2.authorizeUrl ouraProvider clientId redirectUri scopes state
--   -- Redirect user to url...
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: The <a>State</a> parameter MUST be:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Cryptographically random</li>
--   <li>Stored in session before redirect</li>
--   <li>Validated on callback before calling <a>exchangeCode</a></li>
--   </ul>
authorizeUrl :: Provider -> ClientId -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> State -> Text

-- | Build an OAuth2 authorization URL with PKCE support.
--   
--   Use this instead of <a>authorizeUrl</a> when the provider supports
--   PKCE (recommended for all public clients, optional for confidential
--   clients).
--   
--   <pre>
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   -- Store verifier securely for use in exchangeCodeWithPkce
--   </pre>
authorizeUrlWithPkce :: Provider -> ClientId -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> State -> CodeChallenge -> CodeChallengeMethod -> Text

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens.
--   
--   Call this after the user is redirected back to your callback URL with
--   an authorization code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCode provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   -- Store tokens in SecretStore...
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: Validate the <a>State</a> parameter BEFORE calling this
--   function.
exchangeCode :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens with PKCE verification.
--   
--   Use this when the authorization was initiated with
--   <a>authorizeUrlWithPkce</a>. The code verifier must be the same one
--   used to generate the code challenge.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Retrieve the verifier stored during authorization
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkce provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--   </pre>
exchangeCodeWithPkce :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> CodeVerifier -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens using a pre-validated
--   provider.
--   
--   This is the high-performance variant of <a>exchangeCode</a> - it skips
--   endpoint URL validation since the provider was already validated via
--   <a>validateProvider</a>.
--   
--   Use this for high-throughput scenarios (50k+ req/s) where repeated DNS
--   validation would be a bottleneck.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Provider validated at startup
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: The <a>ValidatedProvider</a> type ensures endpoints were
--   validated.
exchangeCodeValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code with PKCE using a pre-validated
--   provider.
--   
--   High-performance variant of <a>exchangeCodeWithPkce</a> - skips
--   endpoint URL validation since the provider was already validated.
--   
--   <pre>
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--   </pre>
exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> CodeVerifier -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Refresh an access token using a refresh token.
--   
--   Call this when the access token is expired or about to expire.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case tokens.refreshToken of
--     Just rt -&gt; do
--       newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshToken provider clientId clientSecret rt
--       -- Update stored tokens...
--     Nothing -&gt; -- Cannot refresh, need re-authorization
--   </pre>
--   
--   NOTE: Some providers rotate refresh tokens. Always store the new
--   <a>refreshToken</a> from the response, even if it looks the same.
refreshToken :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RefreshToken -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Refresh an access token using a pre-validated provider.
--   
--   High-performance variant of <a>refreshToken</a> - skips endpoint URL
--   validation since the provider was already validated.
--   
--   <pre>
--   newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshTokenValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret refreshToken
--   </pre>
refreshTokenValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RefreshToken -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Generate a cryptographically secure code verifier for PKCE.
--   
--   Returns a 43-character base64url-encoded random string, suitable for
--   the code_verifier parameter in PKCE flows.
--   
--   <pre>
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   </pre>
generateCodeVerifier :: Task error CodeVerifier

-- | Derive a code challenge from a code verifier using S256 method.
--   
--   Computes: base64url(sha256(code_verifier))
--   
--   This is the recommended method per RFC 7636.
deriveCodeChallenge :: CodeVerifier -> CodeChallenge
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Client.TokenErrorResponse
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.OAuth2.Client.TokenResponse
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Client.TokenErrorResponse
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Client.TokenResponse


-- | OAuth2 client support for NeoHaskell.
--   
--   This module provides client-side OAuth2 functionality for connecting
--   to external APIs (Oura, GitHub, Google, etc.) using the Authorization
--   Code flow.
--   
--   <h1>Quick Start</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Auth.OAuth2 (Provider (..), ClientId (..), OAuth2)
--   import Auth.OAuth2 qualified as OAuth2
--   
--   -- Define your provider
--   ouraProvider :: Provider
--   ouraProvider = Provider
--     { name = "oura"
--     , authorizeEndpoint = "<a>https://cloud.ouraring.com/oauth/authorize"</a>
--     , tokenEndpoint = "<a>https://api.ouraring.com/oauth/token"</a>
--     }
--   
--   -- Build authorization URL
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrl ouraProvider clientId redirectUri scopes state
--   
--   -- Exchange code for tokens (after callback)
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCode ouraProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   
--   -- Refresh when needed
--   newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshToken ouraProvider clientId clientSecret refreshToken
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Security Notes</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Store tokens in <a>SecretStore</a>, NOT in the event store</li>
--   <li>Always validate the State parameter on callback using
--   <a>validateState</a> (CSRF protection)</li>
--   <li>Always validate redirect URIs using <a>validateRedirectUri</a>
--   before token exchange</li>
--   <li>Never log or serialize <a>ClientSecret</a> or tokens to
--   events</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Secure Callback Handling</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- In your callback handler:
--   case OAuth2.validateState expectedState returnedState of
--     Err err -&gt; -- Handle CSRF attack / expired state
--     Ok () -&gt; do
--       -- State is valid, proceed with token exchange
--       tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCode provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>See Also</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>SecretStore</a> - Secure token storage</li>
--   <li><tt>Auth</tt> - Server-side JWT validation (for validating tokens
--   FROM providers)</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.OAuth2

-- | OAuth2 provider configuration.
--   
--   Contains the endpoints needed for the Authorization Code flow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   ouraProvider :: Provider
--   ouraProvider = Provider
--     { name = "oura"
--     , authorizeEndpoint = "<a>https://cloud.ouraring.com/oauth/authorize"</a>
--     , tokenEndpoint = "<a>https://api.ouraring.com/oauth/token"</a>
--     }
--   </pre>
data Provider
Provider :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Provider
[name] :: Provider -> Text
[authorizeEndpoint] :: Provider -> Text
[tokenEndpoint] :: Provider -> Text

-- | A provider whose endpoints have been validated.
--   
--   This type proves that both the authorize and token endpoints have
--   passed SSRF validation (HTTPS required, no private IPs, DNS checked).
--   Use <tt>validateProvider</tt> to construct a ValidatedProvider.
--   
--   SECURITY: Once validated at startup, token requests skip redundant
--   validation, improving performance at scale while maintaining security
--   guarantees.
--   
--   Constructor is intentionally not exported - use
--   <tt>validateProvider</tt> in Client.hs.
data ValidatedProvider

-- | Get the underlying Provider from a ValidatedProvider. Use this when
--   you need access to the provider's name or endpoints.
getValidatedProvider :: ValidatedProvider -> Provider

-- | OAuth2 client identifier.
newtype ClientId
ClientId :: Text -> ClientId

-- | OAuth2 client secret.
--   
--   WARNING: Never log or serialize this to events. Show instance is
--   redacted for security. No JSON instances - use explicit conversion if
--   needed. Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkClientSecret</a> and
--   <a>unwrapClientSecret</a>.
data ClientSecret

-- | Create a ClientSecret from raw text. Use with caution - the secret
--   should come from secure configuration.
mkClientSecret :: Text -> ClientSecret

-- | Extract the raw secret text. Use with caution. Only use this when you
--   need to send the secret to the OAuth2 provider.
unwrapClientSecret :: ClientSecret -> Text

-- | OAuth2 redirect URI (callback URL).
--   
--   SECURITY: Must use HTTPS in production (except localhost for
--   development). Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkRedirectUri</a> and
--   <a>unwrapRedirectUri</a>.
data RedirectUri

-- | Create a RedirectUri with HTTPS validation.
--   
--   SECURITY: Redirect URIs MUST use HTTPS to prevent authorization code
--   theft. The only exception is localhost (http:/<i>localhost,
--   http:</i><i>127.0.0.1, http:</i>/[::1]) which is allowed for
--   development/testing.
--   
--   Returns <a>InvalidRedirectUri</a> error if validation fails.
mkRedirectUri :: Text -> Result OAuth2Error RedirectUri

-- | Extract the raw redirect URI text.
unwrapRedirectUri :: RedirectUri -> Text

-- | Authorization code received from the OAuth2 provider.
--   
--   This is exchanged for tokens via <a>exchangeCode</a>. Show instance is
--   redacted for security (codes are secrets). No ToJSON instance -
--   prevent accidental serialization. Constructor is hidden - use
--   <a>mkAuthorizationCode</a> and <a>unwrapAuthorizationCode</a>.
data AuthorizationCode

-- | Create an AuthorizationCode from raw text. Typically from the OAuth2
--   callback query parameter.
mkAuthorizationCode :: Text -> AuthorizationCode

-- | Extract the raw authorization code text. Only use this when exchanging
--   for tokens.
unwrapAuthorizationCode :: AuthorizationCode -> Text

-- | State parameter for CSRF protection.
--   
--   SECURITY: Must be: * Cryptographically random * Stored in session
--   before redirect * Validated on callback before token exchange *
--   Single-use (rejected if reused)
--   
--   Show instance is redacted for security (state tokens are secrets). No
--   ToJSON instance - prevent accidental serialization. Constructor is
--   hidden - use <a>mkState</a> and <a>unwrapState</a>.
data State

-- | Create a State from raw text. The text should be cryptographically
--   random.
mkState :: Text -> State

-- | Extract the raw state text. Only use this for URL building or
--   validation.
unwrapState :: State -> Text

-- | OAuth2 scope(s) being requested.
newtype Scope
Scope :: Text -> Scope

-- | OAuth2 access token.
--   
--   Used in Authorization header: <tt>Authorization: Bearer
--   &lt;token&gt;</tt> Show instance is redacted for security. No JSON
--   instances - use explicit conversion if needed. Constructor is hidden -
--   use <a>mkAccessToken</a> and <a>unwrapAccessToken</a>.
data AccessToken

-- | Create an AccessToken from raw text. Typically from an OAuth2 token
--   response.
mkAccessToken :: Text -> AccessToken

-- | Extract the raw access token text. Only use this when making
--   authenticated API requests.
unwrapAccessToken :: AccessToken -> Text

-- | OAuth2 refresh token.
--   
--   Used to obtain new access tokens without user interaction. Some
--   providers rotate refresh tokens on each use. Show instance is redacted
--   for security. No JSON instances - use explicit conversion if needed.
--   Constructor is hidden - use <a>mkRefreshToken</a> and
--   <a>unwrapRefreshToken</a>.
data RefreshToken

-- | Create a RefreshToken from raw text. Typically from an OAuth2 token
--   response.
mkRefreshToken :: Text -> RefreshToken

-- | Extract the raw refresh token text. Only use this when refreshing
--   tokens with the OAuth2 provider.
unwrapRefreshToken :: RefreshToken -> Text

-- | Complete set of tokens from an OAuth2 token response.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case maybeTokenSet of
--     Just tokens -&gt; do
--       let (AccessToken bearer) = tokens.accessToken
--       Http.request
--         |&gt; Http.addHeader <a>Authorization</a> [fmt|Bearer #{bearer}|]
--         |&gt; Http.get
--     Nothing -&gt; Task.throw "Not connected"
--   </pre>
--   
--   Show instance is redacted (contains secrets). No JSON instances -
--   tokens should be stored via SecretStore, not serialized.
data TokenSet
TokenSet :: AccessToken -> Maybe RefreshToken -> Maybe Int -> TokenSet
[accessToken] :: TokenSet -> AccessToken
[refreshToken] :: TokenSet -> Maybe RefreshToken

-- | Duration in seconds until access token expires (from OAuth2
--   expires_in). This is NOT a unix timestamp - it's a duration from the
--   time of issuance. To get expiry time: currentTime + expiresInSeconds
[expiresInSeconds] :: TokenSet -> Maybe Int

-- | PKCE code verifier (RFC 7636).
--   
--   A cryptographically random string used as the secret in PKCE. Must be
--   43-128 characters from [A-Z], [a-z], [0-9], "-", ".", "_", "~". Show
--   instance is redacted for security. No ToJSON instance - prevent
--   accidental serialization. Constructor is hidden - use
--   <a>mkCodeVerifier</a> and <a>unwrapCodeVerifier</a>.
data CodeVerifier

-- | Create a CodeVerifier from raw text with validation (RFC 7636).
--   
--   Validates: * Length is 43-128 characters * All characters are from the
--   unreserved set: [A-Z], [a-z], [0-9], "-", ".", "_", "~"
--   
--   Returns <a>InvalidPkceVerifier</a> error if validation fails.
mkCodeVerifier :: Text -> Result OAuth2Error CodeVerifier

-- | Create a CodeVerifier without validation (UNSAFE).
--   
--   Only use this when you trust the source (e.g., from
--   <tt>generateCodeVerifier</tt>). For user input, use
--   <a>mkCodeVerifier</a> which validates.
mkCodeVerifierUnsafe :: Text -> CodeVerifier

-- | Extract the raw code verifier text. Only use this when sending to the
--   token endpoint.
unwrapCodeVerifier :: CodeVerifier -> Text

-- | PKCE code challenge (RFC 7636).
--   
--   Derived from CodeVerifier using SHA256 + base64url encoding. Sent to
--   authorization endpoint; verifier is sent to token endpoint.
newtype CodeChallenge
CodeChallenge :: Text -> CodeChallenge

-- | PKCE code challenge method. S256 is recommended; Plain should only be
--   used when SHA256 is unavailable.
data CodeChallengeMethod

-- | SHA256 hash of verifier (recommended)
S256 :: CodeChallengeMethod

-- | Plain text verifier (not recommended)
Plain :: CodeChallengeMethod

-- | Generate a cryptographically secure code verifier for PKCE.
--   
--   Returns a 43-character base64url-encoded random string, suitable for
--   the code_verifier parameter in PKCE flows.
--   
--   <pre>
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   </pre>
generateCodeVerifier :: Task error CodeVerifier

-- | Derive a code challenge from a code verifier using S256 method.
--   
--   Computes: base64url(sha256(code_verifier))
--   
--   This is the recommended method per RFC 7636.
deriveCodeChallenge :: CodeVerifier -> CodeChallenge

-- | Build an OAuth2 authorization URL with PKCE support.
--   
--   Use this instead of <a>authorizeUrl</a> when the provider supports
--   PKCE (recommended for all public clients, optional for confidential
--   clients).
--   
--   <pre>
--   verifier &lt;- OAuth2.generateCodeVerifier
--   let challenge = OAuth2.deriveCodeChallenge verifier
--   let authUrl = OAuth2.authorizeUrlWithPkce provider clientId redirectUri scopes state challenge S256
--   -- Store verifier securely for use in exchangeCodeWithPkce
--   </pre>
authorizeUrlWithPkce :: Provider -> ClientId -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> State -> CodeChallenge -> CodeChallengeMethod -> Text

-- | Errors that can occur during OAuth2 operations.
data OAuth2Error

-- | Token request failed (network or HTTP error)
TokenRequestFailed :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Invalid grant (code expired, already used, etc.)
InvalidGrant :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Invalid client credentials
InvalidClient :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Requested scope was denied
ScopeDenied :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Token response was malformed
MalformedResponse :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Network error during token exchange
NetworkError :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Endpoint URL failed security validation (HTTPS/SSRF)
EndpointValidationFailed :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | State parameter validation failed (CSRF protection)
InvalidState :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | PKCE verifier validation failed
InvalidPkceVerifier :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Redirect URI validation failed
InvalidRedirectUri :: Text -> OAuth2Error

-- | Validate that the returned state matches the expected state. SECURITY:
--   This is critical for CSRF protection. Always validate state before
--   exchanging the authorization code for tokens.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case validateState expectedState returnedState of
--     Ok () -&gt; OAuth2.exchangeCode provider clientId secret redirectUri code
--     Err err -&gt; Task.throw err
--   </pre>
validateState :: State -> State -> Result OAuth2Error ()

-- | Validate that a redirect URI matches the expected registered URI.
--   SECURITY: Redirect URI validation is critical to prevent authorization
--   code theft.
--   
--   Validates: 1. Exact string match (no normalization that could allow
--   bypasses) 2. No fragment component (fragments are forbidden in
--   redirect URIs per RFC 6749) 3. Uses HTTPS (except localhost for
--   development)
--   
--   <pre>
--   case validateRedirectUri registeredUri receivedUri of
--     Ok () -&gt; -- proceed with token exchange
--     Err err -&gt; -- reject the request
--   </pre>
validateRedirectUri :: RedirectUri -> RedirectUri -> Result OAuth2Error ()

-- | Validate a provider's endpoints once, returning a
--   <a>ValidatedProvider</a>.
--   
--   Use this at application startup to pre-validate OAuth2 providers. Then
--   use the <a>exchangeCodeValidated</a>,
--   <a>exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated</a>, and <a>refreshTokenValidated</a>
--   functions for high-throughput token operations without repeated
--   endpoint validation.
--   
--   SECURITY: Validates both authorize and token endpoints for:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>HTTPS requirement (tokens contain secrets)</li>
--   <li>No private/loopback IPs (SSRF protection)</li>
--   <li>DNS resolution check (rebinding attack prevention)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- At application startup
--   validatedProvider &lt;- OAuth2.validateProvider ouraProvider
--   
--   -- Later, in request handlers (50k req/s without validation overhead)
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   </pre>
--   
--   NOTE: Provider endpoint validation happens ONCE here, not on every
--   token request. If provider endpoints change (rare), restart the
--   application to re-validate.
validateProvider :: Provider -> Task OAuth2Error ValidatedProvider

-- | Build an OAuth2 authorization URL.
--   
--   The user should be redirected to this URL to begin the OAuth2 flow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   let state = State "random-csrf-token"
--   let scopes = [Scope "read", Scope "write"]
--   let url = OAuth2.authorizeUrl ouraProvider clientId redirectUri scopes state
--   -- Redirect user to url...
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: The <a>State</a> parameter MUST be:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Cryptographically random</li>
--   <li>Stored in session before redirect</li>
--   <li>Validated on callback before calling <a>exchangeCode</a></li>
--   </ul>
authorizeUrl :: Provider -> ClientId -> RedirectUri -> Array Scope -> State -> Text

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens.
--   
--   Call this after the user is redirected back to your callback URL with
--   an authorization code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCode provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   -- Store tokens in SecretStore...
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: Validate the <a>State</a> parameter BEFORE calling this
--   function.
exchangeCode :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens with PKCE verification.
--   
--   Use this when the authorization was initiated with
--   <a>authorizeUrlWithPkce</a>. The code verifier must be the same one
--   used to generate the code challenge.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Retrieve the verifier stored during authorization
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkce provider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--   </pre>
exchangeCodeWithPkce :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> CodeVerifier -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Refresh an access token using a refresh token.
--   
--   Call this when the access token is expired or about to expire.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case tokens.refreshToken of
--     Just rt -&gt; do
--       newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshToken provider clientId clientSecret rt
--       -- Update stored tokens...
--     Nothing -&gt; -- Cannot refresh, need re-authorization
--   </pre>
--   
--   NOTE: Some providers rotate refresh tokens. Always store the new
--   <a>refreshToken</a> from the response, even if it looks the same.
refreshToken :: Provider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RefreshToken -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code for tokens using a pre-validated
--   provider.
--   
--   This is the high-performance variant of <a>exchangeCode</a> - it skips
--   endpoint URL validation since the provider was already validated via
--   <a>validateProvider</a>.
--   
--   Use this for high-throughput scenarios (50k+ req/s) where repeated DNS
--   validation would be a bottleneck.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Provider validated at startup
