--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code
--   </pre>
--   
--   SECURITY: The <a>ValidatedProvider</a> type ensures endpoints were
--   validated.
exchangeCodeValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Exchange an authorization code with PKCE using a pre-validated
--   provider.
--   
--   High-performance variant of <a>exchangeCodeWithPkce</a> - skips
--   endpoint URL validation since the provider was already validated.
--   
--   <pre>
--   tokens &lt;- OAuth2.exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret redirectUri code verifier
--   </pre>
exchangeCodeWithPkceValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> AuthorizationCode -> CodeVerifier -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet

-- | Refresh an access token using a pre-validated provider.
--   
--   High-performance variant of <a>refreshToken</a> - skips endpoint URL
--   validation since the provider was already validated.
--   
--   <pre>
--   newTokens &lt;- OAuth2.refreshTokenValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret refreshToken
--   </pre>
refreshTokenValidated :: ValidatedProvider -> ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RefreshToken -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet


-- | OpenID Connect Discovery and JWKS fetching. This module handles
--   fetching configuration from OAuth providers.
--   
--   SECURITY: All URLs are validated to require HTTPS and block SSRF
--   vectors. Optional domain allowlist restricts IdPs to specific trusted
--   domains.
module Auth.Discovery

-- | Discover auth configuration from an OAuth provider's base URL. Fetches
--   the OpenID Connect discovery document and JWKS keys.
--   
--   SECURITY: Validates that authServerUrl uses HTTPS and is not a private
--   IP.
--   
--   Example: <tt> config &lt;- discoverConfig
--   "<a>https://auth.example.com"</a> defaultOverrides </tt>
discoverConfig :: Text -> AuthOverrides -> Task DiscoveryError AuthConfig

-- | Fetch JWKS from the given URI and extract keys. Returns an array of
--   JWKs that can be used for token validation.
--   
--   SECURITY: Validates that jwksUri uses HTTPS and is not a private IP.
--   Uses DNS resolution to prevent SSRF via DNS rebinding attacks.
--   SECURITY: 30 second timeout to prevent indefinite hangs
fetchJwks :: Text -> Task err (Result DiscoveryError (Array JWK))

-- | OpenID Connect Discovery document structure. We only parse the fields
--   we need.
data DiscoveryDocument
DiscoveryDocument :: Text -> Text -> DiscoveryDocument
[issuer] :: DiscoveryDocument -> Text
[jwks_uri] :: DiscoveryDocument -> Text
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.Discovery.DiscoveryDocument
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.Discovery.DiscoveryDocument
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Discovery.DiscoveryDocument
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.Discovery.DiscoveryDocument


-- | In-memory TransactionStore implementation.
--   
--   WARNING: This implementation is for DEVELOPMENT ONLY.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Data is lost on process restart</li>
--   <li>NOT suitable for multi-instance deployments (breaks one-time use
--   guarantee)</li>
--   <li>Not encrypted at rest</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   For production with multiple instances, use Redis (GETDEL for atomic
--   consume).
--   
--   <h1>TTL Cleanup</h1>
--   
--   This store includes a background reaper that runs every 60 seconds to
--   clean up expired transactions. This prevents unbounded memory growth.
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   store &lt;- TransactionStore.InMemory.new
--   
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   let tx = Transaction { verifier = verifier, userId = userId, expiresAt = now + 300 }
--   store.put key tx
--   
--   -- Later, in callback:
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   maybeTx &lt;- store.consume key  -- Atomically get + delete
--   </pre>
module Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.InMemory

-- | Create a new in-memory TransactionStore.
--   
--   WARNING: Development only. See module documentation.
--   
--   The returned store guarantees atomic <a>consume</a> semantics within a
--   single process. Multiple instances will break the one-time use
--   guarantee - use Redis for production multi-instance deployments.
--   
--   Includes a background reaper that cleans up expired transactions every
--   60 seconds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   store &lt;- TransactionStore.InMemory.new
--   </pre>
new :: Task Text TransactionStore


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter
--   
--   Simple in-memory rate limiter for OAuth2 endpoints.
--   
--   <h1>Security Purpose</h1>
--   
--   Rate limiting prevents abuse of OAuth2 endpoints:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>/connect</tt> - Prevents attackers from generating excessive
--   state tokens</li>
--   <li><tt>/callback</tt> - Prevents brute-force attacks on state
--   validation</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Algorithm</h1>
--   
--   Uses a sliding window counter per key (IP address, userId, or
--   provider). Requests are counted within a configurable time window.
--   
--   <h1>Limitations</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>In-memory only - does not work across multiple instances</li>
--   <li>No persistence - limits reset on restart</li>
--   <li>For production with multiple instances, use Redis</li>
--   </ul>
module Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter

-- | Rate limiter interface.
data RateLimiter
RateLimiter :: (Text -> Task Text RateLimitResult) -> RateLimiter

-- | Check if a request should be rate limited. Takes a key (e.g., IP
--   address, userId) and returns the result.
[checkLimit] :: RateLimiter -> Text -> Task Text RateLimitResult

-- | Rate limit configuration.
data RateLimitConfig
RateLimitConfig :: Int -> Int -> RateLimitConfig

-- | Maximum requests allowed in the window
[maxRequests] :: RateLimitConfig -> Int

-- | Time window in seconds
[windowSeconds] :: RateLimitConfig -> Int

-- | Result of a rate limit check.
data RateLimitResult

-- | Request is allowed
Allowed :: RateLimitResult

-- | Request is rate limited (retry after N seconds)
Limited :: Int -> RateLimitResult

-- | Create a new rate limiter with the given configuration.
--   
--   Includes a background reaper that cleans up old buckets every 5
--   minutes.
new :: RateLimitConfig -> Task Text RateLimiter

-- | Default rate limit for /connect endpoint.
--   
--   10 requests per minute per key (userId). This is generous for normal
--   use but limits abuse.
defaultConnectConfig :: RateLimitConfig

-- | Default rate limit for /callback endpoint.
--   
--   20 requests per minute per key (IP address). Higher limit because
--   legitimate callbacks may retry.
defaultCallbackConfig :: RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.Routes
--   
--   HTTP route handlers for OAuth2 provider integration.
--   
--   <h1>Routes</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>GET <i>connect</i>{provider}</tt> - Initiate OAuth2 flow
--   (requires JWT)</li>
--   <li><tt>GET <i>callback</i>{provider}</tt> - OAuth2 callback (no JWT,
--   validates state)</li>
--   <li><tt>POST <i>disconnect</i>{provider}</tt> - Disconnect provider
--   (requires JWT)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Security</h1>
--   
--   The connect and disconnect routes require JWT authentication because
--   they need the userId to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Store userId server-side in TransactionStore (connect) - GDPR
--   compliant</li>
--   <li>Delete tokens for the correct user (disconnect)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   The callback route does NOT require JWT because:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>User is redirected back from external OAuth2 provider</li>
--   <li>The state token (HMAC-signed) identifies the transaction</li>
--   <li>userId is retrieved from server-side TransactionStore (NOT from
--   token)</li>
--   <li>State is validated and consumed atomically (one-time use)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>GDPR Compliance</h1>
--   
--   NO PII (userId, email, etc.) is stored in the state token. The state
--   token only contains: provider name, cryptographic nonce, and
--   timestamps. userId is stored server-side in TransactionStore and
--   retrieved at callback.
--   
--   <h1>Flow</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   1. User clicks "Connect Oura" button
--   2. Frontend calls GET <i>connect</i>oura (with JWT)
--   3. Server generates state token (provider + nonce), stores verifier + userId
--   4. Server redirects to Oura authorization URL
--   5. User authorizes on Oura
--   6. Oura redirects to GET <i>callback</i>oura?code=...&amp;state=...
--   7. Server validates state, retrieves userId from TransactionStore
--   8. Server exchanges code for tokens, calls onSuccess callback
--   9. Server redirects user to successRedirectUrl
--   </pre>
module Auth.OAuth2.Routes

-- | OAuth2 route handlers.
--   
--   These are WAI-independent handlers that can be integrated into
--   WebTransport.
data OAuth2Routes
OAuth2Routes :: (Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError Text) -> (Text -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError (Text, Maybe OAuth2Action)) -> (Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError OAuth2Action) -> OAuth2Routes

-- | Handle GET <i>connect</i>{provider} Returns: Redirect URL to OAuth2
--   provider
[handleConnect] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError Text

-- | Handle GET <i>callback</i>{provider}?code=...&amp;state=... Returns:
--   (redirect URL, maybe action to dispatch)
[handleCallback] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError (Text, Maybe OAuth2Action)

-- | Handle POST <i>disconnect</i>{provider} Returns: Action to dispatch
[handleDisconnect] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError OAuth2Action

-- | Create OAuth2 route handlers with the given dependencies.
createRoutes :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> OAuth2Routes

-- | Dependencies required by OAuth2 routes.
--   
--   These are injected at route creation time.
data OAuth2RouteDeps
OAuth2RouteDeps :: HmacKey -> TransactionStore -> SecretStore -> Map Text ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> RateLimiter -> RateLimiter -> OAuth2RouteDeps

-- | HMAC key for signing/verifying state tokens
[hmacKey] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> HmacKey

-- | Store for PKCE verifiers (one-time use)
[transactionStore] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> TransactionStore

-- | Secure storage for OAuth2 tokens
[secretStore] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> SecretStore

-- | Configured OAuth2 providers by name (pre-validated at startup)
[providers] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> Map Text ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig

-- | Rate limiter for /connect endpoint (per userId)
[connectRateLimiter] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> RateLimiter

-- | Rate limiter for /callback endpoint (per IP, but we use state token as
--   proxy)
[callbackRateLimiter] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> RateLimiter

-- | Errors that can occur in OAuth2 routes.
data OAuth2RouteError

-- | Provider not configured
ProviderNotFound :: Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | State token validation failed
StateValidationFailed :: StateTokenError -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | State not found in transaction store (expired or already used)
StateNotFound :: OAuth2RouteError

-- | OAuth2 token exchange failed
TokenExchangeFailed :: OAuth2Error -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Provider name in state doesn't match route
ProviderMismatch :: Text -> Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Missing required query parameter
MissingParameter :: Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Rate limit exceeded (retry after N seconds)
RateLimited :: Int -> OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError


-- | High-performance JWKS manager for JWT authentication. Provides
--   lock-free key lookups with background refresh.
--   
--   Architecture: - Hot path (per request): AtomicVar.peek for lock-free
--   reads - Control plane (background): Periodic refresh, missing-key
--   handling
--   
--   Performance target: 50k req/s with lock-free key lookups.
module Auth.Jwks

-- | JWKS Manager with background refresh.
data JwksManager
JwksManager :: AuthConfig -> AtomicVar KeySnapshot -> AtomicVar RefreshState -> AtomicVar (Maybe (AsyncTask Text Unit)) -> AtomicVar Bool -> JwksManager

-- | Auth configuration (issuer, jwksUri, etc.)
[config] :: JwksManager -> AuthConfig

-- | Hot path: lock-free reads
[keySnapshot] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar KeySnapshot

-- | Atomic refresh state (cooldown + mutual exclusion)
[refreshState] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar RefreshState

-- | Background refresh task (if running)
[refreshTask] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar (Maybe (AsyncTask Text Unit))

-- | Whether the manager is running
[isRunning] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar Bool

-- | Start a new JWKS manager with background refresh. Performs initial key
--   fetch before returning.
startManager :: AuthConfig -> Task Text JwksManager

-- | Stop the JWKS manager and cancel background refresh.
stopManager :: JwksManager -> Task Text Unit

-- | Look up a key by kid (key ID). This is the hot path - uses lock-free
--   reads.
lookupKey :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err (Maybe JWK)

-- | Get all cached keys. Used when no kid is specified in the token.
getAllKeys :: JwksManager -> Task err (Array JWK)

-- | Get the cached JWKSet for signature verification. This is the
--   preferred hot path - avoids per-request JWKSet construction.
getJwkSet :: JwksManager -> Task err JWKSet

-- | Get a JWKSet containing only the key with the given kid. This is the
--   optimal path when the token contains a kid claim. Uses pre-cached
--   singleton JWKSet to avoid per-request allocation. Returns the full
--   JWKSet if kid is not found (fallback to try all keys).
getJwkSetForKid :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err JWKSet

-- | Get JWKSet for kid, triggering refresh if kid not found. Returns
--   (JWKSet, kidWasFound). If kid not found: 1. Triggers background
--   refresh (rate-limited) 2. Re-reads snapshot to pick up newly rotated
--   keys 3. Returns full JWKSet as fallback if still not found Uses
--   pre-cached singleton JWKSet to avoid per-request allocation.
getJwkSetForKidWithRefresh :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err (JWKSet, Bool)

-- | Check if keys are stale beyond acceptable threshold. Returns True if
--   keys should not be used (triggers 503 response). OPTIMIZED: No
--   per-request clock IO - just reads the precomputed isStale flag. The
--   isStale flag is set by the refresh loop when refresh fails.
checkStaleness :: JwksManager -> Task err Bool

-- | Request an immediate refresh (e.g., when a kid is not found). This is
--   rate-limited and uses atomic state to prevent thundering herd. Only
--   one refresh can run at a time across all callers (including background
--   refresh). Uses Task.finally to ensure lock is released even if refresh
--   throws an exception.
requestRefresh :: JwksManager -> Task err Unit

-- | Snapshot of cached keys for lock-free reads.
data KeySnapshot
KeySnapshot :: Map Text JWK -> Map Text JWKSet -> JWKSet -> Array JWK -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Bool -> KeySnapshot

-- | Current keys indexed by kid
[keysByKid] :: KeySnapshot -> Map Text JWK

-- | Pre-built singleton JWKSets per kid (avoid per-request allocation)
[jwkSetsByKid] :: KeySnapshot -> Map Text JWKSet

-- | Pre-built JWKSet with all keys (for tokens without kid)
[cachedJwkSet] :: KeySnapshot -> JWKSet

-- | Pre-built array of all keys (for tokens without kid)
[allKeysArray] :: KeySnapshot -> Array JWK

-- | Unix timestamp when keys were fetched
[fetchedAt] :: KeySnapshot -> Int64

-- | Monotonic version for stale-race prevention
[snapshotVersion] :: KeySnapshot -> Int64

-- | True if refresh failed and keys are beyond max stale time
[isStale] :: KeySnapshot -> Bool

-- | Refresh state for atomic cooldown and mutual exclusion.
data RefreshState
RefreshState :: Int64 -> Bool -> RefreshState

-- | Unix timestamp of last refresh attempt
[lastAttemptAt] :: RefreshState -> Int64

-- | True if a refresh is currently running
[refreshInProgress] :: RefreshState -> Bool
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwks.KeySnapshot
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwks.RefreshState


-- | User claims extracted from a validated JWT. This is the primary type
--   Jess interacts with - everything else is internal.
module Auth.Claims

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.Claims.UserClaims


-- | Query authorization types and helper combinators.
--   
--   This module provides the building blocks for declarative query
--   authorization. Queries define two functions:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>canAccess</tt>: Checked before fetching data. "Can this user
--   access this query type at all?"</li>
--   <li><tt>canView</tt>: Checked after fetching data. "Can this user view
--   this specific instance?"</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Secure by Default (Whitelist Approach)</h2>
--   
--   NeoHaskell uses a whitelist approach to authorization:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Default to authenticated**: Use <a>authenticatedAccess</a>
--   unless you have a specific reason not to</li>
--   <li>**Public access is opt-in**: Only use <a>publicAccess</a> for
--   truly public data (product catalogs, etc.)</li>
--   <li>**Explicit is better**: Every query must define its auth policy -
--   there are no implicit defaults</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- User's private data (RECOMMENDED PATTERN)
--   canAccess = authenticatedAccess
--   canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId)
--   
--   -- Admin-only query
--   canAccess = requirePermission "admin:read"
--   canView = publicView  -- OK: canAccess already restricts to admins
--   
--   -- Truly public data (use sparingly)
--   canAccess = publicAccess  -- EXPLICIT opt-in to public access
--   canView = publicView
--   </pre>
module Service.AccessControl

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value

-- | Errors that can occur during query authorization.
data AccessError

-- | No user identity provided (not authenticated)
Unauthenticated :: AccessError

-- | User lacks permission to view this specific instance
Forbidden :: AccessError

-- | User lacks required permission/scope
InsufficientPermissions :: Array Text -> AccessError

-- | Errors that can occur during query endpoint execution. This typed
--   error enables proper HTTP status mapping without string matching.
data QueryEndpointError

-- | Authorization failed (maps to 401 or 403)
AuthorizationError :: AccessError -> QueryEndpointError

-- | Storage/serialization failed (maps to 500)
StorageError :: Text -> QueryEndpointError

-- | Require authentication to access this query type.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>*This is the recommended default.** Use this unless you have a
--   specific reason to allow unauthenticated access.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   canAccess = authenticatedAccess  -- RECOMMENDED
--   </pre>
authenticatedAccess :: Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Require a specific permission to access this query type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canAccess = requirePermission "inventory:read"
--   </pre>
requirePermission :: Text -> Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Require ANY of the specified permissions to access this query type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canAccess = requireAnyPermission ["admin:read", "manager:read"]
--   </pre>
requireAnyPermission :: Array Text -> Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Require ALL of the specified permissions to access this query type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canAccess = requireAllPermissions ["inventory:read", "warehouse:access"]
--   </pre>
requireAllPermissions :: Array Text -> Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Allow any user (authenticated or not) to access this query type.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>*Use sparingly.** This explicitly opts into public access. Only
--   appropriate for truly public data like product catalogs.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Only use for genuinely public data
--   canAccess = publicAccess
--   </pre>
publicAccess :: Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Restrict viewing to the owner of the resource.
--   
--   Takes a function that extracts the owner ID from the query instance
--   and compares it to the authenticated user's subject (sub) claim.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId)
--   </pre>
ownerOnly :: (query -> Text) -> Maybe UserClaims -> query -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Restrict viewing to instances belonging to the requesting user's
--   tenant.
--   
--   Takes a function that extracts the tenant ID from the query instance
--   and compares it to the authenticated user's tenantId claim. Comparison
--   is case-insensitive to handle OAuth providers that may return UUIDs in
--   different casing.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canView = tenantOnly (query -&gt; query.tenantId)
--   </pre>
tenantOnly :: (query -> Text) -> Maybe UserClaims -> query -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Allow any user to view any instance of this query.
--   
--   Use for public data where instance-level access control is not needed.
--   
--   <pre>
--   canView = publicView
--   </pre>
publicView :: Maybe UserClaims -> query -> Maybe AccessError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.AccessControl.AccessError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.AccessControl.QueryEndpointError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.AccessControl.AccessError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.AccessControl.AccessError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.AccessControl.QueryEndpointError
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.AccessControl.AccessError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.AccessControl.AccessError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.AccessControl.QueryEndpointError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.AccessControl.AccessError


-- | JWT token validation. This module handles parsing and validating JWT
--   tokens against the auth configuration. It implements RFC 8725 JOSE
--   hardening.
module Auth.Jwt

-- | Validate a JWT token against the auth configuration. Returns
--   UserClaims on success, AuthError on failure.
--   
--   Validation order (per RFC 8725 and performance optimization): 1. Parse
--   JWT header ONCE (optimized - single parse for alg<i>kid</i>typ/crit)
--   2. Reject alg not in allowlist (fail fast, no crypto) 3. Reject
--   unknown crit headers 4. Validate typ if present (RFC 8725) 5. Decode
--   and verify with jose library (CPU-heavy) 6. Issuer validation (exact
--   match) 7. Audience validation 8. Time claims (exp/nbf) with clock skew
--   9. Extract UserClaims
validateToken :: AuthConfig -> Array JWK -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token using a pre-built JWKSet. This is the optimized
--   hot path - avoids JWKSet construction per request.
validateTokenWithJwkSet :: AuthConfig -> JWKSet -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token with a pre-parsed header. This is the most
--   optimized hot path for the middleware: 1. Parse header once in
--   middleware (for kid extraction) 2. Pass ParsedHeader here to avoid
--   re-parsing
validateTokenWithParsedHeader :: AuthConfig -> JWKSet -> ParsedHeader -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Extract kid (key ID) from JWT token header. Returns Nothing if kid is
--   not present in the header. Note: For hot path, prefer parseHeader once
--   and access .kid
extractKidFromToken :: Text -> Maybe Text

-- | Parsed JWT header for hot-path optimization. Parse once, use for all
--   validation checks.
data ParsedHeader
ParsedHeader :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> ParsedHeader

-- | Algorithm (required)
[alg] :: ParsedHeader -> Text

-- | Key ID (optional)
[kid] :: ParsedHeader -> Maybe Text

-- | Type header (optional, RFC 8725 recommends checking)
[typ] :: ParsedHeader -> Maybe Text

-- | Critical headers (empty if not present)
[crit] :: ParsedHeader -> Array Text

-- | Parse JWT header once for all validation checks. This is the optimized
--   hot path - avoids re-parsing header 3-4 times. Uses strict base64url
--   decoding per RFC 8725 security recommendations.
parseHeader :: Text -> Result AuthError ParsedHeader

-- | Parse just the token header (for testing/debugging)
parseTokenHeader :: Text -> Result AuthError Text

-- | Extract algorithm from JWT token without full parsing. Note: For hot
--   path, prefer parseHeader once and access .alg
extractAlgorithmFromToken :: Text -> Result AuthError Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Jwt.ParsedHeader
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwt.ParsedHeader


-- | Test utilities for JWT validation. Provides functions to generate test
--   tokens for various scenarios.
module Test.Auth.Jwt.Core

-- | Default test configuration
testConfig :: AuthConfig

-- | Test configuration with audience validation
testConfigWithAudience :: Text -> AuthConfig

-- | Pre-computed test keys for signing tokens. Keys are parsed from JSON
--   rather than generated at runtime, eliminating expensive RSA key
--   generation (~300ms per call). These are TEST-ONLY keys (1024-bit RSA)
--   — NOT suitable for production.
testKeys :: Task Text TestKeys

-- | Test keys for signing tokens
data TestKeys
TestKeys :: JWK -> JWK -> JWK -> TestKeys
[es256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK
[rs256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK
[hs256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK

-- | Sign a valid token with ES256
signValidToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a valid token with RS256
signValidTokenRS256 :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign an expired token
signExpiredToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token not yet valid (nbf in future)
signNotYetValidToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token that expired recently (within clock skew tolerance)
signRecentlyExpiredToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific issuer
signTokenWithIssuer :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific audience
signTokenWithAudience :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific sub claim
signTokenWithSub :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with email claim
signTokenWithEmail :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with permissions claim
signTokenWithPermissions :: TestKeys -> Array Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with tenant ID
signTokenWithTenantId :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with arbitrary custom claims
signTokenWithClaims :: TestKeys -> Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Create a token with alg=none (MUST be rejected) Constructed
--   programmatically at runtime to avoid triggering secret scanners.
--   Header: {"alg":"none","typ":<a>JWT</a>} Payload:
--   {"sub":"user","iss":"<a>https://auth.example.com"}</a>
makeAlgNoneToken :: Text

-- | Create a token signed with HS256 (symmetric, should be rejected for
--   public key validation) Constructed programmatically at runtime to
--   avoid triggering secret scanners. Header:
--   {"alg":<a>HS256</a>,"typ":<a>JWT</a>} Payload:
--   {"sub":"user","iss":"<a>https://auth.example.com"}</a> Note: Signature
--   is a placeholder since we only need to test algorithm rejection.
makeHS256Token :: Text

-- | Create a malformed token for testing format validation
makeMalformedToken :: Text

-- | Validate a JWT token format against the auth configuration. Wrapper
--   that uses empty key set - only validates token format, NOT signatures.
--   For signature validation, use validateTokenWithKeys instead.
validateTokenFormat :: AuthConfig -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token with a specific key set
validateTokenWithKeys :: AuthConfig -> Array JWK -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Create a test JwksManager with pre-populated keys. This manager has no
--   background refresh - it's purely for testing. Keys are extracted from
--   TestKeys and stored directly in the snapshot.
createTestManager :: TestKeys -> Task Text JwksManager


-- | HTTP middleware for JWT authentication. Integrates with WebTransport
--   to authenticate requests.
module Auth.Middleware

-- | Extract Bearer token from Authorization header. Returns Nothing if no
--   Authorization header or not Bearer scheme. Handles whitespace
--   variations: " Bearer token " is accepted.
extractToken :: Request -> Maybe Text

-- | Extract token from ?token= query parameter. SECURITY: Only use for
--   endpoints where Authorization header is infeasible (e.g., browser
--   redirect flows like <i>connect</i>{provider}). Prefer
--   <a>extractToken</a> (header-based) for all other endpoints. See
--   ADR-0031.
extractTokenFromQuery :: Request -> Maybe Text

-- | Check authentication and authorization for a request. Returns Ok
--   AuthContext on success, Err AuthError on failure.
checkAuth :: Maybe JwksManager -> AuthConfig -> AuthOptions -> Request -> Task err (Result AuthError AuthContext)

-- | Validate a pre-extracted token and build auth context. SECURITY: Use
--   when the token is obtained from a non-standard source (e.g., query
--   parameter for browser redirect flows). The token receives the same
--   validation as header-extracted tokens. See ADR-0031.
checkAuthWithToken :: Maybe JwksManager -> AuthConfig -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError AuthContext)

-- | Convert AuthError to appropriate HTTP response. SECURITY: Uses generic
--   messages to prevent information leakage.
respondWithAuthError :: AuthError -> (Response -> Task Text ResponseReceived) -> Task Text ResponseReceived

-- | Authentication context passed to handlers.
data AuthContext
AuthContext :: Maybe UserClaims -> Bool -> AuthContext

-- | User claims (Nothing if endpoint is public)
[claims] :: AuthContext -> Maybe UserClaims

-- | Whether the request was authenticated
[isAuthenticated] :: AuthContext -> Bool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Middleware.AuthContext
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Middleware.AuthContext


-- | JWT Authentication for NeoHaskell.
--   
--   This module provides JWT token validation for event-sourced
--   applications. It's designed to work with external OAuth providers
--   (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, etc.) using OpenID Connect Discovery.
--   
--   <h1>Quick Start</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   app :: Application
--   app =
--     Application.new
--       |&gt; Application.withAuth "<a>https://auth.example.com"</a>
--       |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>What Jess Sees</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>UserClaims</a> - The authenticated user's identity</li>
--   <li><a>AuthOptions</a> - Declarative auth requirements (Everyone,
--   Authenticated, etc.)</li>
--   <li><a>AuthOverrides</a> - Optional advanced configuration</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Everything else (JWKS caching, token validation, etc.) is internal.
module Auth

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value

-- | Authentication requirements for an endpoint. Note: Permission checks
--   belong in the command's decide method.
data AuthOptions

-- | No authentication required (public endpoint)
Everyone :: AuthOptions

-- | Valid JWT required
Authenticated :: AuthOptions

-- | Optional overrides for advanced users. Most fields use sensible
--   defaults; only override if you have special requirements.
data AuthOverrides
AuthOverrides :: Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int64 -> Maybe (Array Text) -> Maybe (Array Text) -> AuthOverrides

-- | Override aud claim validation (default: None)
[audience] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Override permissions claim name (default: "permissions")
[permissionsClaim] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Claim name for tenant ID (default: None)
[tenantIdClaim] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Override clock skew tolerance (default: 60)
[clockSkewSeconds] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Int64

-- | Override allowed algorithms
[allowedAlgorithms] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe (Array Text)

-- | Restrict IdP to specific domains (e.g., ["auth.example.com",
--   "login.company.eu"]) When set, discovery will reject URLs from
--   non-allowlisted domains. Default: None (allow any HTTPS URL that
--   passes SSRF checks)
[allowedIdpDomains] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe (Array Text)

-- | Default overrides (all Nothing - use discovered/default values).
defaultOverrides :: AuthOverrides

-- | Errors that can occur during authentication. These are mapped to HTTP
--   responses by the middleware. Note: Error responses use generic
--   messages to prevent information leakage.
data AuthError

-- | No Authorization header
TokenMissing :: AuthError

-- | Not a valid JWT format
TokenMalformed :: Text -> AuthError

-- | exp claim in the past
TokenExpired :: AuthError

-- | nbf claim in the future
TokenNotYetValid :: AuthError

-- | Signature doesn't verify
SignatureInvalid :: AuthError

-- | alg not in allowlist (RFC 8725)
AlgorithmNotAllowed :: Text -> AuthError

-- | Unknown crit header (RFC 8725)
UnsupportedCritHeader :: Array Text -> AuthError

-- | Key type doesn't match alg (RFC 8725)
KeyAlgorithmMismatch :: AuthError

-- | Expected vs actual issuer
IssuerMismatch :: Text -> Text -> AuthError

-- | Expected vs actual audience
AudienceMismatch :: Text -> Array Text -> AuthError

-- | JWKS/keys unavailable (503, not 401)
AuthInfraUnavailable :: Text -> AuthError

-- | Key ID not found in JWKS
KeyNotFound :: Text -> AuthError

module Unknown

-- | The <a>Unknown</a> type represents a dynamically typed value.
data Unknown

-- | Convert a value of any type to <a>Unknown</a>.
fromValue :: Typeable value => value -> Unknown

-- | Convert an <a>Unknown</a> value back to its original type, if
--   possible.
toValue :: Typeable value => Unknown -> Maybe value

-- | The <a>Convertible</a> type class represents types that can be
--   converted to <a>Unknown</a>.
type Convertible (value :: k) = Typeable value

-- | Applies a function that is inside the <a>Unknown</a> value.
apply :: Unknown -> Unknown -> Maybe Unknown

-- | Returns the name of the type
getTypeName :: forall {k} (a :: k). Typeable a => Text

-- | Gets the name of the type of an unknown value
getUnknownTypeName :: Unknown -> Text
instance (Unknown.Convertible a, Unknown.Convertible b) => GHC.Show.Show (a -> b)
instance GHC.Show.Show Unknown.Unknown


-- | Type-erased registry of pre-bound dispatcher closures, keyed by
--   <a>TypeRep</a>.
--   
--   Populated once at startup by <a>run</a>, read once per <tt>emit</tt>
--   at request time.
module Service.Integration.DispatchRegistry

-- | Map of <a>TypeRep</a> to a type-erased dispatcher closure.
data DispatchRegistry

-- | Empty registry.
empty :: DispatchRegistry

-- | Register a dispatcher closure for a request type.
--   
--   Re-registering the same type overwrites the previous closure.
register :: (Integration request, Typeable request, Typeable (Response request)) => (request -> Task IntegrationError (Response request)) -> DispatchRegistry -> DispatchRegistry

-- | Look up a dispatcher closure for a request type.
--   
--   Returns <a>Nothing</a> when:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>The type was never registered.</li>
--   <li>The stored closure's erased type does not match <tt>request -&gt;
--   Task _ _</tt> (guards against <tt>unsafeCoerce</tt> slippage).</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Marked <tt>INLINE</tt> so the 'case Maybe' in <a>emit</a> can fuse
--   across module boundaries after specialisation, preserving ADR-0055
--   §3's "one direct function call per emit" hot-path property.
lookup :: (Integration request, Typeable request, Typeable (Response request)) => DispatchRegistry -> Maybe (request -> Task IntegrationError (Response request))

-- | Count of registrations.
size :: DispatchRegistry -> Int


-- | The production <tt>emit</tt> hot-path shim.
--   
--   At emit time, looks up the pre-bound closure in the
--   <a>DispatchRegistry</a> and invokes it directly. A missing entry is a
--   wire-up bug per ADR-0055 §6 — throws <a>PermanentFailure</a> rather
--   than silently falling back to <tt>runReal</tt>. Silent fallback would
--   (a) hide the misconfiguration, (b) bypass the selection-flag decision
--   made at startup, and (c) pay a dictionary-passing call per emit
--   instead of the pre-bound closure call the 50k req/s budget assumes.
module Service.Integration.ShimEmit
emit :: (Integration request, Typeable request, Typeable (Response request)) => DispatchRegistry -> request -> Task IntegrationError (Response request)

module Uuid
data Uuid
generate :: Task w Uuid
toLegacy :: Uuid -> UUID
fromLegacy :: UUID -> Uuid
toText :: Uuid -> Text

-- | Parse a UUID from its text representation. Returns Nothing if the text
--   is not a valid UUID.
fromText :: Text -> Maybe Uuid
nil :: Uuid
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Uuid.Uuid
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Uuid.Uuid
instance GHC.Show.Show Uuid.Uuid
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Uuid.Uuid

module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core

-- | A simple test query type for testing QueryObjectStore.
data TestQuery
TestQuery :: Uuid -> Text -> Int -> TestQuery
[queryId] :: TestQuery -> Uuid
[name] :: TestQuery -> Text
[count] :: TestQuery -> Int
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance GHC.Show.Show Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery

module Service.QueryObjectStore.Core

-- | Storage interface for query instances (read models).
--   
--   Similar in pattern to SnapshotCache, but specifically designed for
--   storing and retrieving query instances. Each query instance is
--   identified by a Uuid.
--   
--   The key operation is <a>atomicUpdate</a> which enables safe concurrent
--   updates to query instances when multiple entity events arrive
--   simultaneously.
--   
--   Example usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Get a specific query instance
--   maybeUserOrders &lt;- queryStore.get userId
--   
--   -- Atomically update a query instance
--   queryStore.atomicUpdate userId \maybeExisting -&gt;
--     case maybeExisting of
--       Just existing -&gt; Just (existing { orderCount = existing.orderCount + 1 })
--       Nothing -&gt; Just (UserOrders { userId = userId, orderCount = 1 })
--   
--   -- Get all query instances (for HTTP endpoint)
--   allUserOrders &lt;- queryStore.getAll
--   </pre>
data QueryObjectStore query
QueryObjectStore :: (Uuid -> Task Error (Maybe query)) -> (Uuid -> (Maybe query -> Maybe query) -> Task Error Unit) -> Task Error (Array query) -> QueryObjectStore query

-- | Get a query instance by its ID.
--   
--   Returns Nothing if no query instance exists with the given ID.
[get] :: QueryObjectStore query -> Uuid -> Task Error (Maybe query)

-- | Atomically update a query instance.
--   
--   The update function receives the current value (or Nothing if not
--   exists) and returns the new value (or Nothing to delete the entry).
--   
--   This operation is atomic to handle concurrent updates safely. When
--   multiple entity events arrive that affect the same query instance,
--   this ensures updates are applied sequentially without data loss.
--   
--   To delete an entry, return Nothing from the update function.
[atomicUpdate] :: QueryObjectStore query -> Uuid -> (Maybe query -> Maybe query) -> Task Error Unit

-- | Get all query instances.
--   
--   Used primarily for the HTTP endpoint: GET <i>queries</i>{query-name}
--   Returns all stored query instances as an array.
[getAll] :: QueryObjectStore query -> Task Error (Array query)

-- | Errors that can occur during QueryObjectStore operations.
data Error

-- | Generic storage failure
StorageError :: Text -> Error

-- | JSON serialization/deserialization failure
SerializationError :: Text -> Error

-- | Configuration typeclass for creating QueryObjectStore instances.
class QueryObjectStoreConfig config
createQueryObjectStore :: (QueryObjectStoreConfig config, FromJSON query, ToJSON query) => config -> Task Text (QueryObjectStore query)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.Error

module Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory

-- | Configuration for creating an in-memory QueryObjectStore.
--   
--   This is a simple configuration type with no options. Use it for
--   testing and development.
data InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig
InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig :: InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig

-- | Create a new in-memory QueryObjectStore.
--   
--   The store uses a ConcurrentVar containing a Map for atomic updates.
--   This ensures that concurrent calls to <a>atomicUpdate</a> are properly
--   serialized.
new :: Task Error (QueryObjectStore query)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.QueryObjectStoreConfig Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.InMemoryQueryObjectStoreConfig

module Service.QueryObjectStore

module Service.Event.StreamId
newtype StreamId
StreamId :: Text -> StreamId

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to StreamId.
--   
--   Note: These conversions are considered "safe" because: - StreamId →
--   StreamId: Already validated - Uuid → StreamId: UUIDs are fixed-length
--   (36 chars), always valid
--   
--   For Text, use <a>fromText</a> which validates length.
class ToStreamId idType
toStreamId :: ToStreamId idType => idType -> StreamId
new :: Task w StreamId
toText :: StreamId -> Text

-- | Create a StreamId from text with validation.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Err</tt> if the text exceeds <a>maxLength</a> (1024
--   characters). Use <a>fromTextUnsafe</a> only when you're certain the
--   input is valid (e.g., reading from a trusted database).
fromText :: Text -> Result Text StreamId

-- | Create a StreamId from text without validation.
--   
--   CAUTION: Only use this when reading from trusted sources (e.g.,
--   database) where the value was previously validated. For user input,
--   use <a>fromText</a>.
fromTextUnsafe :: Text -> StreamId

-- | Create a tenant-scoped StreamId by prefixing with the tenant UUID.
--   
--   Produces: <tt>tenant-{tenantUuid}/{streamIdText}</tt>
--   
--   Used by CommandExecutor for multi-tenant commands to ensure event
--   streams are physically isolated per tenant.
withTenant :: Uuid -> StreamId -> StreamId

-- | Maximum allowed length for a StreamId (1024 characters).
--   
--   StreamIds are used as keys in various places (database indexes, map
--   keys, log messages) and excessively long IDs can cause: - Performance
--   degradation in index lookups - Log spam and truncation - Potential DoS
--   vectors from unbounded input
maxLength :: Int
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance Service.Event.StreamId.ToStreamId Service.Event.StreamId.StreamId
instance Service.Event.StreamId.ToStreamId Uuid.Uuid

module Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot
data Snapshot state
Snapshot :: SnapshotKey -> state -> StreamPosition -> Snapshot state
[key] :: Snapshot state -> SnapshotKey
[state] :: Snapshot state -> state
[position] :: Snapshot state -> StreamPosition
data SnapshotKey
SnapshotKey :: EntityName -> StreamId -> SnapshotKey
[entityName] :: SnapshotKey -> EntityName
[streamId] :: SnapshotKey -> StreamId
instance GHC.Classes.Eq state => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.Snapshot state)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON state => Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.Snapshot state)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.Snapshot state)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance GHC.Show.Show state => GHC.Show.Show (Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.Snapshot state)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON state => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.Snapshot state)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.SnapshotCache.Snapshot.SnapshotKey

module Service.SnapshotCache.Core
data SnapshotCache state
SnapshotCache :: (EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (Maybe (Snapshot state))) -> (Snapshot state -> Task Error Unit) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error Unit) -> Task Error Unit -> SnapshotCache state
[get] :: SnapshotCache state -> EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (Maybe (Snapshot state))
[set] :: SnapshotCache state -> Snapshot state -> Task Error Unit
[delete] :: SnapshotCache state -> EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error Unit
[clear] :: SnapshotCache state -> Task Error Unit
data Error
StorageError :: Text -> Error
SerializationError :: Text -> Error
class SnapshotCacheConfig config
createSnapshotCache :: (SnapshotCacheConfig config, FromJSON state, ToJSON state) => config -> Task Text (SnapshotCache state)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.SnapshotCache.Core.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.SnapshotCache.Core.Error

module Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory
data InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig
InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig :: InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig
new :: Task Error (SnapshotCache state)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig
instance Service.SnapshotCache.Core.SnapshotCacheConfig Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.InMemorySnapshotCacheConfig

module Service.SnapshotCache

module Service.Event.EventMetadata
data EventMetadata
EventMetadata :: Uuid -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> DateTime -> Maybe StreamPosition -> Maybe StreamPosition -> EventMetadata
[eventId] :: EventMetadata -> Uuid
[relatedUserSub] :: EventMetadata -> Maybe Text
[correlationId] :: EventMetadata -> Maybe Text
[causationId] :: EventMetadata -> Maybe Text
[createdAt] :: EventMetadata -> DateTime
[localPosition] :: EventMetadata -> Maybe StreamPosition
[globalPosition] :: EventMetadata -> Maybe StreamPosition
new :: Task w EventMetadata
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.Event.EventMetadata.EventMetadata

module Service.Event
data Event eventType
Event :: EntityName -> StreamId -> eventType -> EventMetadata -> Event eventType
[entityName] :: Event eventType -> EntityName
[streamId] :: Event eventType -> StreamId
[event] :: Event eventType -> eventType
[metadata] :: Event eventType -> EventMetadata
data InsertionPayload eventType
InsertionPayload :: StreamId -> EntityName -> InsertionType -> Array (Insertion eventType) -> InsertionPayload eventType
[streamId] :: InsertionPayload eventType -> StreamId
[entityName] :: InsertionPayload eventType -> EntityName
[insertionType] :: InsertionPayload eventType -> InsertionType
[insertions] :: InsertionPayload eventType -> Array (Insertion eventType)
newtype StreamId
StreamId :: Text -> StreamId

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to StreamId.
--   
--   Note: These conversions are considered "safe" because: - StreamId →
--   StreamId: Already validated - Uuid → StreamId: UUIDs are fixed-length
--   (36 chars), always valid
--   
--   For Text, use <a>fromText</a> which validates length.
class ToStreamId idType
toStreamId :: ToStreamId idType => idType -> StreamId
newtype StreamPosition
StreamPosition :: Int64 -> StreamPosition
newtype EntityName
EntityName :: Text -> EntityName
data InsertionType
StreamCreation :: InsertionType
InsertAfter :: StreamPosition -> InsertionType
ExistingStream :: InsertionType
AnyStreamState :: InsertionType
data Insertion eventType
Insertion :: Uuid -> eventType -> EventMetadata -> Insertion eventType
[id] :: Insertion eventType -> Uuid
[event] :: Insertion eventType -> eventType
[metadata] :: Insertion eventType -> EventMetadata
data InsertionSuccess
InsertionSuccess :: StreamPosition -> StreamPosition -> InsertionSuccess
[globalPosition] :: InsertionSuccess -> StreamPosition
[localPosition] :: InsertionSuccess -> StreamPosition
data InsertionFailure
ConsistencyCheckFailed :: InsertionFailure
InsertionFailed :: Text -> InsertionFailure
PayloadTooLarge :: InsertionFailure
EmptyPayload :: InsertionFailure
payloadFromEvents :: EntityName -> StreamId -> Array eventType -> Task w (InsertionPayload eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq eventType => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq eventType => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.Event.Insertion eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Event.InsertionFailure
instance GHC.Classes.Eq eventType => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.Event.InsertionPayload eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Event.InsertionSuccess
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Event.InsertionType
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON eventType => Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.Event.Insertion eventType)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Event.InsertionFailure
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.Event.InsertionPayload eventType)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Event.InsertionSuccess
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Event.InsertionType
instance GHC.Classes.Ord eventType => GHC.Classes.Ord (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord eventType => GHC.Classes.Ord (Service.Event.Insertion eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Event.InsertionFailure
instance GHC.Classes.Ord eventType => GHC.Classes.Ord (Service.Event.InsertionPayload eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Event.InsertionSuccess
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Event.InsertionType
instance GHC.Show.Show eventType => GHC.Show.Show (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance GHC.Show.Show eventType => GHC.Show.Show (Service.Event.Insertion eventType)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Event.InsertionFailure
instance GHC.Show.Show eventType => GHC.Show.Show (Service.Event.InsertionPayload eventType)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Event.InsertionSuccess
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Event.InsertionType
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON eventType => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Service.Event.Event eventType)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON eventType => Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Service.Event.Insertion eventType)

instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.Event.InsertionType

module Service.Query.Registry

-- | Maps entity names to their associated query updaters.
data QueryRegistry

-- | A function that updates a query when an entity changes. Receives the
--   raw event (Event Json.Value) and handles deserialization, entity
--   reconstruction, and query update internally.
data QueryUpdater
QueryUpdater :: Text -> (Event Value -> Task Text Unit) -> QueryUpdater
[queryName] :: QueryUpdater -> Text
[updateQuery] :: QueryUpdater -> Event Value -> Task Text Unit

-- | Create an empty QueryRegistry.
empty :: QueryRegistry

-- | Check if the QueryRegistry has no registered updaters.
isEmpty :: QueryRegistry -> Bool

-- | Register a QueryUpdater for an entity type. Multiple updaters can be
--   registered for the same entity (when multiple queries depend on the
--   same entity).
register :: EntityName -> QueryUpdater -> QueryRegistry -> QueryRegistry

-- | Get all QueryUpdaters registered for a given entity type.
getUpdatersForEntity :: EntityName -> QueryRegistry -> Array QueryUpdater

-- | Get all QueryUpdaters registered across all entity types.
getAllUpdaters :: QueryRegistry -> Array QueryUpdater

-- | Merge all updaters from source registry into target registry. Updaters
--   for the same entity are combined (both will be called).
mergeInto :: QueryRegistry -> QueryRegistry -> QueryRegistry

module Service.EventStore.Core

-- | An interface for the operations of an event store
data EventStore eventType
EventStore :: (InsertionPayload eventType -> Task Error InsertionSuccess) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))) -> (StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))) -> (StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))) -> (StreamPosition -> Limit -> Array EntityName -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))) -> (StreamPosition -> Limit -> Array EntityName -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))) -> ((Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId) -> (StreamPosition -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId) -> ((Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId) -> (EntityName -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId) -> (SubscriptionId -> Task Error Unit) -> (EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Task Error Unit) -> Task Text Unit -> EventStore eventType

-- | Insert an event in a stream Returns the local and global stream
--   position if successful, or an error on conflict or failure.
[insert] :: EventStore eventType -> InsertionPayload eventType -> Task Error InsertionSuccess

-- | Read events from a stream in forward direction starting from a given
--   revision. Returns a stream of ReadStreamMessage or an Error.
[readStreamForwardFrom] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))

-- | Read events from a stream in backward direction starting from a given
--   revision. Useful for looking at recent events.
[readStreamBackwardFrom] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))

-- | Read all events from a specific stream for a given entity. Returns a
--   stream of ReadStreamMessage or an Error.
[readAllStreamEvents] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (Stream (ReadStreamMessage eventType))

-- | Read events from the global stream in forward direction starting from
--   a given global position. This reads across all streams and entities.
[readAllEventsForwardFrom] :: EventStore eventType -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))

-- | Read events from the global stream in backward direction starting from
--   a given global position. This reads across all streams and entities in
--   reverse order.
[readAllEventsBackwardFrom] :: EventStore eventType -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))

-- | Read events from the global stream in forward direction starting from
--   a given global position, but only for specific entities. Useful for
--   event sourcing projections that only care about certain entities.
[readAllEventsForwardFromFiltered] :: EventStore eventType -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Array EntityName -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))

-- | Read events from the global stream in backward direction starting from
--   a given global position, but only for specific entities. Useful for
--   filtered historical analysis and debugging.
[readAllEventsBackwardFromFiltered] :: EventStore eventType -> StreamPosition -> Limit -> Array EntityName -> Task Error (Stream (ReadAllMessage eventType))

-- | Subscribe to all events in the event store. The subscriber function
--   will be called for every event that gets appended. Returns a
--   SubscriptionId for unsubscribing.
[subscribeToAllEvents] :: EventStore eventType -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId

-- | Subscribe to all events from a specific global position onwards. This
--   allows catching up from historical events and then receiving new
--   events as they are appended.
[subscribeToAllEventsFromPosition] :: EventStore eventType -> StreamPosition -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId

-- | Subscribe to all events from the very beginning of the event store.
--   This delivers ALL historical events first, then continues with new
--   events as they are appended.
[subscribeToAllEventsFromStart] :: EventStore eventType -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId

-- | Subscribe to events for a specific entity. The subscriber function
--   will be called only for events belonging to the specified entity.
[subscribeToEntityEvents] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId

-- | Subscribe to events for a specific stream within an entity. The
--   subscriber function will be called only for events in the specified
--   entity and stream.
[subscribeToStreamEvents] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> StreamId -> (Event eventType -> Task Text Unit) -> Task Error SubscriptionId

-- | Unsubscribe from event notifications. After this call, the subscriber
--   function will no longer be called for new events.
[unsubscribe] :: EventStore eventType -> SubscriptionId -> Task Error Unit

-- | Removes all the events up to a position
[truncateStream] :: EventStore eventType -> EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Task Error Unit

-- | Release all resources held by this EventStore (connections, background
--   tasks)
[close] :: EventStore eventType -> Task Text Unit
data Error
StreamNotFound :: EntityName -> StreamId -> Error
EventNotFound :: StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Error
StorageFailure :: Text -> Error
SubscriptionNotFound :: SubscriptionId -> Error
SubscriptionError :: SubscriptionId -> Text -> Error
TruncationError :: EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Text -> Error
InsertionError :: InsertionFailure -> Error
ReadingAllError :: Text -> Error
newtype Limit
Limit :: Int64 -> Limit
newtype SubscriptionId
SubscriptionId :: Text -> SubscriptionId
data ReadAllMessage eventType
ReadingStarted :: ReadAllMessage eventType
AllEvent :: Event eventType -> ReadAllMessage eventType
ToxicAllEvent :: ToxicContents -> ReadAllMessage eventType
Checkpoint :: StreamPosition -> ReadAllMessage eventType
Terminated :: Text -> ReadAllMessage eventType
CaughtUp :: ReadAllMessage eventType
FellBehind :: ReadAllMessage eventType
data ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamReadingStarted :: ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamEvent :: Event eventType -> ReadStreamMessage eventType
ToxicStreamEvent :: ToxicContents -> ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamCheckpoint :: StreamPosition -> ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamTerminated :: Text -> ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamCaughtUp :: ReadStreamMessage eventType
StreamFellBehind :: ReadStreamMessage eventType
data ToxicContents
ToxicContents :: Text -> Value -> StreamPosition -> StreamPosition -> Text -> ToxicContents
[locator] :: ToxicContents -> Text
[metadata] :: ToxicContents -> Value
[globalPosition] :: ToxicContents -> StreamPosition
[localPosition] :: ToxicContents -> StreamPosition
[additionalInfo] :: ToxicContents -> Text
class EventStoreConfig config
createEventStore :: EventStoreConfig config => config -> Task Text (EventStore Value)
collectAllEvents :: Array (ReadAllMessage eventType) -> Array (Event eventType)
collectStreamEvents :: Array (ReadStreamMessage eventType) -> Array (Event eventType)
streamMessageToAllMessage :: ReadStreamMessage eventType -> ReadAllMessage eventType

-- | Wrap an EventStore Json.Value with typed
--   serialization/deserialization. This allows multiple services with
--   different event types to share a single EventStore.
castEventStore :: (FromJSON eventType, ToJSON eventType) => EventStore Value -> EventStore eventType
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Core.Error
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Core.Limit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq eventType => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.EventStore.Core.ReadAllMessage eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq eventType => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.EventStore.Core.ReadStreamMessage eventType)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Core.SubscriptionId
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Core.ToxicContents
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Core.Limit
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.EventStore.Core.ReadStreamMessage eventType)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Core.SubscriptionId
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Core.Limit
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Core.SubscriptionId
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Core.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Core.Limit
instance GHC.Show.Show eventType => GHC.Show.Show (Service.EventStore.Core.ReadAllMessage eventType)
instance GHC.Show.Show eventType => GHC.Show.Show (Service.EventStore.Core.ReadStreamMessage eventType)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Core.SubscriptionId
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Core.ToxicContents

module Service.EventStore

module Service.EntityFetcher.Core

-- | An entity fetcher is responsible for fetching an entity's current
--   state by reading all events from the event store and applying a
--   reduction function.
--   
--   The fetcher takes an initial state and a reduce function that applies
--   events to update the state.
data EntityFetcher state (event :: k)
EntityFetcher :: (EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (EntityFetchResult state)) -> EntityFetcher state (event :: k)

-- | Fetch an entity's current state by entity name and stream ID. Reads
--   all events from the event store and applies the reduction function to
--   compute the current state. Returns EntityNotFound if no events exist
--   for the stream, or EntityFound with the current state if events exist.
[fetch] :: EntityFetcher state (event :: k) -> EntityName -> StreamId -> Task Error (EntityFetchResult state)

-- | Result of fetching an entity from the event store
data EntityFetchResult state
EntityNotFound :: EntityFetchResult state
EntityFound :: FetchedEntity state -> EntityFetchResult state

-- | Entity state along with position information for caching
data FetchedEntity state
FetchedEntity :: state -> Maybe StreamPosition -> FetchedEntity state
[state] :: FetchedEntity state -> state
[lastPosition] :: FetchedEntity state -> Maybe StreamPosition

-- | Errors that can occur during entity operations
data Error
EventStoreError :: Error -> Error
ReductionError :: Text -> Error
CacheError :: Error -> Error

-- | Create a new entity fetcher with the given event store, initial state,
--   and reduce function.
--   
--   The reduce function takes an event and the current state, and returns
--   the new state after applying the event.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   fetcher &lt;- EntityFetcher.new eventStore initialState applyEvent
--   state &lt;- fetcher.fetch entityName streamId
--   
--   </pre>
new :: forall state event. EventStore event -> state -> (event -> state -> state) -> Task Error (EntityFetcher state event)

-- | Create a new entity fetcher with cache support.
--   
--   When a cache is provided, the fetcher will: 1. Check the cache for an
--   existing snapshot 2. If found, read only events after the snapshot
--   position 3. Apply new events on top of the cached state 4. Update the
--   cache with the final state
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   fetcher &lt;- EntityFetcher.newWithCache eventStore cache initialState applyEvent
--   state &lt;- fetcher.fetch entityName streamId
--   
--   </pre>
newWithCache :: forall state event. EventStore event -> SnapshotCache state -> state -> (event -> state -> state) -> Task Error (EntityFetcher state event)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq state => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.EntityFetcher.Core.EntityFetchResult state)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EntityFetcher.Core.Error
instance GHC.Classes.Eq state => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.EntityFetcher.Core.FetchedEntity state)
instance GHC.Show.Show state => GHC.Show.Show (Service.EntityFetcher.Core.EntityFetchResult state)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EntityFetcher.Core.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show state => GHC.Show.Show (Service.EntityFetcher.Core.FetchedEntity state)

module Service.EntityFetcher

module Service.Entity.Core

-- | The Entity typeclass represents domain objects that can be
--   reconstructed from events.
--   
--   Entities are the "left fold" of their event history - starting from an
--   initial state and applying each event in sequence via
--   <a>updateImpl</a>.
class Entity entity where {
    
    -- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
    --   
    --   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
    --   different ID types.
    type EntityIdType entity;
    type EntityIdType entity = Uuid;
}

-- | The initial state of the entity before any events are applied.
--   
--   This is used as the starting point when reconstructing an entity from
--   its event stream.
initialStateImpl :: Entity entity => entity

-- | Apply an event to the entity state, producing a new state.
--   
--   This is the reducer function used to reconstruct entity state from
--   events. It should be a pure function with no side effects.
updateImpl :: Entity entity => EventOf entity -> entity -> entity

-- | The Event typeclass defines how events are routed to their entity
--   streams.
--   
--   Each event must be able to identify which entity it belongs to via
--   <a>getEventEntityIdImpl</a>. This is used by the event store to
--   determine which stream to write the event to.
class Event event

-- | Extract the entity ID from an event.
--   
--   This allows the system to route events to the correct entity stream.
getEventEntityIdImpl :: Event event => event -> EntityIdType (EntityOf event)

-- | Maps a command type to its corresponding entity type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EntityOf CreateCart = CartEntity
--   </pre>
type family EntityOf command

-- | Maps an entity type to its corresponding event type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EventOf CartEntity = CartEvent
--   </pre>
type family EventOf entityType

module Service.Query.Core

-- | Represents the outcome of combining an entity state with a query.
--   
--   When an entity changes, we call <a>combine</a> to determine what
--   should happen to the corresponding query instance:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Update</a>: Store or update the query instance with new
--   data</li>
--   <li><a>Delete</a>: Remove the query instance from the store</li>
--   <li><a>NoOp</a>: Take no action (query instance remains
--   unchanged)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   combine user maybeExistingQuery =
--     case user.status of
--       Active -&gt; Update (UserOrders { userId = user.id, orderCount = ... })
--       Deleted -&gt; Delete
--       _ -&gt; NoOp
--   </pre>
data QueryAction query

-- | Store or update this query instance
Update :: query -> QueryAction query

-- | Remove this query instance from the store
Delete :: QueryAction query

-- | Take no action
NoOp :: QueryAction query

-- | Typeclass for query types (read models).
--   
--   This typeclass defines authorization for queries:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>canAccessImpl</a>: Checked before fetching data. "Can this user
--   access this query type?"</li>
--   <li><a>canViewImpl</a>: Checked after fetching data. "Can this user
--   view this specific instance?"</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   The user writes <tt>canAccess</tt> and <tt>canView</tt> functions in
--   the query module, and Template Haskell (<tt>deriveQuery</tt>) wires
--   them to these methods.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- In the query module:
--   canAccess :: Maybe UserClaims -&gt; Maybe AccessError
--   canAccess = authenticatedAccess
--   
--   canView :: Maybe UserClaims -&gt; CartSummary -&gt; Maybe AccessError
--   canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId)
--   
--   deriveQuery ''CartSummary [''CartEntity]
--   -- This generates:
--   -- instance Query CartSummary where
--   --   canAccessImpl = canAccess
--   --   canViewImpl = canView
--   </pre>
class Query query

-- | Authorization check before fetching data.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if access is allowed, or 'Just error' to
--   deny. This is called BEFORE any database/storage access.
canAccessImpl :: Query query => Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Authorization check after fetching data.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if viewing is allowed, or 'Just error' to
--   deny. This is called AFTER data is fetched, allowing ownership checks.
canViewImpl :: Query query => Maybe UserClaims -> query -> Maybe AccessError

-- | Maximum results this query type will return in a single page.
--   
--   Default: <a>absoluteMaxLimit</a> (1000). To override, define
--   <tt>maxResults :: Int</tt> in your query module before calling
--   <tt>deriveQuery</tt>.
maxResultsImpl :: Query query => Int

-- | Defines how an entity contributes to a query (read model).
--   
--   Each entity that participates in a query must implement this
--   typeclass, specifying:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>queryId</a>: Extract the query instance ID from an entity. For
--   a UserOrders query, both User and Order entities return the user's
--   ID.</li>
--   <li><a>combine</a>: Merge the entity state with the existing query
--   state (if any) to produce a QueryAction indicating what should happen
--   to the query.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance QueryOf User UserOrders where
--     queryId user = user.id
--     combine user maybeExisting = Update UserOrders
--       { userId = user.id
--       , userName = user.name
--       , orders = maybeExisting |&gt; Maybe.map (.orders) |&gt; Maybe.withDefault Array.empty
--       }
--   
--   instance QueryOf Order UserOrders where
--     queryId order = order.userId
--     combine order maybeExisting = case maybeExisting of
--       Just existing -&gt; Update existing { orders = existing.orders |&gt; Array.append order }
--       Nothing -&gt; NoOp  -- Can't add order to non-existent user query
--   </pre>
class (Entity entity, Query query) => QueryOf entity query

-- | Extract the query instance ID from an entity.
--   
--   This determines which query instance will be updated when the entity
--   changes. Multiple entities may map to the same query instance (e.g.,
--   User and Order both contribute to the same UserOrders instance, keyed
--   by user ID).
queryId :: QueryOf entity query => entity -> Uuid

-- | Combine entity state with existing query state to produce a
--   QueryAction.
--   
--   Called whenever an entity changes. The function receives the current
--   entity state and the existing query instance (if any), and returns a
--   QueryAction indicating whether to update, delete, or take no action on
--   the query.
combine :: QueryOf entity query => entity -> Maybe query -> QueryAction query

-- | Maps a query type to its constituent entity types.
--   
--   This type family declares which entity types contribute to a query. It
--   is generated automatically by Template Haskell via
--   <tt>deriveQuery</tt>.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Generated by: deriveQuery ''UserOrders ['User, 'Order]
--   type instance EntitiesOf UserOrders = '[User, Order]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This is used by the query subscriber to know which entity events
--   should trigger updates to which queries.
type family EntitiesOf query :: [Type]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq query => GHC.Classes.Eq (Service.Query.Core.QueryAction query)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.Query.Core.QueryAction query)
instance GHC.Show.Show query => GHC.Show.Show (Service.Query.Core.QueryAction query)

module Service.Query.Updater

-- | Create a QueryUpdater for a specific entity-query relationship.
--   
--   The updater handles the complete flow of updating a query when an
--   entity event occurs:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Receives a raw JSON event from the event store</li>
--   <li>Fetches the current entity state using the provided
--   EntityFetcher</li>
--   <li>Calls the <a>combine</a> function from <a>QueryOf</a> to determine
--   the QueryAction</li>
--   <li>Applies the action (Update, Delete, or NoOp) to the
--   QueryObjectStore</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   Example usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Create an updater for User -&gt; UserOrders relationship
--   userUpdater &lt;- Query.Updater.createUpdater @User @UserOrders
--     <a>UserOrders</a>
--     userEntityFetcher
--     userOrdersStore
--   
--   -- Register it in the QueryRegistry
--   let registry = Registry.register userEntityName userUpdater Registry.empty
--   </pre>
createUpdater :: (Entity entity, Query query, QueryOf entity query, FromJSON (EventOf entity)) => Text -> EntityFetcher entity (EventOf entity) -> QueryObjectStore query -> QueryUpdater

module Service.Query.Endpoint

-- | Create an endpoint handler for a query type.
--   
--   Returns a paginated response with items, total count, hasMore flag,
--   and the effective limit that was applied.
--   
--   This handler performs two-phase authorization: 1. canAccessImpl
--   (pre-fetch): "Can this user access this query type at all?" 2.
--   canViewImpl (post-fetch): "Can this user view this specific instance?"
--   
--   Pagination is applied AFTER authorization and NeoQL filtering. The
--   <tt>total</tt> count reflects only authorized, filtered results.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   handler &lt;- Endpoint.createQueryEndpoint @UserOrders queryStore
--   -- Returns: "{"items":[...],"total":142,"hasMore":true,"effectiveLimit":100}"
--   </pre>
createQueryEndpoint :: (ToJSON query, Query query) => QueryObjectStore query -> Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe Expr -> QueryPageRequest -> Task QueryEndpointError Text

module Service.Query

module Service.Entity


-- | Automatic schema generation for NeoHaskell types.
--   
--   This module provides a library-agnostic schema representation that can
--   be converted to OpenAPI, JSON Schema, or other formats.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   data AddItem = AddItem
--     { cartId :: Uuid
--     , productId :: Text
--     , quantity :: Int
--     }
--     deriving (Generic)
--   
--   instance ToSchema AddItem
--   -- Automatically generates schema with field names and types
--   </pre>
module Schema

-- | Library-agnostic schema representation. Can be converted to OpenAPI,
--   JSON Schema, CLI args, etc.
data Schema

-- | Unit, ()
SNull :: Schema

-- | Bool
SBool :: Schema

-- | Int, Integer
SInt :: Schema

-- | Double, Float
SNumber :: Schema

-- | Text, String
SText :: Schema

-- | Array a, [a]
SArray :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Maybe a (marks field as non-required)
SOptional :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Record types
SObject :: Array FieldSchema -> Schema

-- | Sum types without fields (data Status = Active | Inactive)
SEnum :: Array Text -> Schema

-- | Sum types with fields (data Shape = Circle {radius :: Int} | ...)
SUnion :: Array (Text, Schema) -> Schema

-- | Reference for recursive types
SRef :: Text -> Schema

-- | Schema for a single field in a record type.
data FieldSchema
FieldSchema :: Text -> Schema -> Bool -> Text -> FieldSchema
[fieldName] :: FieldSchema -> Text
[fieldSchema] :: FieldSchema -> Schema
[fieldRequired] :: FieldSchema -> Bool
[fieldDescription] :: FieldSchema -> Text

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to a Schema.
--   
--   Types with Generic instances get ToSchema for free using the default
--   implementation.
class ToSchema value
toSchema :: ToSchema value => Schema
($dmtoSchema) :: (ToSchema value, Generic value, GToSchema (Rep value)) => Schema

-- | Generic schema derivation for GHC.Generics representations.
class GToSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
