gToSchema :: GToSchema rep => Schema

-- | Generic field schema derivation for record fields.
class GToFieldSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
gToFieldSchema :: GToFieldSchema rep => Array FieldSchema

-- | Helper typeclass for sum type variants. Returns list of
--   (constructorName, schema) pairs.
class GToSumSchema (rep :: Type -> Type)
gToSumSchema :: GToSumSchema rep => Array (Text, Schema)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Schema.FieldSchema
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Schema.Schema
instance (Schema.GToFieldSchema left, Schema.GToFieldSchema right) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (left GHC.Generics.:*: right)
instance Schema.GToFieldSchema rep => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance (GHC.Generics.Selector selector, Schema.ToSchema fieldType) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.S1 selector (GHC.Generics.K1 index fieldType))
instance (GHC.Generics.Selector selector, Schema.ToSchema inner) => Schema.GToFieldSchema (GHC.Generics.S1 selector (GHC.Generics.K1 index (GHC.Maybe.Maybe inner)))
instance Schema.GToFieldSchema GHC.Generics.U1
instance (Schema.GToSumSchema left, Schema.GToSumSchema right) => Schema.GToSchema (left GHC.Generics.:+: right)
instance (Schema.GToFieldSchema rep, GHC.Generics.Constructor meta) => Schema.GToSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance Schema.GToSchema rep => Schema.GToSchema (GHC.Generics.D1 meta rep)
instance Schema.GToSchema GHC.Generics.U1
instance (Schema.GToSumSchema left, Schema.GToSumSchema right) => Schema.GToSumSchema (left GHC.Generics.:+: right)
instance (GHC.Generics.Constructor meta, Schema.GToFieldSchema rep) => Schema.GToSumSchema (GHC.Generics.C1 meta rep)
instance GHC.Show.Show Schema.FieldSchema
instance GHC.Show.Show Schema.Schema
instance Schema.ToSchema element => Schema.ToSchema (Array.Array element)
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Types.Bool
instance Schema.ToSchema Basics.Float
instance Schema.ToSchema Basics.Int
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Num.Integer.Integer
instance Schema.ToSchema GHC.Base.String
instance Schema.ToSchema element => Schema.ToSchema [element]
instance Schema.ToSchema inner => Schema.ToSchema (GHC.Maybe.Maybe inner)
instance Schema.ToSchema Text.Text
instance Schema.ToSchema Uuid.Uuid

module Service.FileUpload.Core

-- | Opaque reference to an uploaded file (used in command payloads) Show
--   instance is redacted to prevent leaking file identifiers in logs
newtype FileRef
FileRef :: Text -> FileRef

-- | Internal storage key for blob store Show instance is redacted to
--   prevent leaking storage paths in logs
newtype BlobKey
BlobKey :: Text -> BlobKey

-- | Pseudonymous owner identity (HMAC of user ID) Show instance is
--   redacted to prevent leaking user information in logs
newtype OwnerHash
OwnerHash :: Text -> OwnerHash

-- | SHA-256 hash of file content for deduplication. Encoded as a
--   64-character lowercase hex string (Base16). Show instance is redacted
--   to prevent leaking content fingerprints in logs.
newtype ContentHash
ContentHash :: Text -> ContentHash

-- | Metadata about an uploaded file
data FileMetadata
FileMetadata :: Text -> Text -> Int64 -> FileMetadata
[filename] :: FileMetadata -> Text
[contentType] :: FileMetadata -> Text
[sizeBytes] :: FileMetadata -> Int64

-- | Resolved file with full metadata (available in RequestContext.files)
data ResolvedFile
ResolvedFile :: FileRef -> FileMetadata -> BlobKey -> Int64 -> ResolvedFile
[ref] :: ResolvedFile -> FileRef
[metadata] :: ResolvedFile -> FileMetadata
[blobKey] :: ResolvedFile -> BlobKey
[uploadedAt] :: ResolvedFile -> Int64

-- | Shared error type for file access operations (resolution, download,
--   etc.)
data FileAccessError

-- | No file upload exists for this reference
FileNotFound :: FileRef -> FileAccessError

-- | Failed to query state store (preserves original error)
StateLookupFailed :: FileRef -> Text -> FileAccessError

-- | File exists but belongs to a different user
NotOwner :: FileRef -> FileAccessError

-- | File upload has expired (pending TTL exceeded)
FileExpired :: FileRef -> FileAccessError

-- | File has been deleted (named to avoid collision with FileDeleted
--   event)
FileIsDeleted :: FileRef -> FileAccessError

-- | File metadata exists but blob is missing from storage
BlobMissing :: FileRef -> FileAccessError

-- | Error accessing blob store
StorageError :: Text -> FileAccessError

-- | File lifecycle events (event-sourced state) Each constructor carries a
--   dedicated record type to avoid partial field selectors
data FileUploadEvent
FileUploaded :: FileUploadedData -> FileUploadEvent
FileConfirmed :: FileConfirmedData -> FileUploadEvent
FileDeleted :: FileDeletedData -> FileUploadEvent

-- | Data for FileUploaded event
data FileUploadedData
FileUploadedData :: FileRef -> OwnerHash -> ContentHash -> Text -> Text -> Int64 -> BlobKey -> Int64 -> Int64 -> FileUploadedData
[fileRef] :: FileUploadedData -> FileRef
[ownerHash] :: FileUploadedData -> OwnerHash
[contentHash] :: FileUploadedData -> ContentHash
[filename] :: FileUploadedData -> Text
[contentType] :: FileUploadedData -> Text
[sizeBytes] :: FileUploadedData -> Int64
[blobKey] :: FileUploadedData -> BlobKey
[expiresAt] :: FileUploadedData -> Int64
[uploadedAt] :: FileUploadedData -> Int64

-- | Data for FileConfirmed event
data FileConfirmedData
FileConfirmedData :: FileRef -> Text -> Int64 -> FileConfirmedData
[fileRef] :: FileConfirmedData -> FileRef
[confirmedByRequestId] :: FileConfirmedData -> Text
[confirmedAt] :: FileConfirmedData -> Int64

-- | Data for FileDeleted event
data FileDeletedData
FileDeletedData :: FileRef -> FileDeletionReason -> Int64 -> FileDeletedData
[fileRef] :: FileDeletedData -> FileRef
[reason] :: FileDeletedData -> FileDeletionReason
[deletedAt] :: FileDeletedData -> Int64

-- | Reason why a file was deleted
data FileDeletionReason
Orphaned :: FileDeletionReason
UserRequested :: FileDeletionReason
AdminPurge :: FileDeletionReason

-- | Declarative configuration for file uploads.
--   
--   This is the user-facing configuration type. IO initialization is
--   deferred to <a>run</a>, making application setup purely declarative.
--   
--   <h2>Basic Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withEventStore postgresConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withFileUpload FileUploadConfig
--         { blobStoreDir = "./uploads"
--         , stateStoreBackend = InMemoryStateStore  -- For development
--         , maxFileSizeBytes = 10485760  -- 10 MB
--         , pendingTtlSeconds = 21600    -- 6 hours
--         , cleanupIntervalSeconds = 900 -- 15 minutes
--         , allowedContentTypes = Nothing
--         , storeOriginalFilename = True
--         }
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Reusing PostgreSQL Connection Parameters</h2>
--   
--   For production, you can reuse the same connection parameters as your
--   event store:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Define once
--   postgresConfig = PostgresEventStore
--     { host = "localhost", port = 5432, databaseName = "myapp"
--     , user = "myuser", password = "mypassword"
--     }
--   
--   -- Reuse for file state store
--   fileUploadConfig = FileUploadConfig
--     { blobStoreDir = "./uploads"
--     , stateStoreBackend = PostgresStateStore
--         { pgHost = postgresConfig.host
--         , pgPort = postgresConfig.port
--         , pgDatabase = postgresConfig.databaseName
--         , pgUser = postgresConfig.user
--         , pgPassword = postgresConfig.password
--         }
--     , ...
--     }
--   
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withEventStore postgresConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withFileUpload fileUploadConfig
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Migration from Imperative Setup</h2>
--   
--   If you previously used the Task-based setup:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- OLD (imperative)
--   appTask :: Task Text Application
--   appTask = do
--     blobStore &lt;- LocalBlobStore.createBlobStore config
--     stateStore &lt;- PostgresFileStore.new postgresConfig
--     let setup = FileUpload.defaultFileUploadSetup blobStore stateStore
--     Task.yield (baseApp |&gt; Application.withFileUpload setup)
--   
--   -- NEW (declarative)
--   app :: Application
--   app = baseApp
--     |&gt; Application.withFileUpload FileUploadConfig
--         { blobStoreDir = "./uploads"
--         , stateStoreBackend = PostgresStateStore {...}
--         , ...
--         }
--   </pre>
--   
--   For config-dependent values, pass a factory function to
--   <a>withFileUpload</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.withFileUpload makeFileUploadConfig
--   </pre>
--   
--   The config type is inferred from the factory signature.
data FileUploadConfig
FileUploadConfig :: Text -> FileStateStoreBackend -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Maybe (Array Text) -> Bool -> FileUploadConfig

-- | Directory for storing uploaded files (created if missing)
[blobStoreDir] :: FileUploadConfig -> Text

-- | Where to track file lifecycle state
[stateStoreBackend] :: FileUploadConfig -> FileStateStoreBackend

-- | Maximum upload size (default: 10485760 = 10 MB)
[maxFileSizeBytes] :: FileUploadConfig -> Int64

-- | TTL for unconfirmed uploads before cleanup (default: 21600 = 6 hours)
[pendingTtlSeconds] :: FileUploadConfig -> Int64

-- | How often the cleanup worker runs (default: 900 = 15 minutes)
[cleanupIntervalSeconds] :: FileUploadConfig -> Int64

-- | MIME type allowlist (Nothing = all types allowed)
[allowedContentTypes] :: FileUploadConfig -> Maybe (Array Text)

-- | Whether to store the original filename in events (default: True)
[storeOriginalFilename] :: FileUploadConfig -> Bool

-- | Backend for storing file upload state (lifecycle tracking).
--   
--   Choose based on your deployment:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>InMemoryStateStore</a> - For development/testing. State is lost
--   on restart.</li>
--   <li><a>PostgresStateStore</a> - For production. Uses same connection
--   parameters as your event store.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Development
--   stateStoreBackend = InMemoryStateStore
--   
--   -- Production (same connection as event store)
--   stateStoreBackend = PostgresStateStore
--       { pgHost = "localhost"
--       , pgPort = 5432
--       , pgDatabase = "neohaskell"
--       , pgUser = "neohaskell"
--       , pgPassword = "neohaskell"
--       }
--   </pre>
--   
--   For config-dependent values, pass a factory function to
--   <a>withFileUpload</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.withFileUpload makeFileUploadConfig
--   </pre>
--   
--   The config type is inferred from the factory signature.
data FileStateStoreBackend

-- | In-memory storage (lost on restart, for development/testing)
InMemoryStateStore :: FileStateStoreBackend
PostgresStateStore :: Text -> Int -> Text -> Text -> Text -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> FileStateStoreBackend

-- | PostgreSQL host
[pgHost] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Text

-- | PostgreSQL port
[pgPort] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Int

-- | Database name
[pgDatabase] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Text

-- | Database user
[pgUser] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Text

-- | Database password
[pgPassword] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Text

-- | libpq TLS negotiation mode (WI-5 #684 / ADR-0064).
--   <tt>SslModeUnset</tt> (the dev/CI default) emits no sslmode param;
--   <tt>SslModeRequire</tt> and the verifying modes forbid a silent TLS
--   downgrade on the FileUpload pool.
[pgSslMode] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> SslMode

-- | Optional CA-bundle path for the verifying modes (WI-5 #684). Server
--   auth only -- never a client cert.
[pgSslRootCert] :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Maybe Text

-- | Internal configuration for file uploads (used after initialization).
--   
--   This type holds the runtime configuration after IO initialization has
--   occurred. Exported for use by other nhcore modules (Web.hs,
--   Application.hs). End users should use <a>FileUploadConfig</a> for
--   application configuration.
data InternalFileUploadConfig
InternalFileUploadConfig :: Int64 -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Maybe (Array Text) -> Bool -> InternalFileUploadConfig
[pendingTtlSeconds] :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> Int64
[cleanupIntervalSeconds] :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> Int64
[maxFileSizeBytes] :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> Int64
[allowedContentTypes] :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> Maybe (Array Text)
[storeOriginalFilename] :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> Bool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileAccessError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileConfirmedData
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletedData
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletionReason
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileStateStoreBackend
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadEvent
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadedData
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.InternalFileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Core.ResolvedFile
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileAccessError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileConfirmedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletionReason
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileMetadata
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileStateStoreBackend
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadConfig
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.InternalFileUploadConfig
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.ResolvedFile
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileAccessError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileConfirmedData
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletedData
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletionReason
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileStateStoreBackend
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadEvent
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadedData
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.InternalFileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Core.ResolvedFile
instance Data.Hashable.Class.Hashable Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileAccessError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileConfirmedData
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletedData
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletionReason
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileStateStoreBackend
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadEvent
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadedData
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.InternalFileUploadConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Core.ResolvedFile
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.BlobKey
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.ContentHash
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileAccessError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileConfirmedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileDeletionReason
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileMetadata
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileStateStoreBackend
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadConfig
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.FileUploadedData
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.InternalFileUploadConfig
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.OwnerHash
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Core.ResolvedFile
instance Schema.ToSchema Service.FileUpload.Core.FileRef

module Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle

-- | File upload lifecycle state machine
data FileUploadState
Initial :: FileUploadState
Pending :: PendingFile -> FileUploadState
Confirmed :: ConfirmedFile -> FileUploadState
Deleted :: FileUploadState

-- | A file in pending state (uploaded but not yet confirmed by a command)
data PendingFile
PendingFile :: FileMetadata -> OwnerHash -> Int64 -> PendingFile
[metadata] :: PendingFile -> FileMetadata
[ownerHash] :: PendingFile -> OwnerHash
[expiresAt] :: PendingFile -> Int64

-- | A file in confirmed state (successfully used by a command)
data ConfirmedFile
ConfirmedFile :: FileMetadata -> OwnerHash -> Text -> ConfirmedFile
[metadata] :: ConfirmedFile -> FileMetadata
[ownerHash] :: ConfirmedFile -> OwnerHash
[confirmedByRequestId] :: ConfirmedFile -> Text

-- | Full metadata for lifecycle state tracking
data FileMetadata
FileMetadata :: FileRef -> Text -> Text -> Int64 -> BlobKey -> Int64 -> ContentHash -> FileMetadata
[ref] :: FileMetadata -> FileRef
[filename] :: FileMetadata -> Text
[contentType] :: FileMetadata -> Text
[sizeBytes] :: FileMetadata -> Int64
[blobKey] :: FileMetadata -> BlobKey
[uploadedAt] :: FileMetadata -> Int64
[contentHash] :: FileMetadata -> ContentHash

-- | Apply an event to transition state
update :: FileUploadEvent -> FileUploadState -> FileUploadState

-- | Initial state for a new file upload stream
initialState :: FileUploadState

-- | Check if file can be confirmed at the given time Returns True only for
--   Pending state before expiry
canConfirm :: FileUploadState -> Int64 -> Bool

-- | Check if file has expired at the given time Returns True only for
--   Pending state at or after expiry
isExpired :: FileUploadState -> Int64 -> Bool

-- | Check if file can be deleted Returns True for Pending and Confirmed
--   states
canDelete :: FileUploadState -> Bool

-- | Get owner hash if available
getOwnerHash :: FileUploadState -> Maybe OwnerHash

-- | Get blob key if available
getBlobKey :: FileUploadState -> Maybe BlobKey

-- | Get metadata if available
getMetadata :: FileUploadState -> Maybe FileMetadata
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.ConfirmedFile
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileUploadState
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.PendingFile
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.ConfirmedFile
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileUploadState
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.PendingFile
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.ConfirmedFile
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileMetadata
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.FileUploadState
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Lifecycle.PendingFile

module Service.FileUpload.BlobStore

-- | Interface for blob storage backends Implementations: Local
--   (filesystem), S3 (future), GCS (future)
data BlobStore
BlobStore :: (BlobKey -> Bytes -> Task BlobStoreError Unit) -> (BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Bytes) -> (BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Unit) -> (BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Bool) -> BlobStore

-- | Store bytes under the given key (overwrites if exists)
[store] :: BlobStore -> BlobKey -> Bytes -> Task BlobStoreError Unit

-- | Retrieve bytes for the given key
[retrieve] :: BlobStore -> BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Bytes

-- | Delete blob (idempotent - succeeds if already deleted)
[delete] :: BlobStore -> BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Unit

-- | Check if blob exists
[exists] :: BlobStore -> BlobKey -> Task BlobStoreError Bool

-- | Errors that can occur when interacting with the blob store
data BlobStoreError
NotFound :: BlobKey -> BlobStoreError
StorageError :: Text -> BlobStoreError
InvalidBlobKey :: Text -> BlobStoreError

-- | Internal storage key for blob store Show instance is redacted to
--   prevent leaking storage paths in logs
newtype BlobKey
BlobKey :: Text -> BlobKey
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.BlobStoreError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.BlobStoreError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.BlobStoreError

module Service.FileUpload.Routes

-- | Upload request data (parsed from multipart form)
data UploadRequest
UploadRequest :: Text -> Text -> Bytes -> Text -> UploadRequest

-- | Original filename (may be empty)
[filename] :: UploadRequest -> Text

-- | MIME content type
[contentType] :: UploadRequest -> Text

-- | File content
[content] :: UploadRequest -> Bytes

-- | Hash of owner identity (for ownership checks)
[ownerHash] :: UploadRequest -> Text

-- | Successful upload response Note: blobKey is internal and intentionally
--   omitted from JSON serialization
data UploadResponse
UploadResponse :: FileRef -> BlobKey -> Text -> Text -> Int64 -> DateTime -> UploadResponse

-- | Opaque reference for use in commands
[fileRef] :: UploadResponse -> FileRef

-- | Internal key where blob is stored (NOT exposed to clients via JSON)
[blobKey] :: UploadResponse -> BlobKey

-- | Original filename
[filename] :: UploadResponse -> Text

-- | MIME content type
[contentType] :: UploadResponse -> Text

-- | File size in bytes
[sizeBytes] :: UploadResponse -> Int64

-- | When the pending upload expires
[expiresAt] :: UploadResponse -> DateTime

-- | Upload errors
data UploadError
FileTooLarge :: Int64 -> Int64 -> UploadError
[maxBytes] :: UploadError -> Int64
[actualBytes] :: UploadError -> Int64
InvalidContentType :: Text -> Array Text -> UploadError
[contentType] :: UploadError -> Text
[allowedTypes] :: UploadError -> Array Text
InvalidFilename :: Text -> UploadError
StorageFailure :: Text -> UploadError

-- | Handle file upload request 1. Validate content type and size 2.
--   Generate FileRef and BlobKey 3. Store blob in BlobStore 4. Return
--   response with metadata
--   
--   Note: Event emission (FileUploaded) is handled by the caller
--   (Transport layer) to allow for transaction coordination with
--   EventStore
handleUpload :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> BlobStore -> UploadRequest -> Task UploadError UploadResponse
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadRequest
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadResponse
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadRequest
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadResponse
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadRequest
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadResponse
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Routes.UploadResponse

module Service.FileUpload.Resolver

-- | A resolved file with validated metadata This is what commands see in
--   RequestContext.files
data ResolvedFile
ResolvedFile :: FileRef -> Text -> Text -> Int64 -> DateTime -> BlobKey -> ResolvedFile

-- | The original file reference
[ref] :: ResolvedFile -> FileRef

-- | Original filename
[filename] :: ResolvedFile -> Text

-- | MIME content type
[contentType] :: ResolvedFile -> Text

-- | File size in bytes (Int64 to match FileUploadConfig.maxFileSizeBytes)
[sizeBytes] :: ResolvedFile -> Int64

-- | When the file was uploaded
[uploadedAt] :: ResolvedFile -> DateTime

-- | Internal key for blob retrieval (not exposed to commands)
[blobKey] :: ResolvedFile -> BlobKey

-- | Type alias for file resolution errors (uses shared FileAccessError)
type ResolveError = FileAccessError

-- | Resolve a single file reference
--   
--   Validates: 1. File exists in event store 2. File is not deleted 3.
--   File is not expired (if pending) 4. Owner matches the requesting user
--   5. Blob exists in storage
resolveFileRef :: BlobStore -> (FileRef -> Task err (Maybe FileUploadState)) -> OwnerHash -> FileRef -> Task ResolveError ResolvedFile

-- | Resolve multiple file references Returns a Map from FileRef to
--   ResolvedFile Fails fast on first error
resolveFileRefs :: BlobStore -> (FileRef -> Task err (Maybe FileUploadState)) -> OwnerHash -> [FileRef] -> Task ResolveError (Map FileRef ResolvedFile)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Resolver.ResolvedFile
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.FileUpload.Resolver.ResolvedFile
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Resolver.ResolvedFile
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Resolver.ResolvedFile
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.FileUpload.Resolver.ResolvedFile


-- | Web transport integration for file uploads.
--   
--   This module provides route handlers for file upload and download that
--   can be integrated into WebTransport.
--   
--   <h1>Usage in Application</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withTransport WebTransport.server
--     |&gt; Application.withFileUpload fileUploadConfig
--   </pre>
module Service.FileUpload.Web

-- | Internal configuration for file upload routes (after initialization).
--   
--   This is created by <tt>initializeFileUpload</tt> from a
--   <tt>FileUploadConfig</tt>. Users should not create this directly - use
--   <tt>FileUploadConfig</tt> instead.
data FileUploadSetup
FileUploadSetup :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> BlobStore -> FileStateStore -> FileUploadSetup

-- | Upload limits and allowed content types
[config] :: FileUploadSetup -> InternalFileUploadConfig

-- | Where to store file content
[blobStore] :: FileUploadSetup -> BlobStore

-- | Where to track file lifecycle state
[stateStore] :: FileUploadSetup -> FileStateStore

-- | Default file upload configuration. Uses 10MB max file size, 6 hour
--   pending TTL, all content types allowed.
defaultFileUploadSetup :: BlobStore -> FileStateStore -> FileUploadSetup

-- | File upload route handlers.
data FileUploadRoutes
FileUploadRoutes :: (Text -> Text -> Text -> Bytes -> Task Text UploadResponse) -> (Text -> FileRef -> Task FileAccessError (Bytes, Text, Text)) -> (Text -> [FileRef] -> Task FileAccessError (Map FileRef ResolvedFile)) -> (FileRef -> Text -> Task Text ()) -> FileUploadRoutes

-- | Handle POST <i>files</i>upload Parameters: ownerHash, filename,
--   contentType, content
[handleUpload] :: FileUploadRoutes -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Bytes -> Task Text UploadResponse

-- | Handle GET <i>files</i>:fileRef Returns (content, contentType,
--   filename)
[handleDownload] :: FileUploadRoutes -> Text -> FileRef -> Task FileAccessError (Bytes, Text, Text)

-- | Resolve multiple file references for command context
[resolveFileRefs] :: FileUploadRoutes -> Text -> [FileRef] -> Task FileAccessError (Map FileRef ResolvedFile)

-- | Confirm a file after successful command execution
[confirmFile] :: FileUploadRoutes -> FileRef -> Text -> Task Text ()

-- | Create file upload route handlers from configuration.
createRoutes :: FileUploadSetup -> FileUploadRoutes

-- | Interface for storing file upload state. This allows different
--   backends (in-memory, event-sourced, database).
data FileStateStore
FileStateStore :: (FileRef -> Task Text (Maybe FileUploadState)) -> (FileRef -> FileUploadState -> Task Text ()) -> (FileRef -> FileUploadEvent -> Task Text ()) -> (OwnerHash -> ContentHash -> Task Text (Maybe FileRef)) -> FileStateStore

-- | Get the current state for a file reference
[getState] :: FileStateStore -> FileRef -> Task Text (Maybe FileUploadState)

-- | Set the state for a file reference
[setState] :: FileStateStore -> FileRef -> FileUploadState -> Task Text ()

-- | Apply an event to update state
[updateState] :: FileStateStore -> FileRef -> FileUploadEvent -> Task Text ()

-- | Find an existing non-deleted file with matching owner and content hash
[findByContentHash] :: FileStateStore -> OwnerHash -> ContentHash -> Task Text (Maybe FileRef)

-- | In-memory file state store for development/testing.
type InMemoryFileStateStore = ConcurrentMap FileRef FileUploadState

-- | Create a new in-memory file state store.
newInMemoryFileStateStore :: Task err InMemoryFileStateStore

-- | Create a FileStateStore backed by a ConcurrentMap.
inMemoryFileStateStore :: InMemoryFileStateStore -> FileStateStore

-- | Compute SHA-256 content hash of file bytes. Returns a 64-character
--   lowercase hex string (Base16 encoding). Follows the same pattern as
--   Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.TransactionKey but uses hex encoding
--   instead of base64url for VARCHAR(64) compatibility.
computeContentHash :: Bytes -> ContentHash

-- | Handle file upload. The caller (WebTransport) handles multipart
--   parsing and provides all parameters.
handleUploadImpl :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> BlobStore -> FileStateStore -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Bytes -> Task Text UploadResponse

-- | Start a background worker that periodically cleans up expired pending
--   files. Returns the AsyncTask handle for the worker (can be cancelled
--   on shutdown).
startCleanupWorker :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> FileStateStore -> BlobStore -> InMemoryFileStateStore -> Task Text (AsyncTask Text ())

-- | Clean up expired pending files. Returns the number of files cleaned
--   up.
cleanupExpiredFiles :: FileStateStore -> BlobStore -> InMemoryFileStateStore -> Task Text Int

module Service.FileUpload.Download

-- | Successful download response containing file content and metadata
data DownloadResponse
DownloadResponse :: Bytes -> Text -> Text -> DownloadResponse
[content] :: DownloadResponse -> Bytes
[contentType] :: DownloadResponse -> Text
[filename] :: DownloadResponse -> Text

-- | Type alias for download errors (uses shared FileAccessError)
type DownloadError = FileAccessError

-- | Handle a file download request Validates ownership and file state
--   before retrieving content
handleDownload :: Show stateError => BlobStore -> (FileRef -> Task stateError (Maybe FileUploadState)) -> OwnerHash -> FileRef -> Task DownloadError DownloadResponse
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.Download.DownloadResponse
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.Download.DownloadResponse
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.Download.DownloadResponse

module Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.Local

-- | Configuration for local filesystem blob store
data LocalBlobStoreConfig
LocalBlobStoreConfig :: Path -> LocalBlobStoreConfig

-- | Root directory where blobs are stored
[rootDir] :: LocalBlobStoreConfig -> Path

-- | Create a local filesystem blob store
createBlobStore :: LocalBlobStoreConfig -> Task Text BlobStore
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.Local.LocalBlobStoreConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.Local.LocalBlobStoreConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.BlobStore.Local.LocalBlobStoreConfig


-- | Request context for command authentication.
--   
--   This module provides the <a>RequestContext</a> type that is passed to
--   all command decide functions, enabling authorization decisions in the
--   domain layer.
--   
--   <h1>Design Philosophy</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Uniform signature**: ALL commands receive RequestContext, no
--   type-level complexity</li>
--   <li>**Explicit context**: Type signature shows what's available</li>
--   <li>**Domain-driven auth**: Commands decide their own authorization
--   logic</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Anonymous-friendly (ignore context)
--   decide :: CreateCart -&gt; Maybe CartEntity -&gt; RequestContext -&gt; Decision CartEvent
--   decide _ entity _ctx = ...
--   
--   -- Auth-required
--   decide :: AddItem -&gt; Maybe CartEntity -&gt; RequestContext -&gt; Decision CartEvent
--   decide cmd entity ctx = case ctx.user of
--     Nothing -&gt; Decider.reject "Authentication required"
--     Just user | entity.ownerId /= user.sub -&gt; Decider.reject "Not your cart"
--     Just user -&gt; Decider.acceptExisting [...]
--   
--   -- Using file attachments
--   decide :: UploadDocument -&gt; Maybe DocEntity -&gt; RequestContext -&gt; Decision DocEvent
--   decide cmd entity ctx = case Map.get cmd.attachment ctx.files of
--     Nothing -&gt; Decider.reject "Attachment not found or expired"
--     Just file -&gt; Decider.acceptExisting [DocumentUploaded { fileRef = file.ref, ... }]
--   </pre>
module Service.Auth

-- | Context for command execution.
--   
--   Available to ALL decide functions regardless of auth requirements.
--   This provides a uniform interface - commands decide what auth logic
--   they need.
data RequestContext
RequestContext :: Maybe UserClaims -> Map FileRef ResolvedFile -> Uuid -> DateTime -> Bool -> RequestContext

-- | User identity from validated JWT. Nothing if
--   anonymous/unauthenticated.
[user] :: RequestContext -> Maybe UserClaims

-- | Resolved file references from the command payload. Commands can look
--   up FileRef values to get file metadata.
[files] :: RequestContext -> Map FileRef ResolvedFile

-- | Unique identifier for this request (for tracing/logging)
[requestId] :: RequestContext -> Uuid

-- | When the request was received
[timestamp] :: RequestContext -> DateTime

-- | When True, the command executor's <tt>canExecuteImpl</tt> gate is
--   skipped. Set by <a>trustedContext</a> for transports that ran without
--   JWT auth configured (<a>withAuth</a> not wired); preserves the
--   "no-auth deployment" semantics where every command was reachable
--   regardless of its <tt>canAccess</tt> declaration. False on every other
--   constructor.
[trustedBypass] :: RequestContext -> Bool

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value

-- | Create an empty context for testing or internal use. Uses a nil UUID
--   and Unix epoch timestamp.
emptyContext :: RequestContext

-- | Create an anonymous context with generated request ID and current
--   timestamp.
anonymousContext :: Task err RequestContext

-- | Create an authenticated context with the given user claims.
authenticatedContext :: UserClaims -> Task err RequestContext

-- | Create a context for "no JWT auth configured" deployments.
--   
--   The command executor's <tt>canExecuteImpl</tt> gate is skipped when
--   this context is supplied, so every command remains reachable
--   regardless of its <tt>canAccess</tt> declaration. Only the Web
--   transport should construct this — and only when 'authEnabled =
--   Nothing' (i.e. the application never wired <a>withAuth</a>).
--   
--   Tests that exercise the gate should use <a>anonymousContext</a> or
--   <a>authenticatedContext</a>; <a>trustedContext</a> is reserved for the
--   transport-level configuration switch.
trustedContext :: Task err RequestContext

-- | Add resolved files to a request context. Used by the command execution
--   pipeline after resolving FileRefs.
withFiles :: Map FileRef ResolvedFile -> RequestContext -> RequestContext
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Auth.RequestContext
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Auth.RequestContext

module Schema.JsonSchema

-- | Convert a NeoHaskell Schema to a JSON Schema Aeson Value.
--   
--   Follows JSON Schema draft-07 semantics. See conversion rules below.
toJsonSchema :: Schema -> Value


-- | Fixed-point decimal type for precise financial calculations.
--   
--   <tt>Decimal</tt> stores values with 4 internal decimal places using
--   <tt>Int64</tt>, eliminating floating-point precision errors common
--   with <tt>Float</tt>/<tt>Double</tt>.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Decimal (Decimal)
--   import Decimal qualified
--   
--   -- Construction
--   let price = Decimal.decimal 12.50
--   let tax = Decimal.decimal 2.625
--   let total = price + tax
--   
--   -- Formatting
--   Decimal.formatDecimal total  -- "15.1250"
--   
--   -- JSON serialization (as string)
--   Json.toJSON price  -- String "12.5000"
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Internal Representation</h2>
--   
--   Intermediate arithmetic (multiplication, division) uses
--   arbitrary-precision <tt>Integer</tt> to avoid <tt>Int64</tt> overflow,
--   while stored values remain <tt>Int64</tt>.
--   
--   Values are stored multiplied by 10,000:
--   
--   <pre>
--   12.50   -&gt; 125000
--   0.0001  -&gt; 1
--   -5.25   -&gt; -52500
--   </pre>
module Decimal

-- | Fixed-point decimal with 4 internal decimal places.
--   
--   Internal representation: value * 10000
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal 125000  -- represents 12.5000
--   Decimal 1       -- represents 0.0001
--   Decimal (-52500) -- represents -5.2500
--   </pre>
newtype Decimal
Decimal :: Int64 -> Decimal
[unDecimal] :: Decimal -> Int64

-- | Create a <tt>Decimal</tt> from a <tt>Float</tt> value.
--   
--   <b>Note:</b> Uses <a>round</a> which performs round-half-to-even
--   (banker's rounding). For most use cases (constructing from
--   human-readable literals) this is fine. For precision-sensitive
--   callers, be aware that values at the exact midpoint of the smallest
--   representable unit may round unexpectedly: e.g. <tt>decimal
--   0.00005</tt> yields <tt>Decimal 0</tt>, not <tt>Decimal 1</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50   -- Decimal 125000
--   Decimal.decimal 0.01    -- Decimal 100
--   Decimal.decimal (-5.25) -- Decimal (-52500)
--   </pre>
decimal :: Float -> Decimal

-- | Create a <tt>Decimal</tt> from a cents value (2 decimal places).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.fromCents 1250  -- same as Decimal.decimal 12.50
--   Decimal.fromCents 100   -- same as Decimal.decimal 1.00
--   </pre>
fromCents :: Int64 -> Decimal

-- | Convert a <tt>Decimal</tt> to cents (truncates to 2 decimal places).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.toCents  -- 1250
--   Decimal.decimal 12.999 |&gt; Decimal.toCents -- 1299
--   </pre>
toCents :: Decimal -> Int64

-- | Convert a <tt>Decimal</tt> to a <tt>Float</tt>. This is a lossy
--   conversion and should only be used for display purposes.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.toFloat  -- 12.5
--   </pre>
toFloat :: Decimal -> Float

-- | Divide two <tt>Decimal</tt> values. Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if the
--   divisor is zero.
--   
--   NeoHaskell redefines <tt>/</tt> as <tt>Float -&gt; Float -&gt;
--   Float</tt>, so we provide this explicit division function instead of a
--   <tt>Fractional</tt> instance.
--   
--   Division truncates towards zero (integer division behavior).
--   Intermediate arithmetic uses <tt>Integer</tt> to avoid overflow.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.divide (Decimal.decimal 100.00) (Decimal.decimal 4.00) -- Just (Decimal.decimal 25.00)
--   Decimal.divide (Decimal.decimal 100.00) (Decimal.decimal 0.00) -- Nothing
--   </pre>
divide :: Decimal -> Decimal -> Maybe Decimal

-- | Format a <tt>Decimal</tt> as <tt>Text</tt> with 4 decimal places.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.50 |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal   -- "12.5000"
--   Decimal.decimal (-5.25) |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal  -- "-5.2500"
--   Decimal.decimal 0.00 |&gt; Decimal.formatDecimal     -- "0.0000"
--   </pre>
formatDecimal :: Decimal -> Text

-- | Parse a <tt>Text</tt> value into a <tt>Decimal</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "12.50"   -- Just (Decimal.decimal 12.50)
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "-5.25"   -- Just (Decimal.decimal (-5.25))
--   Decimal.parseDecimal "abc"     -- Nothing
--   Decimal.parseDecimal ""        -- Nothing
--   </pre>
parseDecimal :: Text -> Maybe Decimal

-- | Round a <tt>Decimal</tt> to 2 decimal places.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.decimal 12.5678 |&gt; Decimal.roundTo2  -- Decimal with internal value 125700
--   </pre>
roundTo2 :: Decimal -> Decimal

-- | The zero value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal.zero == Decimal.decimal 0.00
--   </pre>
zero :: Decimal
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Decimal.Decimal
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Num.Num Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Decimal.Decimal
instance GHC.Show.Show Decimal.Decimal
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Decimal.Decimal
instance Schema.ToSchema Decimal.Decimal

module Command
data OptionsParser value
data CommandOptions value
CommandOptions :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Version -> OptionsParser value -> CommandOptions value
[name] :: CommandOptions value -> Text
[description] :: CommandOptions value -> Text
[version] :: CommandOptions value -> Maybe Version
[decoder] :: CommandOptions value -> OptionsParser value
data PathConfig
PathConfig :: Text -> Text -> Char -> Text -> Maybe Path -> PathConfig
[help] :: PathConfig -> Text
[long] :: PathConfig -> Text
[short] :: PathConfig -> Char
[metavar] :: PathConfig -> Text
[value] :: PathConfig -> Maybe Path
data TextConfig
TextConfig :: Text -> Text -> Char -> Text -> Maybe Text -> TextConfig
[help] :: TextConfig -> Text
[long] :: TextConfig -> Text
[short] :: TextConfig -> Char
[metavar] :: TextConfig -> Text
[value] :: TextConfig -> Maybe Text
text :: TextConfig -> OptionsParser Text
path :: PathConfig -> OptionsParser Path
parseWith :: ToText value => (Text -> Result Text value) -> JsonConfig value -> OptionsParser value
json :: (Default value, ToText value, Eq value, FromJSON value) => JsonConfig value -> OptionsParser value
flag :: FlagConfig -> OptionsParser Bool
parseHandler :: CommandOptions event -> Task Error event
commands :: Array (CommandOptions value) -> OptionsParser value
map :: (a -> b) -> OptionsParser a -> OptionsParser b

-- | Generate a parser from a Schema that produces a JSON Value. This
--   allows schema-driven CLI argument parsing where each field in the
--   schema becomes a CLI option.
fromSchema :: Schema -> OptionsParser Value

-- | Generate a parser for a single field producing a (fieldName, value)
--   pair. The field name is converted to kebab-case for the CLI long
--   option.
fromFieldSchema :: FieldSchema -> OptionsParser (Text, Value)
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Command.OptionsParser
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.FlagConfig
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default value => Data.Default.Internal.Default (Command.JsonConfig value)
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.PathConfig
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Command.TextConfig
instance GHC.Base.Functor Command.OptionsParser
instance Unknown.Convertible value => GHC.Show.Show (Command.CommandOptions value)
instance GHC.Show.Show Command.Error
instance Unknown.Convertible value => GHC.Show.Show (Command.OptionsParser value)

module Decider

-- | The Decision monad for expressing command acceptance/rejection logic.
--   
--   Decision is a free monad that allows you to:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Generate UUIDs via <a>generateUuid</a></li>
--   <li>Accept commands with events via <a>acceptNew</a>,
--   <a>acceptExisting</a>, <a>acceptAny</a>, <a>acceptAfter</a></li>
--   <li>Reject commands with a reason via <a>reject</a></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Decisions are pure and can be easily tested without side effects.
data Decision a
[Return] :: forall a. a -> Decision a
[Bind] :: forall a1 a. Decision a1 -> (a1 -> Decision a) -> Decision a
[GenUuid] :: Decision Uuid
[Accept] :: forall a. InsertionType -> Array a -> Decision a
[Reject] :: forall a. Text -> Decision a

-- | Context required to execute a Decision.
--   
--   Provides effectful capabilities like UUID generation.
data DecisionContext
DecisionContext :: Task Text Uuid -> DecisionContext
[genUuid] :: DecisionContext -> Task Text Uuid

-- | The result of running a Decision.
--   
--   Either the command is accepted with events to persist, or rejected
--   with a reason.
data CommandResult event
AcceptCommand :: InsertionType -> Array event -> CommandResult event
RejectCommand :: Text -> CommandResult event

-- | Execute a Decision within a Task context, producing a CommandResult.
--   
--   The Decision must terminate with either <a>Accept</a> or
--   <a>Reject</a>. A bare <a>Return</a> or an <a>Accept</a>/<a>Reject</a>
--   in the middle of a chain will result in an error.
runDecision :: HasCallStack => DecisionContext -> Decision a -> Task Text (CommandResult a)

-- | Generate a UUID within a Decision context.
--   
--   This allows you to generate unique identifiers when constructing
--   events.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     cartId &lt;- Decision.generateUuid
--     Decision.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
generateUuid :: Decision Uuid

-- | Accept a command regardless of stream state, emitting the given
--   events.
--   
--   Use this when you don't care whether the entity exists or not. This is
--   the least safe option as it doesn't enforce any stream state
--   constraints.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity =
--     Decider.acceptAny [EventOccurred]
--   </pre>
acceptAny :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream doesn't exist yet, emitting the
--   given events.
--   
--   Use this for entity creation commands. The command will fail if the
--   entity already exists, preventing duplicate creation.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart already exists!"
--       Nothing -&gt; do
--         cartId &lt;- Decider.generateUuid
--         Decider.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
acceptNew :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream already exists, emitting the given
--   events.
--   
--   Use this for entity update commands. The command will fail if the
--   entity doesn't exist, ensuring you can't update something that hasn't
--   been created.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Nothing -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart does not exist!"
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decider.acceptExisting [CartUpdated]
--   </pre>
acceptExisting :: EventVariantOf event variant => Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Accept a command only if the stream is at the specified position,
--   emitting the given events.
--   
--   Use this for optimistic concurrency control. The command will fail if
--   the stream has been modified since the position was read, preventing
--   concurrent modification conflicts.
--   
--   Events can be the entity's event ADT directly, or any type with an
--   <a>EventVariantOf</a> instance for that ADT.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Nothing -&gt;
--         Decider.reject "Cart does not exist!"
--       Just e -&gt; do
--         let expectedPosition = e.version
--         Decider.acceptAfter expectedPosition [CartUpdated]
--   </pre>
acceptAfter :: EventVariantOf event variant => StreamPosition -> Array variant -> Decision event

-- | Reject a command with a reason message.
--   
--   Use this when a command cannot be processed due to business rule
--   violations, invalid state, or other constraints.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   decide cmd entity = do
--     case entity of
--       Just _ -&gt;
--         Decision.reject "Cart already exists!"
--       Nothing -&gt; do
--         cartId &lt;- Decision.generateUuid
--         Decision.acceptNew [CartCreated {cartCreatedId = cartId}]
--   </pre>
reject :: Text -> Decision a
instance GHC.Base.Applicative Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Classes.Eq event => GHC.Classes.Eq (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Base.Functor Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Base.Monad Decider.Decision
instance GHC.Classes.Ord event => GHC.Classes.Ord (Decider.CommandResult event)
instance GHC.Show.Show event => GHC.Show.Show (Decider.CommandResult event)

module Service.Command.Core

-- | The Command typeclass defines the contract for all commands.
--   
--   Commands represent user intent to change the system. Each command must
--   provide:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>getEntityIdImpl</a>: Extract the entity ID from the command
--   (Nothing for creation)</li>
--   <li><a>decideImpl</a>: Pure decision logic that produces events or
--   rejects</li>
--   </ul>
class Command command where {
    
    -- | Whether this command supports multi-tenancy.
    --   
    --   When True, <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> and <a>decideImpl</a> receive a
    --   tenant UUID as first argument.
    type IsMultiTenant command :: Bool;
    type IsMultiTenant command = 'False;
}

-- | Extract the entity ID from a command.
--   
--   Returns Nothing for creation commands where no entity exists yet.
getEntityIdImpl :: Command command => GetEntityIdFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityIdType (EntityOf command))

-- | The decision function that determines whether to accept or reject the
--   command.
--   
--   This is pure business logic that takes the command and optional
--   current entity state, returning a Decision that either accepts with
--   events or rejects with a reason.
decideImpl :: Command command => DecideFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityOf command) (EventOf (EntityOf command))

-- | Authorization check run before <a>decideImpl</a>.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if the caller may execute this command, or
--   <tt>Just</tt> an <a>AccessError</a> explaining why not. Defaults to
--   <a>authenticatedAccess</a> so a command with no <tt>canAccess</tt>
--   definition blocks unauthenticated callers — going public is an
--   explicit opt-in.
--   
--   The name pairs with the dispatcher entry point (<tt>execute</tt>) and
--   avoids a re-export clash with <a>canAccessImpl</a>. The user still
--   writes a top-level <tt>canAccess</tt>; the TH macro wires it here.
--   
--   An invalid token is never silently accepted: the Web transport rejects
--   any AuthError other than TokenMissing before reaching this method.
canExecuteImpl :: Command command => Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError

-- | The result of running a Decision.
--   
--   Either the command is accepted with events to persist, or rejected
--   with a reason.
data CommandResult event
AcceptCommand :: InsertionType -> Array event -> CommandResult event
RejectCommand :: Text -> CommandResult event

-- | Maps a type to its symbolic name.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance NameOf CreateCart = <a>CreateCart</a>
--   </pre>
type family NameOf t :: Symbol

-- | Maps a command type to its transport adapters (type-level list).
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance TransportsOf CreateCart = '[WebTransport]
--   </pre>
type family TransportsOf commandType :: [Type]

-- | Extracts the symbolic names from a type-level list of types.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   NamesOf '[WebTransport, CliTransport] ~ '[<a>WebTransport</a>, <a>CliTransport</a>]
--   </pre>
type family NamesOf (types :: [Type]) :: [Symbol]

-- | Appends two type-level symbol lists.
type family AppendSymbols (xs :: [Symbol]) (ys :: [Symbol]) :: [Symbol]

-- | Reifies a type-level list of symbols into an Array of Text values.
class AllKnownSymbols (names :: [Symbol])
allSymbolTexts :: AllKnownSymbols names => Array Text

-- | Determines the signature of <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> based on
--   multi-tenancy.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Single-tenant: <tt>command -&gt; Maybe id</tt></li>
--   <li>Multi-tenant: <tt>Uuid -&gt; command -&gt; Maybe id</tt></li>
--   </ul>
type family GetEntityIdFunction (isTenant :: Bool) command id

-- | Determines the signature of <a>decideImpl</a> based on multi-tenancy.
--   
--   ALL commands receive RequestContext for uniform authorization support.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Single-tenant: <tt>command -&gt; Maybe entity -&gt; RequestContext
--   -&gt; Decision event</tt></li>
--   <li>Multi-tenant: <tt>Uuid -&gt; command -&gt; Maybe entity -&gt;
--   RequestContext -&gt; Decision event</tt></li>
--   </ul>
type family DecideFunction (isTenant :: Bool) command entity event

-- | Singleton boolean for reifying IsMultiTenant at runtime. Used
--   internally by CommandExecutor to dispatch between single-tenant and
--   multi-tenant execution paths.
data SBool (b :: Bool)
[STrue] :: SBool 'True
[SFalse] :: SBool 'False

-- | Reify the IsMultiTenant type family at the value level.
class KnownMultiTenant (b :: Bool)
sbool :: KnownMultiTenant b => SBool b

-- | Context for command execution.
--   
--   Available to ALL decide functions regardless of auth requirements.
--   This provides a uniform interface - commands decide what auth logic
--   they need.
data RequestContext
RequestContext :: Maybe UserClaims -> Map FileRef ResolvedFile -> Uuid -> DateTime -> Bool -> RequestContext

-- | User identity from validated JWT. Nothing if
--   anonymous/unauthenticated.
[user] :: RequestContext -> Maybe UserClaims

-- | Resolved file references from the command payload. Commands can look
--   up FileRef values to get file metadata.
[files] :: RequestContext -> Map FileRef ResolvedFile

-- | Unique identifier for this request (for tracing/logging)
[requestId] :: RequestContext -> Uuid

-- | When the request was received
[timestamp] :: RequestContext -> DateTime

-- | When True, the command executor's <tt>canExecuteImpl</tt> gate is
--   skipped. Set by <a>trustedContext</a> for transports that ran without
--   JWT auth configured (<a>withAuth</a> not wired); preserves the
--   "no-auth deployment" semantics where every command was reachable
--   regardless of its <tt>canAccess</tt> declaration. False on every other
--   constructor.
[trustedBypass] :: RequestContext -> Bool

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value

-- | Errors that can occur during query authorization.
data AccessError

-- | The Entity typeclass represents domain objects that can be
--   reconstructed from events.
--   
--   Entities are the "left fold" of their event history - starting from an
--   initial state and applying each event in sequence via
--   <a>updateImpl</a>.
class Entity entity where {
    
    -- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
    --   
    --   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
    --   different ID types.
    type EntityIdType entity;
    type EntityIdType entity = Uuid;
}

-- | The initial state of the entity before any events are applied.
--   
--   This is used as the starting point when reconstructing an entity from
--   its event stream.
initialStateImpl :: Entity entity => entity

-- | Apply an event to the entity state, producing a new state.
--   
--   This is the reducer function used to reconstruct entity state from
--   events. It should be a pure function with no side effects.
updateImpl :: Entity entity => EventOf entity -> entity -> entity

-- | The Event typeclass defines how events are routed to their entity
--   streams.
--   
--   Each event must be able to identify which entity it belongs to via
--   <a>getEventEntityIdImpl</a>. This is used by the event store to
--   determine which stream to write the event to.
class Event event

-- | Extract the entity ID from an event.
--   
--   This allows the system to route events to the correct entity stream.
getEventEntityIdImpl :: Event event => event -> EntityIdType (EntityOf event)

-- | Maps a command type to its corresponding entity type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EntityOf CreateCart = CartEntity
--   </pre>
type family EntityOf command

-- | Maps an entity type to its corresponding event type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EventOf CartEntity = CartEvent
--   </pre>
type family EventOf entityType

-- | The Decision monad for expressing command acceptance/rejection logic.
--   
--   Decision is a free monad that allows you to:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Generate UUIDs via <a>generateUuid</a></li>
--   <li>Accept commands with events via <a>acceptNew</a>,
--   <a>acceptExisting</a>, <a>acceptAny</a>, <a>acceptAfter</a></li>
--   <li>Reject commands with a reason via <a>reject</a></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Decisions are pure and can be easily tested without side effects.
data Decision a
[Return] :: forall a. a -> Decision a
[Bind] :: forall a1 a. Decision a1 -> (a1 -> Decision a) -> Decision a
[GenUuid] :: Decision Uuid
[Accept] :: forall a. InsertionType -> Array a -> Decision a
[Reject] :: forall a. Text -> Decision a

-- | Context required to execute a Decision.
--   
--   Provides effectful capabilities like UUID generation.
data DecisionContext
DecisionContext :: Task Text Uuid -> DecisionContext
[genUuid] :: DecisionContext -> Task Text Uuid

-- | Execute a Decision within a Task context, producing a CommandResult.
--   
--   The Decision must terminate with either <a>Accept</a> or
--   <a>Reject</a>. A bare <a>Return</a> or an <a>Accept</a>/<a>Reject</a>
--   in the middle of a chain will result in an error.
runDecision :: HasCallStack => DecisionContext -> Decision a -> Task Text (CommandResult a)
instance (GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol name, Service.Command.Core.AllKnownSymbols rest) => Service.Command.Core.AllKnownSymbols (name : rest)
instance Service.Command.Core.AllKnownSymbols '[]
instance Service.Command.Core.KnownMultiTenant 'GHC.Types.False
instance Service.Command.Core.KnownMultiTenant 'GHC.Types.True

module Service.Transport.Internal
data InternalTransport
InternalTransport :: InternalTransport
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Transport.Internal.InternalTransport
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Transport.Internal.InternalTransport

module Service.Command

module Service.CommandExecutor.Core

-- | Configuration for command execution.
data CommandExecutor event
CommandExecutor :: EventStore event -> Int -> Int -> CommandExecutor event
[eventStore] :: CommandExecutor event -> EventStore event
[maxRetries] :: CommandExecutor event -> Int
[retryDelayMs] :: CommandExecutor event -> Int

-- | Result of executing a command through the CommandExecutor.
--   
--   This captures the full outcome including retry information for
--   debugging.
data ExecutionResult
CommandAccepted :: StreamId -> Int -> Int -> ExecutionResult
[streamId] :: ExecutionResult -> StreamId
[eventsAppended] :: ExecutionResult -> Int
[retriesAttempted] :: ExecutionResult -> Int
CommandRejected :: Text -> ExecutionResult
[reason] :: ExecutionResult -> Text
CommandFailed :: Text -> Int -> ExecutionResult
[error] :: ExecutionResult -> Text
[retriesAttempted] :: ExecutionResult -> Int
CommandUnauthorized :: AccessError -> ExecutionResult
[authError] :: ExecutionResult -> AccessError

-- | Execute a command through the event-sourcing pipeline.
--   
--   This function: 1. Extracts the entity ID from the command 2. Fetches
--   the current entity state from the event store 3. Runs the decision
--   logic to produce events or rejection (with RequestContext) 4. Persists
--   accepted events to the event store 5. Handles concurrency conflicts
--   with exponential backoff retry
execute :: (Command command, commandEntity ~ EntityOf command, commandEvent ~ EventOf commandEntity, commandEntity ~ EntityOf commandEvent, Event commandEvent, Entity commandEntity, ToStreamId (EntityIdType commandEntity), Eq (EntityIdType commandEntity), Show (EntityIdType commandEntity), Show commandEvent, KnownSymbol (NameOf command), KnownMultiTenant (IsMultiTenant command)) => EventStore commandEvent -> EntityFetcher commandEntity commandEvent -> EntityName -> RequestContext -> command -> Task Text ExecutionResult
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.CommandExecutor.Core.ExecutionResult
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Service.CommandExecutor.Core.CommandExecutor event)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.CommandExecutor.Core.ExecutionResult
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.CommandExecutor.Core.ExecutionResult
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.CommandExecutor.Core.ExecutionResult
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.CommandExecutor.Core.ExecutionResult

module Service.Response

-- | Domain-level response for command execution. This is what gets
--   serialized to API clients. Internal details like retry counts and
--   event counts are not exposed.
data CommandResponse
Accepted :: Text -> CommandResponse
[entityId] :: CommandResponse -> Text
Rejected :: Text -> CommandResponse
[reason] :: CommandResponse -> Text
Failed :: Text -> CommandResponse
[error] :: CommandResponse -> Text
Unauthorized :: AccessError -> CommandResponse
[authError] :: CommandResponse -> AccessError

-- | Convert internal ExecutionResult to client-facing CommandResponse
fromExecutionResult :: ExecutionResult -> CommandResponse
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Response.CommandResponse
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.Response.CommandResponse
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Response.CommandResponse
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Response.CommandResponse
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Response.CommandResponse
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Service.Response.CommandResponse

module Service.Transport.Mcp.Response

-- | Convert a CommandResponse to an MCP CallToolResult JSON value.
toCallToolResult :: CommandResponse -> Value

-- | Wrap query result text as an MCP resource content JSON value.
toResourceContent :: Text -> Text -> Value

module Service.Transport.Cli.Output
data OutputFormat
JsonOutput :: OutputFormat
PrettyOutput :: OutputFormat
QuietOutput :: OutputFormat

-- | Format a CommandResponse for CLI output
formatResponse :: OutputFormat -> CommandResponse -> Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Transport.Cli.Output.OutputFormat
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Transport.Cli.Output.OutputFormat

module Service.Transport

-- | Transport abstraction for different API protocols.
--   
--   A Transport defines how to: * Build command handlers that parse
--   requests and serialize responses * Assemble handlers into a runnable
--   server * Run the assembled server
class Transport transport where {
    
    -- | The request type for this transport.
    type Request transport;
    
    -- | The response type for this transport.
    type Response transport;
    
    -- | The assembled, runnable representation of this transport.
    type RunnableTransport transport;
}

-- | Assemble endpoint handlers into a runnable transport.
assembleTransport :: Transport transport => Endpoints transport -> RunnableTransport transport

-- | Run the assembled transport.
runTransport :: Transport transport => transport -> RunnableTransport transport -> Task Text Unit

-- | Build an endpoint handler for a specific command type.
--   
--   This handles: * Deserializing the request into the command type *
--   Passing RequestContext to the domain handler * Calling the domain
--   handler * Serializing the response
buildHandler :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (Transport transport, Command command, FromJSON command, name ~ NameOf command, KnownSymbol name) => transport -> Proxy command -> (RequestContext -> command -> Task Text CommandResponse) -> EndpointHandler

-- | Handler for a single command endpoint.
--   
--   Takes RequestContext, request bytes, and a callback to send the
--   response. The RequestContext provides authentication context for
--   authorization decisions.
type EndpointHandler = RequestContext -> Bytes -> (CommandResponse, Bytes) -> Task Text Unit -> Task Text Unit

-- | Handler for a single query endpoint.
--   
--   Takes Maybe UserClaims for authorization, Maybe NeoQL.Expr for
--   optional filtering, and returns query data as JSON Text. 1. Calling
--   canAccessImpl (pre-fetch auth check) 2. Fetching the data 3. Calling
--   canViewImpl on each result (post-fetch auth filter)
--   
--   Returns typed QueryEndpointError for proper HTTP status mapping: -
--   AuthorizationError Unauthenticated -&gt; 401 - AuthorizationError
--   Forbidden -&gt; 403 - AuthorizationError InsufficientPermissions -&gt;
--   403 - StorageError -&gt; 500
--   
--   Used for GET <i>queries</i>{query-name} endpoints.
type QueryEndpointHandler = Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe Expr -> QueryPageRequest -> Task QueryEndpointError Text

-- | Collection of command and query endpoints for a transport. Groups
--   command handlers and query handlers that share the same transport
--   configuration.
data Endpoints transport
Endpoints :: transport -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Endpoints transport
[transport] :: Endpoints transport -> transport
[commandEndpoints] :: Endpoints transport -> Map Text EndpointHandler
[queryEndpoints] :: Endpoints transport -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler
[commandSchemas] :: Endpoints transport -> Map Text EndpointSchema
[querySchemas] :: Endpoints transport -> Map Text EndpointSchema

-- | Schema information for an endpoint (command or query).
--   
--   Used for OpenAPI spec generation. Captured at registration time before
--   type erasure.
data EndpointSchema
EndpointSchema :: Maybe Schema -> Schema -> Text -> Bool -> Maybe Text -> EndpointSchema

-- | Request body schema. Commands have request bodies, queries don't.
[requestSchema] :: EndpointSchema -> Maybe Schema

-- | Response schema. All endpoints return responses.
[responseSchema] :: EndpointSchema -> Schema

-- | Human-readable description from Documented instance or empty.
[description] :: EndpointSchema -> Text

-- | Whether this endpoint is deprecated.
[deprecated] :: EndpointSchema -> Bool

-- | Entity name for OpenAPI tag grouping. Commands use NameOf entity,
--   queries use Nothing (grouped under fixed <a>Queries</a> tag).
[entityName] :: EndpointSchema -> Maybe Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Transport.EndpointSchema
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Transport.EndpointSchema


-- | Built-in endpoint schemas for WebTransport's non-command/query
--   endpoints.
--   
--   These schemas document the OAuth2 authentication and file upload
--   endpoints that are automatically added by WebTransport when
--   configured.
--   
--   Used by OpenAPI generation to include all endpoints in the API
--   documentation.
module Service.Transport.Web.BuiltinSchemas

-- | Generate endpoint schemas for OAuth2 routes.
--   
--   Returns schemas for <i>connect</i>{provider},
--   <i>callback</i>{provider}, <i>disconnect</i>{provider} when OAuth2 is
--   configured. Returns empty map when OAuth2 is not configured.
--   
--   All OAuth2 endpoints are tagged under <a>Authentication</a>.
oauth2EndpointSchemas :: Bool -> Map Text EndpointSchema

-- | Generate endpoint schemas for file upload routes.
--   
--   Returns schemas for POST <i>files</i>upload and GET
--   <i>files</i>{fileRef} when file uploads are configured. Returns empty
--   map when not configured.
--   
--   All file endpoints are tagged under <a>Files</a>.
fileUploadEndpointSchemas :: Bool -> Map Text EndpointSchema

module Service.Transport.Mcp.Protocol

-- | Mutable server state tracking initialization and cached responses.
data ServerState
ServerState :: ConcurrentVar Bool -> ConcurrentVar Bool -> Text -> Text -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler -> Value -> Value -> ServerState

-- | Whether <tt>initialize</tt> request was received
[initReceived] :: ServerState -> ConcurrentVar Bool

-- | Whether 'notifications/initialized' was received (full handshake
--   complete)
[initialized] :: ServerState -> ConcurrentVar Bool
[serverName] :: ServerState -> Text
[serverVersion] :: ServerState -> Text
[commandEndpoints] :: ServerState -> Map Text EndpointHandler
[queryEndpoints] :: ServerState -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler
[cachedToolsList] :: ServerState -> Value
[cachedResourcesList] :: ServerState -> Value

-- | Create initial server state with pre-computed cached responses.
newServerState :: Text -> Text -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Task Text ServerState

-- | Process a single JSON-RPC request and produce a response. Returns
--   Nothing for notifications (no response expected).
handleRequest :: ServerState -> JsonRpcRequest -> Task Text (Maybe JsonRpcResponse)

module Service.Query.Definition

-- | Captures how to wire a query type at runtime.
--   
--   A QueryDefinition contains all the information needed to: 1. Create a
--   QueryObjectStore for the query 2. Create EntityFetchers and
--   QueryUpdaters for each contributing entity 3. Register updaters in the
--   QueryRegistry 4. Create an HTTP endpoint handler 5. Capture the query
--   schema for OpenAPI generation
--   
--   QueryDefinitions are created declaratively via <a>createDefinition</a>
--   and evaluated at runtime by <a>runWith</a>.
data QueryDefinition
QueryDefinition :: Text -> Schema -> (EventStore Value -> Task Text (QueryRegistry, (Text, QueryEndpointHandler, EndpointSchema))) -> QueryDefinition
[queryName] :: QueryDefinition -> Text
[querySchema] :: QueryDefinition -> Schema

-- | Given an EventStore, creates the query infrastructure and returns: -
--   QueryRegistry entries for this query's entities - The query endpoint
--   name, handler, and schema
[wireQuery] :: QueryDefinition -> EventStore Value -> Task Text (QueryRegistry, (Text, QueryEndpointHandler, EndpointSchema))

-- | Create a QueryDefinition for a query type using in-memory storage.
--   
--   This automatically wires all entities listed in 'EntitiesOf query'.
--   The query name is derived from 'NameOf query' (kebab-case).
--   
--   This is a convenience function that uses <a>new</a> as the store
--   factory. For custom storage backends, use
--   <a>createDefinitionWithStore</a>.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- For a query with one entity:
--   cartSummaryDef = createDefinition @CartSummary
--   
--   -- For a query with multiple entities:
--   userOrdersDef = createDefinition @UserOrders
--   </pre>
createDefinition :: forall query (queryName :: Symbol) (entities :: [Type]). (Query query, ToSchema query, ToJSON query, FromJSON query, queryName ~ NameOf query, entities ~ EntitiesOf query, KnownSymbol queryName, WireEntities entities query) => QueryDefinition

-- | Create a QueryDefinition for a query type with a custom store factory.
--   
--   This automatically wires all entities listed in 'EntitiesOf query'.
--   The query name is derived from 'NameOf query' (kebab-case).
--   
--   The store factory is a Task that creates a QueryObjectStore for the
--   query type. This allows using different storage backends (in-memory,
--   PostgreSQL, etc.).
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Using in-memory store (same as createDefinition):
--   cartSummaryDef = createDefinitionWithStore @CartSummary (InMemory.new |&gt; Task.mapError toText)
--   
--   -- Using a custom store factory:
--   cartSummaryDef = createDefinitionWithStore @CartSummary myPostgresStoreFactory
--   </pre>
createDefinitionWithStore :: forall query (queryName :: Symbol) (entities :: [Type]). (Query query, ToSchema query, ToJSON query, FromJSON query, queryName ~ NameOf query, entities ~ EntitiesOf query, KnownSymbol queryName, WireEntities entities query) => Task Text (QueryObjectStore query) -> QueryDefinition

-- | Typeclass for wiring a list of entity types to a query.
--   
--   This recurses over the type-level list 'EntitiesOf query' and for each
--   entity creates an EntityFetcher, QueryUpdater, and registry entry.
class WireEntities (entities :: [Type]) query
wireEntities :: WireEntities entities query => Text -> EventStore Value -> QueryObjectStore query -> Task Text QueryRegistry
instance (Service.Entity.Core.Entity entity, Service.Query.Core.Query query, Service.Query.Core.QueryOf entity query, Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON (Service.Entity.Core.EventOf entity), Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON (Service.Entity.Core.EventOf entity), GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf entity), Service.Query.Definition.WireEntities rest query) => Service.Query.Definition.WireEntities (entity : rest) query
instance Service.Query.Definition.WireEntities '[] query


-- | OpenAPI 3.0 spec generation from NeoHaskell Schema types.
--   
--   This module converts library-agnostic Schema representations to
--   OpenAPI specifications using the openapi3 package.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Schema qualified
--   import Schema.OpenApi qualified as OpenApi
--   
--   -- Convert a Schema to OpenAPI Schema
--   let schema = Schema.SObject [...]
--   let openApiSchema = OpenApi.toOpenApiSchema schema
--   
--   -- Generate full OpenAPI spec
--   let apiInfo = ApiInfo "My API" "1.0.0" "API description"
--   let spec = OpenApi.toOpenApiSpec apiInfo cmdSchemas querySchemas
--   </pre>
module Schema.OpenApi

-- | Convert NeoHaskell Schema to OpenAPI Schema.
--   
--   This handles all Schema constructors and produces a corresponding
--   OpenAPI schema representation.
toOpenApiSchema :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification from endpoint schemas.
--   
--   Creates a complete OpenAPI spec with: - Info section (title, version,
--   description) - Tags for grouping endpoints (entity names for commands,
--   <a>Queries</a> for queries, <a>Authentication</a> for OAuth2,
--   <a>Files</a> for file upload) - Paths for all endpoint types -
--   Request/response schemas - Standard HTTP status codes
toOpenApiSpec :: ApiInfo -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> OpenApi

module Service.CommandExecutor

module Service.ServiceDefinition.Core
data Service (commandRow :: Row Type) (commandTransportNames :: [Symbol])
new :: Service ('[] :: [Pair Symbol Type]) ('[] :: [Symbol])

-- | Register a command type in the service definition
command :: forall cmd (originalCommands :: Row Type) (commandName :: Symbol) (commandTransports :: [Type]) (transportNames :: [Symbol]) (commandTransportNames :: [Symbol]) event entity (entityName :: Symbol) entityIdType. (Command cmd, ToSchema cmd, commandName ~ NameOf cmd, commandTransports ~ TransportsOf cmd, transportNames ~ NamesOf (PublicTransports commandTransports), AllKnownSymbols transportNames, BuildHandlersForTransports (PublicTransports commandTransports), entityName ~ NameOf entity, KnownSymbol commandName, KnownSymbol entityName, KnownHash commandName, entity ~ EntityOf cmd, event ~ EventOf entity, FromJSON event, ToJSON event, CommandInspect (CommandDefinition commandName commandTransports cmd transportNames event entity entityName entityIdType)) => Service originalCommands commandTransportNames -> Service ((commandName ':= CommandDefinition commandName commandTransports cmd transportNames event entity entityName entityIdType) ': originalCommands) (AppendSymbols transportNames commandTransportNames)

-- | Convert a Service to a ServiceRunner that can be used with
--   Application.
--   
--   The ServiceRunner will use the provided EventStore and transports
--   instead of creating its own. This allows multiple services to share
--   the same EventStore and transports within an Application.
toServiceRunner :: forall (cmds :: Row Type) (commandTransportNames :: [Symbol]) event entity. (event ~ ServiceEventType cmds, entity ~ ServiceEntityType cmds, FromJSON event, ToJSON event, FromJSON entity, ToJSON entity, KnownSymbol (NameOf entity)) => Service cmds commandTransportNames -> ServiceRunner

-- | A service runner that provides command endpoints grouped by transport.
--   
--   Services no longer run transports directly - instead, Application
--   collects all endpoints from all services and runs each transport once
--   with combined endpoints.
data ServiceRunner
ServiceRunner :: (EventStore Value -> Map Text TransportValue -> Task Text (Map Text (Map Text EndpointHandler), Map Text (Map Text EndpointSchema), Map Text EndpointHandler)) -> ServiceRunner

-- | Get command endpoints and schemas for this service, grouped by
--   transport name. Returns a tuple of (public handlers, public schemas,
