--   dispatch handlers). The dispatch handlers are transport-independent
--   command handlers used by integrations.
[getEndpointsByTransport] :: ServiceRunner -> EventStore Value -> Map Text TransportValue -> Task Text (Map Text (Map Text EndpointHandler), Map Text (Map Text EndpointSchema), Map Text EndpointHandler)
data TransportValue
TransportValue :: transport -> TransportValue

-- | Extracts the common event type from all commands in a service. All
--   commands must use the same event type. If they don't, a compile-time
--   error is generated with a helpful message.
type family ServiceEventType (cmds :: Row Type)

-- | Extracts the common entity type from all commands in a service. Since
--   all commands share the same event type, they also share the same
--   entity type.
type family ServiceEntityType (cmds :: Row Type)
instance (Service.Transport.Transport transport, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf transport), Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.BuildHandlersForTransports rest) => Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.BuildHandlersForTransports (transport : rest)
instance Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.BuildHandlersForTransports '[]
instance (Service.Command.Core.Command cmd, Service.Entity.Core.Entity entity, Service.Entity.Core.Event event, event GHC.Types.~ Service.Entity.Core.EventOf entity, entity GHC.Types.~ Service.Entity.Core.EntityOf cmd, entity GHC.Types.~ Service.Entity.Core.EntityOf event, entityIdType GHC.Types.~ Service.Entity.Core.EntityIdType entity, Service.Event.StreamId.ToStreamId entityIdType, GHC.Classes.Eq entityIdType, GHC.Classes.Ord entityIdType, GHC.Show.Show entityIdType, GHC.Show.Show event, Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON cmd, Schema.ToSchema cmd, Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.BuildHandlersForTransports (Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.PublicTransports transports), Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.KnownBool (Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.IncludesInternalTransport transports), Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.AssertNoMixedTransports transports, Service.Command.Core.AllKnownSymbols transportNames, name GHC.Types.~ Service.Command.Core.NameOf cmd, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol name, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol entityName, Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON event, Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON event, Service.Command.Core.KnownMultiTenant (Service.Command.Core.IsMultiTenant cmd), Data.Record.Anon.Plugin.Internal.Runtime.KnownHash name) => Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.CommandInspect (Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.CommandDefinition name transports cmd transportNames event entity entityName entityIdType)
instance Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.KnownBool 'GHC.Types.False
instance Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.KnownBool 'GHC.Types.True
instance GHC.Show.Show (Service.ServiceDefinition.Core.CommandDefinition name transports cmd transportNames event entity entityName entityIdType)

module Service.ServiceDefinition

module Service


-- | # Integration
--   
--   This module provides the integration pattern for connecting domain
--   events to external systems. Integrations come in two flavors:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Outbound**: React to domain events and trigger external
--   effects</li>
--   <li>**Inbound**: Listen to external sources and translate to domain
--   commands</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Two Personas</h2>
--   
--   The integration API is designed for two distinct users:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Nick (Integration Developer)**: Builds reusable integration
--   packages with HTTP calls, retries, authentication. Implements
--   <a>ToAction</a> instances.</li>
--   <li>**Jess (Integration User)**: Configures integrations by
--   instantiating config records. Never sees <a>Task</a>, error handling,
--   or networking code.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Usage (Jess's perspective)</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   userIntegrations :: User -&gt; UserEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   userIntegrations user event = case event of
--     UserRegistered info -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound Sendgrid.Email
--           { to = user.email
--           , templateId = "welcome"
--           }
--       ]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
module Integration

-- | Collection of outbound actions for a single event. Created by
--   <a>batch</a> or <a>none</a>.
data Outbound

-- | A single outbound action (type-erased). Created by Nick's
--   <a>ToAction</a> instances, used by Jess via <a>outbound</a>.
data Action

-- | Execution context for actions that need runtime dependencies.
--   
--   This is created by Application.run and passed to actions at execution
--   time. OAuth2-aware integrations use <a>secretStore</a> and
--   <a>providerRegistry</a>. File-processing integrations use
--   <a>fileAccess</a>.
data ActionContext
ActionContext :: SecretStore -> ImmutableProviderRegistry -> ConcurrentMap Text Lock -> Maybe FileAccessContext -> DispatchRegistry -> ActionContext
[secretStore] :: ActionContext -> SecretStore
[providerRegistry] :: ActionContext -> ImmutableProviderRegistry

-- | Per-key mutex map for token refresh operations (#333). Prevents
--   duplicate concurrent token refreshes for the same provider:userId.
[refreshLocks] :: ActionContext -> ConcurrentMap Text Lock

-- | File access context for retrieving uploaded files. <a>Nothing</a> when
--   file uploads are not enabled in the application.
[fileAccess] :: ActionContext -> Maybe FileAccessContext

-- | ADR-0055 registry of pre-bound outbound-integration dispatcher
--   closures. Populated once at startup by <a>run</a> based on the
--   <tt>--integrations</tt> CLI flag; read by <a>emit</a>. Defaults to the
--   empty registry in tests and non-integration contexts.
[outboundDispatch] :: ActionContext -> DispatchRegistry

-- | Context for accessing uploaded files from integrations.
--   
--   This allows integrations to retrieve file content and metadata for
--   processing (e.g., PDF text extraction, image analysis).
--   
--   The context is populated by <a>run</a> when file uploads are enabled
--   via <a>withFileUpload</a>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance ToAction PdfExtract.Request where
--     toAction config = Integration.action \ctx -&gt; do
--       fileAccess &lt;- case ctx.fileAccess of
--         Nothing -&gt; Task.throw (Integration.ValidationError "File uploads not enabled")
--         Just fa -&gt; Task.yield fa
--       pdfBytes &lt;- fileAccess.retrieveFile config.fileRef
--       -- ... process PDF ...
--   </pre>
data FileAccessContext
FileAccessContext :: (FileRef -> Task FileAccessError Bytes) -> (FileRef -> Task FileAccessError FileMetadata) -> FileAccessContext

-- | Retrieve file content by FileRef. Validates ownership internally based
--   on request context.
[retrieveFile] :: FileAccessContext -> FileRef -> Task FileAccessError Bytes

-- | Get file metadata (filename, content type, size) without retrieving
--   bytes.
[getFileMetadata] :: FileAccessContext -> FileRef -> Task FileAccessError FileMetadata

-- | Type-safe immutable registry of OAuth2 provider configurations.
--   
--   The constructor is NOT exported, preventing mutation via pattern
--   matching. Use <a>fromMap</a> to create and
--   <a>lookup</a>/<a>entries</a> to read.
--   
--   SECURITY: Prevents accidental mutation of provider configs at runtime.
--   Provider configs are set at startup and must not change.
data ImmutableProviderRegistry

-- | Create an ImmutableProviderRegistry from a Map. Called once at
--   application startup.
fromMap :: Map Text ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> ImmutableProviderRegistry

-- | Look up a provider by name. Returns Nothing if the provider is not
--   registered.
lookup :: Text -> ImmutableProviderRegistry -> Maybe ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig

-- | Get all providers as a list of (name, config) pairs.
entries :: ImmutableProviderRegistry -> Array (Text, ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig)

-- | Collect multiple actions into an <a>Outbound</a> value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   cartIntegrations cart event = case event of
--     ItemAdded {} -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound Command.Emit { ... }
--       ]
--   </pre>
batch :: Array Action -> Outbound

-- | No integrations for this event.
--   
--   <pre>
--   cartIntegrations cart event = case event of
--     CartCreated {} -&gt; Integration.none
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
none :: Outbound

-- | Convert a config record to a type-erased action. Used by Jess to wrap
--   config records for <a>batch</a>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.batch
--     [ Integration.outbound Sendgrid.Email { ... }
--     , Integration.outbound Slack.Message { ... }
--     ]
--   </pre>
outbound :: ToAction config => config -> Action

-- | Typeclass for converting config records to actions. Implemented by
--   Nick in integration packages.
class ToAction config
toAction :: ToAction config => config -> Action

-- | Create an action from a Task. Used by Nick in <a>ToAction</a>
--   instances.
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance ToAction Email where
--     toAction config = Integration.action _ctx -&gt; do
--       response <a>Http.post "..." |</a> Http.send
--       Integration.emitCommand (config.onSuccess response)
--   </pre>
action :: (ActionContext -> Task IntegrationError (Maybe CommandPayload)) -> Action

-- | Emit a command as the result of an integration action. The command
--   will be dispatched to the appropriate service.
--   
--   The command type must have a NameOf instance (derived via command TH).
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance ToAction Email where
--     toAction config = Integration.action _ctx -&gt; do
--       response &lt;- sendEmail config
--       Integration.emitCommand (config.onSuccess response)
--   </pre>
emitCommand :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (ToJSON command, name ~ NameOf command, KnownSymbol name) => command -> Task IntegrationError (Maybe CommandPayload)

-- | No command to emit. Used when integration doesn't produce a follow-up
--   command.
noCommand :: Task IntegrationError (Maybe CommandPayload)

-- | Build a CommandPayload from a command.
--   
--   This is the shared implementation used by <a>emitCommand</a>,
--   <a>encodeCommand</a>, and <a>inbound</a> to ensure consistent payload
--   construction.
--   
--   The command type must have a <a>NameOf</a> instance and
--   <tt>ToJSON</tt> instance.
makeCommandPayload :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (ToJSON command, name ~ NameOf command, KnownSymbol name) => command -> CommandPayload

-- | Encode a command as JSON text in CommandPayload format.
--   
--   This is the sync version of <a>emitCommand</a> for use in contexts
--   that need JSON text directly (e.g., OAuth2 callbacks).
--   
--   The command type must have a <a>NameOf</a> instance and
--   <tt>ToJSON</tt> instance.
--   
--   <b>Note</b>: Token types (<tt>TokenSet</tt>, <tt>AccessToken</tt>,
--   <tt>RefreshToken</tt>) have no <tt>ToJSON</tt> instance by design.
--   Store tokens in SecretStore and pass a reference key in your command
--   instead.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- In OAuth2 callback configuration:
--   onSuccess = \userId tokens -&gt; do
--     -- First, store tokens securely and get a reference key
--     let tokenKey = SecretStore.storeTokens userId tokens
--     -- Then encode a command with the key (not the tokens)
--     Integration.encodeCommand (OuraTokensStored userId tokenKey)
--   </pre>
encodeCommand :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (ToJSON command, name ~ NameOf command, KnownSymbol name) => command -> Text

-- | An inbound integration worker (type-erased).
newtype Inbound
Inbound :: InboundInternal -> Inbound

-- | Configuration for an inbound integration worker.
data InboundConfig command
InboundConfig :: ((command -> Task IntegrationError Unit) -> Task IntegrationError Unit) -> InboundConfig command
[run] :: InboundConfig command -> (command -> Task IntegrationError Unit) -> Task IntegrationError Unit

-- | Create an inbound integration from a config. Used by Nick in
--   integration packages.
--   
--   <pre>
--   webhook config = Integration.inbound
--     { run = emit -&gt; do
--         Http.serve "<i>webhooks</i>stripe" request -&gt; do
--           payload &lt;- Stripe.verifyAndParse request
--           emit (config.toCommand payload)
--     }
--   </pre>
inbound :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (ToJSON command, name ~ NameOf command, KnownSymbol name) => InboundConfig command -> Inbound

-- | Error types for integration failures.
data IntegrationError
NetworkError :: Text -> IntegrationError
AuthenticationError :: Text -> IntegrationError
ValidationError :: Text -> IntegrationError

-- | Retry after N seconds
RateLimited :: Int -> IntegrationError
PermanentFailure :: Text -> IntegrationError
UnexpectedError :: Text -> IntegrationError

-- | Payload for emitting a command from an integration.
data CommandPayload
CommandPayload :: Text -> Value -> CommandPayload
[commandType] :: CommandPayload -> Text
[commandData] :: CommandPayload -> Value

-- | Execute an Action and return its result.
--   
--   This function exposes the internal Task for testing and debugging. In
--   production, the runtime executes actions and dispatches resulting
--   commands.
--   
--   <pre>
--   action &lt;- Integration.outbound myConfig
--   result &lt;- Integration.runAction ctx action
--   case result of
--     Just payload -&gt; -- command was emitted
--     Nothing -&gt; -- no command to emit
--   </pre>
runAction :: ActionContext -> Action -> Task IntegrationError (Maybe CommandPayload)

-- | Extract actions from an Outbound for inspection.
--   
--   This function exposes the internal action list for testing and
--   debugging. In production, the runtime iterates over actions
--   automatically.
--   
--   <pre>
--   let outbound = Integration.batch [action1, action2]
--   let actions = Integration.getActions outbound
--   Array.length actions  -- 2
--   </pre>
getActions :: Outbound -> Array Action

-- | Run an inbound integration worker.
--   
--   The worker runs indefinitely, calling the emit callback whenever it
--   has a command to dispatch. This function exposes the internal runner
--   for the Application runtime.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.runInbound periodicCartCreator dispatchCommand
--   </pre>
runInbound :: Inbound -> (CommandPayload -> Task IntegrationError Unit) -> Task IntegrationError Unit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.CommandPayload
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.IntegrationError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.CommandPayload
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.CommandPayload
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.CommandPayload
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.IntegrationError
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.CommandPayload

module Service.OutboundIntegration.Core

-- | Typeclass for typed outbound integration handlers.
--   
--   Each handler type handles events for an entity. The framework
--   dispatches events to handlers based on the entity type.
--   
--   Jess never implements this typeclass manually — the
--   <tt>outboundIntegration</tt> TH macro generates the instance from her
--   <tt>handleEvent</tt> function.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data ReserveStockOnItemAdded = ReserveStockOnItemAdded
--     deriving (Generic, Typeable, Show)
--   
--   type instance EntityOf ReserveStockOnItemAdded = CartEntity
--   
--   handleEvent :: CartEntity -&gt; CartEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   handleEvent cart event = case event of
--     ItemAdded {stockId, quantity} -&gt; Integration.batch [...]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   
--   outboundIntegration ''ReserveStockOnItemAdded
--   </pre>
class OutboundIntegration handler where {
    
    -- | The event type this handler processes.
    --   
    --   Set by the TH macro to 'EventOf (EntityOf handler)' — the entity's
    --   full event ADT.
    type HandledEvent handler;
}

-- | Process an event for the given entity state.
--   
--   The entity is reconstructed from the event stream via replay. The
--   event is the entity's event ADT.
handleEventImpl :: OutboundIntegration handler => EntityOf handler -> HandledEvent handler -> Outbound


-- | # Service.Integration.Types
--   
--   Internal types for the integration dispatcher system.
--   
--   These types are used by the Application runtime to manage integration
--   workers. They are NOT part of the user-facing API - users interact
--   via:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Outbound</a> - for outbound integrations</li>
--   <li><a>Inbound</a> - for inbound integrations</li>
--   <li><a>OutboundConfig</a> - for stateful integrations</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Architecture</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   User API (Integration.*)          Internal Runtime (Service.Integration.*)
--   =====================================================================
--   Integration.Outbound      ---&gt;    OutboundRunner (type-erased, JSON)
--   Integration.Inbound       ---&gt;    (passed directly to worker)
--   Lifecycle.OutboundConfig  ---&gt;    OutboundLifecycleRunner -&gt; WorkerState
--   </pre>
module Service.Integration.Types

-- | A type-erased outbound integration runner.
--   
--   This wraps an integration function with JSON decoding so it can
--   process raw events from the event store. Created by
--   <a>withOutbound</a> or <a>createTypedOutboundRunner</a>.
--   
--   The <a>processEvent</a> function receives the EventStore so it can
--   reconstruct the entity state via event replay before calling the
--   integration function.
data OutboundRunner
OutboundRunner :: Text -> (ActionContext -> EventStore Value -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)) -> OutboundRunner
[entityTypeName] :: OutboundRunner -> Text
[processEvent] :: OutboundRunner -> ActionContext -> EventStore Value -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)

-- | A type-erased outbound integration runner with lifecycle management.
--   
--   Use this for integrations that need expensive resources like: -
--   Database connection pools - Embedded interpreters - gRPC channels
data OutboundLifecycleRunner
OutboundLifecycleRunner :: Text -> (StreamId -> Task Text WorkerState) -> OutboundLifecycleRunner
[entityTypeName] :: OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Text
[spawnWorkerState] :: OutboundLifecycleRunner -> StreamId -> Task Text WorkerState

-- | Internal state for a running lifecycle worker.
--   
--   Created by the lifecycle runner's <a>spawnWorkerState</a> function.
data WorkerState
WorkerState :: (Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)) -> Task Text Unit -> WorkerState
[processEvent] :: WorkerState -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)
[cleanup] :: WorkerState -> Task Text Unit


-- | # Integration Dispatcher
--   
--   Routes events to per-entity workers to ensure sequential processing
--   within each entity while allowing parallel processing across entities.
--   
--   <h2>Architecture</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Event from Global Subscription
--           |
--           v
--     IntegrationDispatcher
--           |
--           v
--     Route by StreamId
--           |
--      /----|----\
--     v     v     v
--   Worker Worker Worker
--    (A)    (B)    (C)
--   </pre>
--   
--   Each entity gets its own worker with a dedicated Channel. Events for
--   the same entity are processed sequentially by that entity's worker.
--   Different entities process in parallel.
--   
--   <h2>Lifecycle Management</h2>
--   
--   Workers with lifecycle support can hold expensive resources (DB
--   connections, interpreters, etc.). The dispatcher manages their
--   lifecycle:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>**Initialize**: Called when worker spawns for an entity</li>
--   <li>**Process**: Called for each event</li>
--   <li>**Cleanup**: Called when worker is reaped (idle) or on
--   shutdown</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   A background reaper task periodically checks for idle workers and
--   cleans them up to prevent resource leaks.
module Service.Integration.Dispatcher

-- | Dispatcher that routes events to per-entity workers.
--   
--   Uses ConcurrentMap for worker storage to enable fine-grained
--   concurrent access. This eliminates the MVar contention bottleneck that
--   would otherwise serialize all worker lookups at high throughput (50k+
--   events/second).
data IntegrationDispatcher

-- | Configuration for the dispatcher.
data DispatcherConfig
DispatcherConfig :: Int -> Int -> Bool -> Int -> Int -> Maybe Int -> Int -> Int -> DispatcherConfig

-- | How long a worker can be idle before being reaped (milliseconds).
--   Default: 60000 (60 seconds)
[idleTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | How often the reaper checks for idle workers (milliseconds). Default:
--   10000 (10 seconds)
[reaperIntervalMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Whether to enable the reaper. Set to False for testing. Default: True
[enableReaper] :: DispatcherConfig -> Bool

-- | Capacity of worker channels (bounded). Events queue up to this limit
--   before backpressure is applied. Default: 100 Sizing rule of thumb:
--   capacity = processing_rate * acceptable_latency
[workerChannelCapacity] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Timeout in milliseconds for writing to a full channel. If exceeded,
--   event is logged as dropped. Default: 5000 (5 seconds)
[channelWriteTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Timeout in milliseconds for processing a single event. If exceeded,
--   the event processing is cancelled and the worker continues with the
--   next event. Default: Nothing (no timeout). This prevents slow
--   integrations from blocking a worker indefinitely.
[eventProcessingTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Maybe Int

-- | Maximum number of retry attempts for transient IO failures in outbound
--   event processing. Only IO exceptions (e.g., network errors) are
--   retried; application-level errors (e.g., JSON decode failures) are
--   not. Default: 3 (total attempts = initial + 3 retries)
[maxEventRetries] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Base delay in milliseconds for exponential backoff between retries.
--   Actual delays: baseDelay * 2^attempt (e.g., 500ms, 1000ms, 2000ms).
--   Default: 500
[retryBaseDelayMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Default dispatcher configuration.
defaultConfig :: DispatcherConfig

-- | A type-erased outbound integration runner.
--   
--   This wraps an integration function with JSON decoding so it can
--   process raw events from the event store. Created by
--   <a>withOutbound</a> or <a>createTypedOutboundRunner</a>.
--   
--   The <a>processEvent</a> function receives the EventStore so it can
--   reconstruct the entity state via event replay before calling the
--   integration function.
data OutboundRunner
OutboundRunner :: Text -> (ActionContext -> EventStore Value -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)) -> OutboundRunner
[entityTypeName] :: OutboundRunner -> Text
[processEvent] :: OutboundRunner -> ActionContext -> EventStore Value -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)

-- | A type-erased outbound integration runner with lifecycle management.
--   
--   Use this for integrations that need expensive resources like: -
--   Database connection pools - Embedded interpreters - gRPC channels
data OutboundLifecycleRunner
OutboundLifecycleRunner :: Text -> (StreamId -> Task Text WorkerState) -> OutboundLifecycleRunner
[entityTypeName] :: OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Text
[spawnWorkerState] :: OutboundLifecycleRunner -> StreamId -> Task Text WorkerState

-- | Internal state for a running lifecycle worker.
--   
--   Created by the lifecycle runner's <a>spawnWorkerState</a> function.
data WorkerState
WorkerState :: (Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)) -> Task Text Unit -> WorkerState
[processEvent] :: WorkerState -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)
[cleanup] :: WorkerState -> Task Text Unit

-- | Create a new integration dispatcher (stateless runners only).
--   
--   The dispatcher starts with no workers. Workers are created on-demand
--   when events arrive for new entities.
new :: EventStore Value -> Array OutboundRunner -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Task Text IntegrationDispatcher

-- | Create a new integration dispatcher with lifecycle runners.
--   
--   Supports both stateless and lifecycle-managed runners.
newWithLifecycle :: EventStore Value -> Array OutboundRunner -> Array OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Task Text IntegrationDispatcher

-- | Create a new integration dispatcher with custom configuration.
newWithLifecycleConfig :: DispatcherConfig -> EventStore Value -> Array OutboundRunner -> Array OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> ActionContext -> Task Text IntegrationDispatcher

-- | Dispatch an event to the appropriate entity worker.
--   
--   If no worker exists for this entity, one is created. Events are queued
--   to the worker's channel for sequential processing.
--   
--   Uses atomic get-or-create to prevent race conditions when multiple
--   events for the same entity arrive simultaneously.
dispatch :: IntegrationDispatcher -> Event Value -> Task Text Unit

-- | Gracefully shutdown the dispatcher.
--   
--   Signals all workers to stop by sending Stop messages. Note: This is an
--   async operation - workers may not have stopped when this returns. For
--   production use, the application framework handles proper shutdown
--   coordination.
shutdown :: IntegrationDispatcher -> Task Text Unit

-- | Dispatch a command to the appropriate handler. Uses an anonymous
--   RequestContext since integrations are system-triggered.
dispatchCommand :: Map Text EndpointHandler -> CommandPayload -> Task Text Unit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Integration.Dispatcher.WorkerStatus
instance GHC.Show.Show value => GHC.Show.Show (Service.Integration.Dispatcher.WorkerMessage value)
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Integration.Dispatcher.WorkerStatus


-- | # Integration.Timer
--   
--   Timer-based inbound integrations for periodic command submission.
--   
--   <h2>Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.Timer qualified as Timer
--   
--   periodicCartCreator :: Integration.Inbound
--   periodicCartCreator =
--     Timer.every
--       { interval = Timer.seconds 30
--       , toCommand = _ -&gt; CreateCart
--       }
--   </pre>
module Integration.Timer

-- | Config record for periodic command submission.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Timer.every
--     { interval = Timer.seconds 30
--     , toCommand = tick -&gt; CreateCart
--     }
--   </pre>
data Every command
Every :: Interval -> (Int -> command) -> Every command
[interval] :: Every command -> Interval

-- | Tick count passed to command generator
[toCommand] :: Every command -> Int -> command

-- | Create an inbound integration that fires periodically.
--   
--   The <a>toCommand</a> function receives a tick count (starting at 1)
--   each time the timer fires.
--   
--   <pre>
--   periodicCartCreator :: Integration.Inbound
--   periodicCartCreator =
--     Timer.every
--       { interval = Timer.seconds 30
--       , toCommand = _ -&gt; CreateCart
--       }
--   </pre>
every :: (ToJSON command, KnownSymbol (NameOf command)) => Every command -> Inbound

-- | Time interval for periodic integrations (in milliseconds).
type Interval = Int

-- | Create an interval from seconds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Timer.seconds 30  -- 30 seconds
--   </pre>
seconds :: Int -> Interval

-- | Create an interval from minutes.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Timer.minutes 5  -- 5 minutes
--   </pre>
minutes :: Int -> Interval

-- | Create an interval from hours.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Timer.hours 1  -- 1 hour
--   </pre>
hours :: Int -> Interval


-- | # Integration.Lifecycle
--   
--   Lifecycle management for stateful integrations.
--   
--   Use this module when your integration needs to manage expensive
--   resources like:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Database connection pools</li>
--   <li>Embedded interpreters (Lua, Python, etc.)</li>
--   <li>gRPC channels</li>
--   <li>WebSocket subscriptions</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration.Lifecycle qualified as Lifecycle
--   
--   postgresNotifier :: Lifecycle.OutboundConfig PostgresPool
--   postgresNotifier = Lifecycle.OutboundConfig
--     { initialize = \streamId -&gt; do
--         pool &lt;- Postgres.createPool connectionString
--         Task.yield pool
--   
--     , processEvent = \pool event -&gt; do
--         Postgres.notify pool event
--         Task.yield []
--   
--     , cleanup = \pool -&gt; do
--         Postgres.closePool pool
--     }
--   </pre>
--   
--   Workers are created per-entity. When a worker is idle for too long
--   (configurable timeout), it is reaped and cleanup is called. The next
--   event for that entity will spawn a fresh worker.
module Integration.Lifecycle

-- | Configuration for an outbound integration with resource lifecycle.
--   
--   The lifecycle hooks are called as follows:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li><tt>initialize</tt> - Called once when a worker is spawned for an
--   entity</li>
--   <li><tt>processEvent</tt> - Called for each event (uses state from
--   initialize)</li>
--   <li><tt>cleanup</tt> - Called when the worker is reaped (idle timeout)
--   or on shutdown</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   If a worker is reaped and a new event arrives for that entity,
--   <tt>initialize</tt> is called again to create a fresh worker.
data OutboundConfig state
OutboundConfig :: (StreamId -> Task Text state) -> (state -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)) -> (state -> Task Text Unit) -> OutboundConfig state

-- | Initialize resources for this entity.
--   
--   Called once when the worker is spawned. The returned state is passed
--   to <tt>processEvent</tt> and <tt>cleanup</tt>.
--   
--   Should be idempotent - may be called multiple times if worker is
--   reaped and restarted.
[initialize] :: OutboundConfig state -> StreamId -> Task Text state

-- | Process an event using the initialized state.
--   
--   Called for each event that arrives for this entity. Returns command
--   payloads to dispatch to other services.
[processEvent] :: OutboundConfig state -> state -> Event Value -> Task Text (Array CommandPayload)

-- | Cleanup resources when the worker is reaped or on shutdown.
--   
--   Should release any resources created in <tt>initialize</tt> (close
--   connections, stop interpreters, etc.).
--   
--   Should complete quickly. Long-running cleanup operations may delay
--   worker shutdown and system termination.
[cleanup] :: OutboundConfig state -> state -> Task Text Unit


-- | # Service.Application.Integrations
--   
--   Integration configuration and runtime for the Application.
--   
--   This module provides:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>withOutboundLifecycle</a> - Register stateful integrations with
--   resource management</li>
--   <li><a>withInbound</a> - Register inbound integrations (external -&gt;
--   commands)</li>
--   <li>Runtime functions for starting integration workers</li>
--   </ul>
module Service.Application.Integrations

-- | Register an outbound integration with lifecycle management.
--   
--   See <a>createOutboundLifecycleRunner</a> for details.
withOutboundLifecycle :: Inspectable entity => OutboundConfig state -> (Array OutboundRunner, Array OutboundLifecycleRunner, Array Inbound) -> (Array OutboundRunner, Array OutboundLifecycleRunner, Array Inbound)

-- | Register an inbound integration worker.
--   
--   Inbound integrations listen to external sources (timers, webhooks,
--   message queues) and translate external events into domain commands.
--   
--   When the Application runs, each inbound worker is started in its own
--   background task. Workers emit commands that are dispatched to the
--   appropriate service handlers.
withInbound :: Inbound -> (Array OutboundRunner, Array OutboundLifecycleRunner, Array Inbound) -> (Array OutboundRunner, Array OutboundLifecycleRunner, Array Inbound)

-- | Create an OutboundRunner from a typed OutboundIntegration handler.
--   
--   This wraps the typed handler with: 1. JSON decoding of the full event
--   ADT 2. Entity state reconstruction via event replay 3. Dispatch to
--   <a>handleEventImpl</a>
--   
--   The handler's entity type and event type are determined by the type
--   constraints. The event is decoded from JSON, the entity is
--   reconstructed via event replay, and handleEventImpl is called.
createTypedOutboundRunner :: (OutboundIntegration handler, entity ~ EntityOf handler, event ~ EventOf entity, event ~ HandledEvent handler, FromJSON entity, FromJSON event, Inspectable entity, Default entity, Entity entity) => OutboundRunner

-- | Register an outbound integration with lifecycle management.
--   
--   Use this for integrations that need expensive resources like:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Database connection pools</li>
--   <li>Embedded interpreters (Lua, Python, etc.)</li>
--   <li>gRPC channels</li>
--   <li>WebSocket subscriptions</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Workers are created per-entity. When a worker is idle for too long, it
--   is reaped and cleanup is called. The next event for that entity will
--   spawn a fresh worker with initialize called again.
createOutboundLifecycleRunner :: Inspectable entity => OutboundConfig state -> OutboundLifecycleRunner

-- | Start subscription for outbound integrations.
--   
--   Subscribes to all events and processes them through registered
--   OutboundRunners. Commands emitted by integrations are dispatched to
--   the appropriate service handlers.
--   
--   Uses a Dispatcher to route events to per-entity workers, ensuring
--   sequential processing within each entity while allowing parallel
--   processing across entities.
--   
--   Returns the Dispatcher so it can be shutdown when the application
--   stops.
startIntegrationSubscriber :: Maybe DispatcherConfig -> EventStore Value -> Array OutboundRunner -> Array OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> ActionContext -> Task Text (Maybe IntegrationDispatcher)

-- | Start all inbound integration workers.
--   
--   Each worker runs in its own background task, emitting commands that
--   are dispatched to the appropriate service handlers. Workers run
--   indefinitely with automatic restart on failure (with exponential
--   backoff).
--   
--   Returns the spawned AsyncTasks so they can be cancelled on shutdown.
startInboundWorkers :: Array Inbound -> Map Text EndpointHandler -> Task Text (Array (AsyncTask Text Unit))

module Integration.Exit

-- | Create an outbound integration that exits the process when any event
--   fires.
--   
--   Use this as the <tt>handleEvent</tt> function in an
--   OutboundIntegration handler.
onEvent :: entity -> event -> Outbound
instance Integration.ToAction Integration.Exit.ExitAction


-- | # Integration.Command
--   
--   Built-in integration action for emitting commands to other services.
--   This is the foundation of the Process Manager pattern - coordinating
--   workflows across aggregate boundaries.
--   
--   <h2>Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.Command qualified as Command
--   
--   cartIntegrations :: CartEntity -&gt; CartEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   cartIntegrations cart event = case event of
--     ItemAdded {stockId, quantity} -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound Command.Emit
--           { command = ReserveStock
--               { stockId = stockId
--               , quantity = quantity
--               , cartId = cart.cartId
--               }
--           }
--       ]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
module Integration.Command

-- | Config record for emitting a command as an integration action.
--   
--   This is the simplest integration action - it just dispatches a command
--   to another service without any external HTTP calls or side effects.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound Command.Emit
--     { command = ReserveStock { stockId, quantity, cartId }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Emit command
Emit :: command -> Emit command
[command] :: Emit command -> command
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Integration.Command.Emit command)
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Command.Emit command)

module Var
data Var value
new :: value -> Task err (Var value)
get :: Var value -> Task err value
set :: value -> Var value -> Task err Unit
increment :: Num number => Var number -> Task err Unit
decrement :: Num number => Var number -> Task err Unit

module Version

-- | A <a>Version</a> represents the version of a software entity.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Eq</a> is provided, which implements exact equality
--   modulo reordering of the tags in the <a>versionTags</a> field.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Ord</a> is also provided, which gives lexicographic
--   ordering on the <a>versionBranch</a> fields (i.e. 2.1 &gt; 2.0, 1.2.3
--   &gt; 1.2.2, etc.). This is expected to be sufficient for many uses,
--   but note that you may need to use a more specific ordering for your
--   versioning scheme. For example, some versioning schemes may include
--   pre-releases which have tags <tt>"pre1"</tt>, <tt>"pre2"</tt>, and so
--   on, and these would need to be taken into account when determining
--   ordering. In some cases, date ordering may be more appropriate, so the
--   application would have to look for <tt>date</tt> tags in the
--   <a>versionTags</a> field and compare those. The bottom line is, don't
--   always assume that <a>compare</a> and other <a>Ord</a> operations are
--   the right thing for every <a>Version</a>.
--   
--   Similarly, concrete representations of versions may differ. One
--   possible concrete representation is provided (see <a>showVersion</a>
--   and <a>parseVersion</a>), but depending on the application a different
--   concrete representation may be more appropriate.
data Version

-- | Smart text constructor for versions.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let ver = [version|0.0.0-alpha|]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This will create a version with branch [0, 0, 0] and tags ["alpha"].
version :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Parse a version from a Text.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Version.parse "0.0.0-alpha"
--   </pre>
--   
--   Just (Version [0, 0, 0] ["alpha"])
parse :: Text -> Maybe Version
toText :: Version -> Text
instance Language.Haskell.TH.Syntax.Lift Data.Version.Version


-- | # Core
--   
--   This module is automatically imported in all the NeoHaskell files,
module Core

-- | Smart text constructor to make a path from a text literal
path :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Representation of fast immutable arrays. You can create arrays of
--   integers (<tt>Array Int</tt>) or strings (<tt>Array String</tt>) or
--   any other type of value you can dream up.
data Array a
data Bytes

-- | A channel for communication between concurrent tasks.
--   
--   Channels can be either: - **Unbounded**: Writers never block, but
--   memory can grow without limit - **Bounded**: Writers block when
--   channel is full, providing backpressure
--   
--   Use unbounded channels for low-volume or when backpressure isn't
--   needed. Use bounded channels for high-throughput scenarios to prevent
--   OOM.
--   
--   <h2>Performance Notes</h2>
--   
--   Bounded channels use STM's TBQueue for correctness guarantees. Under
--   extreme backpressure (channel consistently full with many concurrent
--   writers), STM transaction retries can cause throughput degradation
--   ("STM thrash").
--   
--   For most workloads (&lt; 50k events/second), this is not a concern. If
--   you observe throughput collapse under sustained backpressure,
--   consider:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Increasing channel capacity to reduce full-channel frequency</li>
--   <li>Adding more workers (horizontal scaling)</li>
--   <li>Investigating why consumers are slower than producers</li>
--   </ol>
data Channel value

-- | The character type <a>Char</a> represents Unicode codespace and its
--   elements are code points as in definitions <a>D9 and D10 of the
--   Unicode Standard</a>.
--   
--   Character literals in Haskell are single-quoted: <tt>'Q'</tt>,
--   <tt>'Я'</tt> or <tt>'Ω'</tt>. To represent a single quote itself use
--   <tt>'\''</tt>, and to represent a backslash use <tt>'\\'</tt>. The
--   full grammar can be found in the section 2.6 of the <a>Haskell 2010
--   Language Report</a>.
--   
--   To specify a character by its code point one can use decimal,
--   hexadecimal or octal notation: <tt>'\65'</tt>, <tt>'\x41'</tt> and
--   <tt>'\o101'</tt> are all alternative forms of <tt>'A'</tt>. The
--   largest code point is <tt>'\x10ffff'</tt>.
--   
--   There is a special escape syntax for ASCII control characters:
--   
--   TODO: table
--   
--   <a>Data.Char</a> provides utilities to work with <a>Char</a>.
data Char

-- | The Command typeclass defines the contract for all commands.
--   
--   Commands represent user intent to change the system. Each command must
--   provide:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>getEntityIdImpl</a>: Extract the entity ID from the command
--   (Nothing for creation)</li>
--   <li><a>decideImpl</a>: Pure decision logic that produces events or
--   rejects</li>
--   </ul>
class Command command where {
    
    -- | Whether this command supports multi-tenancy.
    --   
    --   When True, <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> and <a>decideImpl</a> receive a
    --   tenant UUID as first argument.
    type IsMultiTenant command :: Bool;
    type IsMultiTenant command = 'False;
}

-- | Extract the entity ID from a command.
--   
--   Returns Nothing for creation commands where no entity exists yet.
getEntityIdImpl :: Command command => GetEntityIdFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityIdType (EntityOf command))

-- | The decision function that determines whether to accept or reject the
--   command.
--   
--   This is pure business logic that takes the command and optional
--   current entity state, returning a Decision that either accepts with
--   events or rejects with a reason.
decideImpl :: Command command => DecideFunction (IsMultiTenant command) command (EntityOf command) (EventOf (EntityOf command))

-- | Authorization check run before <a>decideImpl</a>.
--   
--   Returns <tt>Nothing</tt> if the caller may execute this command, or
--   <tt>Just</tt> an <a>AccessError</a> explaining why not. Defaults to
--   <a>authenticatedAccess</a> so a command with no <tt>canAccess</tt>
--   definition blocks unauthenticated callers — going public is an
--   explicit opt-in.
--   
--   The name pairs with the dispatcher entry point (<tt>execute</tt>) and
--   avoids a re-export clash with <a>canAccessImpl</a>. The user still
--   writes a top-level <tt>canAccess</tt>; the TH macro wires it here.
--   
--   An invalid token is never silently accepted: the Web transport rejects
--   any AuthError other than TokenMissing before reaching this method.
canExecuteImpl :: Command command => Maybe UserClaims -> Maybe AccessError
data ConcurrentVar value
data DateTime

-- | Fixed-point decimal with 4 internal decimal places.
--   
--   Internal representation: value * 10000
--   
--   <pre>
--   Decimal 125000  -- represents 12.5000
--   Decimal 1       -- represents 0.0001
--   Decimal (-52500) -- represents -5.2500
--   </pre>
data Decimal
data DurableChannel value

-- | A value of type <tt><a>IO</a> a</tt> is a computation which, when
--   performed, does some I/O before returning a value of type <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   There is really only one way to "perform" an I/O action: bind it to
--   <tt>Main.main</tt> in your program. When your program is run, the I/O
--   will be performed. It isn't possible to perform I/O from an arbitrary
--   function, unless that function is itself in the <a>IO</a> monad and
--   called at some point, directly or indirectly, from <tt>Main.main</tt>.
--   
--   <a>IO</a> is a monad, so <a>IO</a> actions can be combined using
--   either the do-notation or the <a>&gt;&gt;</a> and <a>&gt;&gt;=</a>
--   operations from the <a>Monad</a> class.
data IO a

-- | In Haskell a list type is defined using square brackets. This alias
--   allows us to alternatively write the type like we would in Elm.
type LinkedList a = [a]
data Lock

-- | A Map from keys <tt>k</tt> to values <tt>a</tt>.
--   
--   The <a>Semigroup</a> operation for <a>Map</a> is <a>union</a>, which
--   prefers values from the left operand. If <tt>m1</tt> maps a key
--   <tt>k</tt> to a value <tt>a1</tt>, and <tt>m2</tt> maps the same key
--   to a different value <tt>a2</tt>, then their union <tt>m1 &lt;&gt;
--   m2</tt> maps <tt>k</tt> to <tt>a1</tt>.
data Map k a

-- | The <a>Maybe</a> type encapsulates an optional value. A value of type
--   <tt><a>Maybe</a> a</tt> either contains a value of type <tt>a</tt>
--   (represented as <tt><a>Just</a> a</tt>), or it is empty (represented
--   as <a>Nothing</a>). Using <a>Maybe</a> is a good way to deal with
--   errors or exceptional cases without resorting to drastic measures such
--   as <a>error</a>.
--   
--   The <a>Maybe</a> type is also a monad. It is a simple kind of error
--   monad, where all errors are represented by <a>Nothing</a>. A richer
--   error monad can be built using the <a>Either</a> type.
data Maybe a
Nothing :: Maybe a
Just :: a -> Maybe a
data Path
type Record (r :: Row Type) = Record I r

-- | A value that shows as <tt>&lt;redacted&gt;</tt> in Show output.
--   
--   Two constructors:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Redacted</a> value: Shows as <tt>&lt;redacted&gt;</tt> — most
--   common case</li>
--   <li><a>RedactedLabeled</a> label value: Shows as <tt>&lt;redacted:
--   label&gt;</tt> — for debugging complex structures</li>
--   </ul>
data Redacted value

-- | A <tt>Result</tt> is either <tt>Ok</tt> meaning the computation
--   succeeded, or it is an <tt>Err</tt> meaning that there was some
--   failure.
data Result error value
Ok :: value -> Result error value
Err :: error -> Result error value

-- | Library-agnostic schema representation. Can be converted to OpenAPI,
--   JSON Schema, CLI args, etc.
data Schema

-- | Unit, ()
SNull :: Schema

-- | Bool
SBool :: Schema

-- | Int, Integer
SInt :: Schema

-- | Double, Float
SNumber :: Schema

-- | Text, String
SText :: Schema

-- | Array a, [a]
SArray :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Maybe a (marks field as non-required)
SOptional :: Schema -> Schema

-- | Record types
SObject :: Array FieldSchema -> Schema

-- | Sum types without fields (data Status = Active | Inactive)
SEnum :: Array Text -> Schema

-- | Sum types with fields (data Shape = Circle {radius :: Int} | ...)
SUnion :: Array (Text, Schema) -> Schema

-- | Reference for recursive types
SRef :: Text -> Schema
data Service (commandRow :: Row Type) (commandTransportNames :: [Symbol])
data Task err value

-- | A <tt>Text</tt> is a chunk of text:
--   
--   <pre>
--   "Hello!"
--   "How are you?"
--   "🙈🙉🙊"
--   
--   -- strings with escape characters
--   "this\n\t\"that\""
--   "\x1F648\x1F649\x1F64A" -- "🙈🙉🙊"
--   </pre>
--   
--   A <tt>Text</tt> can represent any sequence of <a>unicode
--   characters</a>. You can use the unicode escapes from <tt>x0000</tt> to
--   <tt>x10FFFF</tt> to represent characters by their code point. You can
--   also include the unicode characters directly. Using the escapes can be
--   better if you need one of the many whitespace characters with
--   different widths.
type Text = Text

-- | A trigger is a way to listen to a process that's running in the
--   background and generates events. When you tell NeoHaskell to subscribe
--   to a process, NeoHaskell will pass a callback function that posts an
--   event to the event queue whenever the process generates an event. The
--   callback function is called a <tt>dispatch</tt> function.
--   
--   An example Trigger is <a>every</a>, which generates an event every
--   specified milliseconds. When you subscribe to <a>every</a>, you pass a
--   callback function that posts an event to the event queue whenever the
--   process ticks.
data Trigger event

-- | The <a>Unknown</a> type represents a dynamically typed value.
data Unknown
data Uuid
data Var value

-- | A <a>Version</a> represents the version of a software entity.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Eq</a> is provided, which implements exact equality
--   modulo reordering of the tags in the <a>versionTags</a> field.
--   
--   An instance of <a>Ord</a> is also provided, which gives lexicographic
--   ordering on the <a>versionBranch</a> fields (i.e. 2.1 &gt; 2.0, 1.2.3
--   &gt; 1.2.2, etc.). This is expected to be sufficient for many uses,
--   but note that you may need to use a more specific ordering for your
--   versioning scheme. For example, some versioning schemes may include
--   pre-releases which have tags <tt>"pre1"</tt>, <tt>"pre2"</tt>, and so
--   on, and these would need to be taken into account when determining
--   ordering. In some cases, date ordering may be more appropriate, so the
--   application would have to look for <tt>date</tt> tags in the
--   <a>versionTags</a> field and compare those. The bottom line is, don't
--   always assume that <a>compare</a> and other <a>Ord</a> operations are
--   the right thing for every <a>Version</a>.
--   
--   Similarly, concrete representations of versions may differ. One
--   possible concrete representation is provided (see <a>showVersion</a>
--   and <a>parseVersion</a>), but depending on the application a different
--   concrete representation may be more appropriate.
data Version

-- | Put two appendable things together. This includes arrays, lists, and
--   text.
--   
--   <pre>
--   "hello" ++ "world" == "helloworld"
--   [1,1,2] ++ [3,5,8] == [1,1,2,3,5,8]
--   </pre>
(++) :: Appendable appendable => appendable -> appendable -> appendable
infixr 5 ++

-- | The class <a>Typeable</a> allows a concrete representation of a type
--   to be calculated.
class Typeable (a :: k)

-- | Smart text constructor for versions.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let ver = [version|0.0.0-alpha|]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This will create a version with branch [0, 0, 0] and tags ["alpha"].
version :: QuasiQuoter

-- | Schema for a single field in a record type.
data FieldSchema
FieldSchema :: Text -> Schema -> Bool -> Text -> FieldSchema
[fieldName] :: FieldSchema -> Text
[fieldSchema] :: FieldSchema -> Schema
[fieldRequired] :: FieldSchema -> Bool
[fieldDescription] :: FieldSchema -> Text

-- | The result of running a Decision.
--   
--   Either the command is accepted with events to persist, or rejected
--   with a reason.
data CommandResult event
AcceptCommand :: InsertionType -> Array event -> CommandResult event
RejectCommand :: Text -> CommandResult event

-- | The Decision monad for expressing command acceptance/rejection logic.
--   
--   Decision is a free monad that allows you to:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Generate UUIDs via <a>generateUuid</a></li>
--   <li>Accept commands with events via <a>acceptNew</a>,
--   <a>acceptExisting</a>, <a>acceptAny</a>, <a>acceptAfter</a></li>
--   <li>Reject commands with a reason via <a>reject</a></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Decisions are pure and can be easily tested without side effects.
data Decision a
class KnownHash (s :: Symbol)
hashVal :: KnownHash s => proxy s -> Int

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to a Schema.
--   
--   Types with Generic instances get ToSchema for free using the default
--   implementation.
class ToSchema value
toSchema :: ToSchema value => Schema
($dmtoSchema) :: (ToSchema value, Generic value, GToSchema (Rep value)) => Schema

-- | Whether this command supports multi-tenancy.
--   
--   When True, <a>getEntityIdImpl</a> and <a>decideImpl</a> receive a
--   tenant UUID as first argument.
type family IsMultiTenant command :: Bool

-- | Maps a type to its symbolic name.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance NameOf CreateCart = <a>CreateCart</a>
--   </pre>
type family NameOf t :: Symbol

-- | The Entity typeclass represents domain objects that can be
--   reconstructed from events.
--   
--   Entities are the "left fold" of their event history - starting from an
--   initial state and applying each event in sequence via
--   <a>updateImpl</a>.
class Entity entity where {
    
    -- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
    --   
    --   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
    --   different ID types.
    type EntityIdType entity;
    type EntityIdType entity = Uuid;
}

-- | The initial state of the entity before any events are applied.
--   
--   This is used as the starting point when reconstructing an entity from
--   its event stream.
initialStateImpl :: Entity entity => entity

-- | Apply an event to the entity state, producing a new state.
--   
--   This is the reducer function used to reconstruct entity state from
--   events. It should be a pure function with no side effects.
updateImpl :: Entity entity => EventOf entity -> entity -> entity

-- | Maps a command type to its corresponding entity type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EntityOf CreateCart = CartEntity
--   </pre>
type family EntityOf command

-- | Maps an entity type to its corresponding event type.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   type instance EventOf CartEntity = CartEvent
--   </pre>
type family EventOf entityType
data InsertionType
StreamCreation :: InsertionType
InsertAfter :: StreamPosition -> InsertionType
ExistingStream :: InsertionType
AnyStreamState :: InsertionType
data StreamId

-- | Typeclass for types that can be converted to StreamId.
--   
--   Note: These conversions are considered "safe" because: - StreamId →
--   StreamId: Already validated - Uuid → StreamId: UUIDs are fixed-length
--   (36 chars), always valid
--   
--   For Text, use <a>fromText</a> which validates length.
class ToStreamId idType
toStreamId :: ToStreamId idType => idType -> StreamId

-- | The type of identifier used for this entity.
--   
--   Defaults to <a>Uuid</a> but can be overridden for entities that use
--   different ID types.
type family EntityIdType entity

module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.Context

Context :: QueryObjectStore TestQuery -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Context
[store] :: Context -> QueryObjectStore TestQuery
[testId1] :: Context -> Uuid
[testId2] :: Context -> Uuid
[testId3] :: Context -> Uuid
initialize :: Task Text (QueryObjectStore TestQuery) -> Task Text Context

module Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core

-- | Example domain events for a shopping cart Note: The Event instance is
--   defined in Test.Service.Command.Core where EntityOf CartEvent =
--   CartEntity is also defined
data CartEvent
CartCreated :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
ItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: CartEvent -> Int
ItemRemoved :: Uuid -> Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
CartCheckedOut :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid

-- | Example entity state for a shopping cart
data CartState
CartState :: Maybe Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> Int -> CartState
[cartId] :: CartState -> Maybe Uuid
[cartItems] :: CartState -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[isCheckedOut] :: CartState -> Bool
[version] :: CartState -> Int

-- | Initial state for a new cart
initialState :: CartState

-- | Apply a cart event to the current state
applyEvent :: CartEvent -> CartState -> CartState

-- | Create a new entity fetcher for carts
newFetcher :: EventStore CartEvent -> Task Error (EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent)

-- | Create a new entity fetcher for carts with cache support
newFetcherWithCache :: EventStore CartEvent -> SnapshotCache CartState -> Task Error (EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Show.Show Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState

initialize :: Task Text (SnapshotCache CartState) -> Task Text Context

module Test.Service.Command.Core
data AddItemToCart
AddItemToCart :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> AddItemToCart
[cartId] :: AddItemToCart -> Uuid
[itemId] :: AddItemToCart -> Uuid
[amount] :: AddItemToCart -> Int
data RemoveItemFromCart
RemoveItemFromCart :: Uuid -> Uuid -> RemoveItemFromCart
[cartId] :: RemoveItemFromCart -> Uuid
[itemId] :: RemoveItemFromCart -> Uuid
data CheckoutCart
CheckoutCart :: Uuid -> CheckoutCart
[cartId] :: CheckoutCart -> Uuid

-- | Command that requires authentication. Rejects if ctx.user is Nothing.
data AuthenticatedAddItem
AuthenticatedAddItem :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> AuthenticatedAddItem
[cartId] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Uuid
[itemId] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Uuid
[amount] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Int

-- | Command that checks ownership. Rejects if ctx.user.sub doesn't match
--   entity.ownerId.
data OwnedCartCheckout
OwnedCartCheckout :: Uuid -> OwnedCartCheckout
[cartId] :: OwnedCartCheckout -> Uuid
data CartEntity
CartEntity :: Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> CartEntity
[cartId] :: CartEntity -> Uuid
[cartItems] :: CartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: CartEntity -> Bool

-- | Cart entity with owner tracking for authorization tests.
data OwnedCartEntity
OwnedCartEntity :: Uuid -> Text -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> OwnedCartEntity
[cartId] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Uuid
[ownerId] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Text
[cartItems] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Bool
initialCartState :: CartEntity
initialOwnedCartState :: OwnedCartEntity
applyCartEvent :: CartEvent -> CartEntity -> CartEntity
applyOwnedCartEvent :: OwnedCartEvent -> OwnedCartEntity -> OwnedCartEntity

-- | Events for owned cart.
data OwnedCartEvent
OwnedCartCreated :: Uuid -> Text -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[ownerId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Text
OwnedItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Int
OwnedCartCheckedOut :: Uuid -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
data TenantAddItem
TenantAddItem :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> TenantAddItem
[cartId] :: TenantAddItem -> Uuid
[itemId] :: TenantAddItem -> Uuid
[amount] :: TenantAddItem -> Int
data TenantCreateCart
TenantCreateCart :: TenantCreateCart
data TenantCartEntity
TenantCartEntity :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> TenantCartEntity
[cartId] :: TenantCartEntity -> Uuid
[tenantId] :: TenantCartEntity -> Uuid
[cartItems] :: TenantCartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: TenantCartEntity -> Bool
data TenantCartEvent
TenantCartCreated :: Uuid -> Uuid -> TenantCartEvent
[entityId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[tenantId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
TenantItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> TenantCartEvent
[entityId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: TenantCartEvent -> Int
initialTenantCartState :: TenantCartEntity
applyTenantCartEvent :: TenantCartEvent -> TenantCartEntity -> TenantCartEntity

module Test.Service.CommandHandler.Execute.Context
data Context
Context :: EventStore CartEvent -> EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent -> StreamId -> EntityName -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Context
[cartStore] :: Context -> EventStore CartEvent
[cartFetcher] :: Context -> EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent
[cartStreamId] :: Context -> StreamId
[cartEntityName] :: Context -> EntityName
[cartId] :: Context -> Uuid
[itemId1] :: Context -> Uuid
[itemId2] :: Context -> Uuid
initialize :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Task Text Context

module Test.Spec
type Spec a = SpecWith a

-- | Describe a group of tests
describe :: Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Define a test case
it :: HasCallStack => Text -> (context -> Task Text Unit) -> Spec context

-- | Define a focused test case (runs only this test)
only_it :: HasCallStack => Text -> (context -> Task Text Unit) -> Spec context

-- | Assert that two values are equal
shouldBe :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that two values are not equal
shouldNotBe :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value satisfies a predicate
shouldSatisfy :: (HasCallStack, Show a) => (a -> Bool) -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an array contains another array
shouldContain :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an array does not contain another array
shouldNotContain :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a text starts with a prefix
shouldStartWith :: HasCallStack => Text -> Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a string ends with a suffix
shouldEndWith :: HasCallStack => Text -> Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that two lists contain the same elements, regardless of order
shouldMatchList :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a task returns a specific value
shouldReturn :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a, Show err) => a -> Task err a -> Task err2 Unit

-- | Assert that an array is in increasing order
shouldHaveIncreasingOrder :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Marks a test as pending
pending :: Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Mark a group of tests as pending
xdescribe :: Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Run tests in parallel
parallel :: Spec -> Spec

-- | Run tests sequentially
sequential :: Spec -> Spec

-- | Run a Task in a test
runTask :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err a -> IO a

-- | Fail the test with a message
fail :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Run a Task before the tests
beforeAll :: (HasCallStack, Show err, AutoCleanup context) => Task err context -> Spec context -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task before each test
before :: (HasCallStack, Show err, AutoCleanup context) => Task err context -> Spec context -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task after each test
after :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err Unit -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task once after all tests in a group
afterAll :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err Unit -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Runs a block of tests if an environment variable is set
whenEnvVar :: HasCallStack => Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Assert that an array is in decreasing order
shouldHaveDecreasingOrder :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is less than or equal to a maximum value
shouldBeLessThanOrEqual :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is greater than or equal to a minimum value
shouldBeGreaterThanOrEqual :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is less than a maximum value
shouldBeLessThan :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is greater than a minimum value
shouldBeGreaterThan :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Modifier that allows asserting the contents of a Var
varContents :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => (a -> a -> Task Text Unit) -> a -> Var a -> Task Text Unit
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.Command.Decide.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.IndividualStreamOrdering.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.ReadAllBackwardsFromEnd.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.ReadAllForwardsFromStart.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.CommandHandler.Execute.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EntityFetcher.FetchWithCache.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Fetch.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.BatchValidation.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.Subscriptions.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.StreamTruncation.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.OptimisticConcurrency.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.GlobalStreamOrdering.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup (Service.EventStore.Core.EventStore eventType)

module Test.Service.CommandHandler.Core

module Test.CompileTime

-- | Assert that an expression should not typecheck. This is useful for
--   testing type-level constraints and validation. Note: Requires {-#
--   OPTIONS_GHC -fdefer-type-errors #-} in test files.
--   
--   Example: <tt> it "should not allow adding Int to String" _ -&gt; do
--   shouldNotTypecheck (5 + "hello") </tt>
--   
--   Example with type families: <tt> it "should reject invalid type family
--   application" _ -&gt; do shouldNotTypecheck (undefined :: MyTypeFamily
--   Int String) </tt>
shouldNotTypecheck :: (HasCallStack, NFData value) => (() ~ () => value) -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an expression should typecheck. This is the opposite of
--   shouldNotTypecheck - it verifies that valid code compiles. Note: This
--   is mainly for documentation - if code typechecks, it compiles
--   normally.
--   
--   Example: <tt> it "should allow adding two Ints" _ -&gt; do
--   shouldTypecheck (5 + 3) </tt>
--   
--   Example with type families: <tt> it "should accept valid type family
--   application" _ -&gt; do shouldTypecheck (undefined :: MyTypeFamily Int
--   Int) </tt>
shouldTypecheck :: NFData value => value -> Task Text Unit

-- | A class of types that can be fully evaluated.
class NFData a

-- | <a>rnf</a> should reduce its argument to normal form (that is, fully
--   evaluate all sub-components), and then return <tt>()</tt>.
--   
--   <h3><a>Generic</a> <a>NFData</a> deriving</h3>
--   
--   Starting with GHC 7.2, you can automatically derive instances for
--   types possessing a <a>Generic</a> instance.
--   
--   Note: <a>Generic1</a> can be auto-derived starting with GHC 7.4
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric #-}
--   
--   import GHC.Generics (Generic, Generic1)
--   import Control.DeepSeq
--   
--   data Foo a = Foo a String
--                deriving (Eq, Generic, Generic1)
--   
--   instance NFData a =&gt; NFData (Foo a)
--   instance NFData1 Foo
--   
--   data Colour = Red | Green | Blue
--                 deriving Generic
--   
--   instance NFData Colour
--   </pre>
--   
--   Starting with GHC 7.10, the example above can be written more
--   concisely by enabling the new <tt>DeriveAnyClass</tt> extension:
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric, DeriveAnyClass #-}
--   
--   import GHC.Generics (Generic)
--   import Control.DeepSeq
--   
--   data Foo a = Foo a String
--                deriving (Eq, Generic, Generic1, NFData, NFData1)
--   
--   data Colour = Red | Green | Blue
--                 deriving (Generic, NFData)
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h3>Compatibility with previous <tt>deepseq</tt> versions</h3>
--   
--   Prior to version 1.4.0.0, the default implementation of the <a>rnf</a>
--   method was defined as
--   
--   <pre>
--   <a>rnf</a> a = <a>seq</a> a ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   However, starting with <tt>deepseq-1.4.0.0</tt>, the default
--   implementation is based on <tt>DefaultSignatures</tt> allowing for
--   more accurate auto-derived <a>NFData</a> instances. If you need the
--   previously used exact default <a>rnf</a> method implementation
--   semantics, use
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf x = seq x ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   or alternatively
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf = rwhnf
--   </pre>
--   
--   or
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE BangPatterns #-}
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf !_ = ()
--   </pre>
rnf :: NFData a => a -> ()
($dmrnf) :: (NFData a, Generic a, GNFData Zero (Rep a)) => a -> ()

module Test.AppSpec.Scenario
data Scenario appModel
Scenario :: Text -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit -> Scenario appModel
[name] :: Scenario appModel -> Text
[steps] :: Scenario appModel -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
data ScenarioSteps appModel result
ScenarioSteps :: ScenarioSteps appModel result
empty :: ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
instance GHC.Base.Applicative (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Base.Functor (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Base.Monad (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)

module Test.AppSpec.AppSpec
data AppSpec appModel
AppSpec :: Array (Scenario appModel) -> AppSpec appModel
[scenarios] :: AppSpec appModel -> Array (Scenario appModel)
data AppSpecSteps appModel result
AppSpecSteps :: Array (Scenario appModel) -> result -> AppSpecSteps appModel result
[stepScenarios] :: AppSpecSteps appModel result -> Array (Scenario appModel)
[result] :: AppSpecSteps appModel result -> result
fromSteps :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpec appModel
emptySteps :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit
instance GHC.Base.Applicative (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq result => GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Base.Functor (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Base.Monad (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord result => GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Show.Show result => GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)

module Test.AppSpec.Verify
run :: Ops appModel -> AppSpec appModel -> Task Text Unit
data Ops appModel
Ops :: (Scenario appModel -> Task Text Unit) -> Ops appModel
[verifyScenario] :: Ops appModel -> Scenario appModel -> Task Text Unit
defaultOps :: Ops appModel

module Test.AppSpec
data AppSpec appModel
specificationFor :: appModel -> AppSpecSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpec appModel
data Scenario appModel
scenario :: Text -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpecSteps appModel Unit
given :: commandPattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
expect :: outcomePattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
and :: outcomePattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
receivedCommand :: adapter
registeredEvent :: adapter
executedTask :: adapter
verifyAppSpec :: AppSpec appModel -> Task Text Unit
emptyScenario :: ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
noScenarios :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit
