module Test.Service.Command
spec :: Spec Unit


-- | Shared TH primitives for concept-marker boilerplate emission.
--   
--   Internal to nhcore. Used by Service.CommandExecutor.TH,
--   Service.Query.TH, and Service.Event.TH to emit the JSON and
--   <tt>deriving stock</tt> instances every command, event, and query type
--   needs, while remaining a no-op when an instance is already in scope
--   (so consumers can declare custom encodings before the marker call).
module Service.TH.Boilerplate

-- | Emit a class instance only when it is not already in scope.
--   
--   Checks via <a>reifyInstances</a> whether <tt>className @typeName</tt>
--   exists. Returns the <tt>fallbackDecl</tt> when the instance is absent,
--   or an empty list when it is already present.
emitInstanceIfMissing :: Name -> Name -> DecsQ -> DecsQ

-- | Emit a <tt>deriving stock instance Class Type</tt> declaration when
--   the class instance is not already in scope. Used for <a>Show</a> and
--   <a>Generic</a>.
emitStockDeriving :: Name -> Name -> DecsQ

-- | Emit an empty-body <tt>instance Class Type</tt> declaration when the
--   class instance is not already in scope. Used for <a>FromJSON</a>,
--   <a>ToJSON</a>, and <a>ToSchema</a> — each defaults its methods via
--   <a>Generic</a>.
emitEmptyInstance :: Name -> Name -> DecsQ

-- | Emit the four-class boilerplate every concept marker shares:
--   <a>Show</a>, <a>Generic</a>, <a>FromJSON</a>, <a>ToJSON</a>. Each is
--   emitted only when not already in scope.
--   
--   <a>deriveQuery</a> calls <a>emitEmptyInstance</a> separately for
--   <a>ToSchema</a> on top of this four-pack.
emitJsonAndDerivingBoilerplate :: Name -> DecsQ


-- | Template Haskell marker for event types.
--   
--   The <a>event</a> marker emits Show, Generic, Json.FromJSON, and
--   Json.ToJSON instances for the named type, skipping any that are
--   already in scope.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   data CartCreated = CartCreated { cartId :: Uuid }
--   
--   event ''CartCreated
--   </pre>
--   
--   CONVENTION — "marker last": Any custom instance you wish to write
--   (e.g. a hand-crafted <a>ToJSON</a>) must be declared <i>before</i> the
--   <tt>event</tt> call. Declaring it after produces a GHC "Duplicate
--   instance" error because the marker will have already emitted the
--   derived version.
module Service.Event.TH

-- | Derive Show, Generic, Json.FromJSON, and Json.ToJSON for an event
--   type.
--   
--   Each class is emitted only when it is not already in scope
--   (idempotent).
event :: Name -> DecsQ

module Service.Query.TH

-- | Derive Query-related instances for a query type.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Define auth functions in your query module
--   canAccess :: Maybe UserClaims -&gt; Maybe AccessError
--   canAccess = authenticatedAccess
--   
--   canView :: Maybe UserClaims -&gt; CartSummary -&gt; Maybe AccessError
--   canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId)
--   
--   deriveQuery ''CartSummary [''CartEntity]
--   </pre>
--   
--   Generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><pre>type instance NameOf CartSummary =
--   <a>CartSummary</a></pre></li>
--   <li><pre>type instance EntitiesOf CartSummary =
--   '[CartEntity]</pre></li>
--   <li><pre>instance Query CartSummary where canAccessImpl = canAccess;
--   canViewImpl = canView</pre></li>
--   <li><pre>instance KnownHash <a>CartSummary</a></pre></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Note: You must define <tt>canAccess</tt> and <tt>canView</tt>
--   functions in your module. You must also manually implement
--   <a>QueryOf</a> instances for each entity-query relationship, as these
--   contain business logic.
deriveQuery :: Name -> [Name] -> DecsQ

module Service.Infra.Postgres.ConnectionConfig

-- | Optional libpq TLS negotiation mode (WI-5 / ADR-0064).
--   <a>SslModeUnset</a> (the <tt>Default</tt> the pool configs pass) emits
--   no sslmode param, so libpq keeps its compiled-in <tt>prefer</tt>
--   default and localhost/dev is unchanged. Server-auth only: there is
--   deliberately no client-cert (sslcert/sslkey) field, because Azure
--   Flexible Server has no mTLS and supplying them breaks the connection
--   (ADR-0064 §3).
data SslMode

-- | no sslmode param; libpq default <tt>prefer</tt> (the default)
SslModeUnset :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=disable
SslModeDisable :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=allow
SslModeAllow :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=prefer (explicit)
SslModePrefer :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=require — forbids silent downgrade
SslModeRequire :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=verify-ca
SslModeVerifyCa :: SslMode

-- | sslmode=verify-full — forbids downgrade + MITM
SslModeVerifyFull :: SslMode

-- | Internal connection inputs shared by all three Postgres pools. Derives
--   (Eq, Ord) only -- Show is dropped per sec-04-001 so the password field
--   can never be rendered by an accidental show<i>trace</i>exception.
data ConnectionParams
ConnectionParams :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Int -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> ConnectionParams
[host] :: ConnectionParams -> Text
[databaseName] :: ConnectionParams -> Text
[user] :: ConnectionParams -> Text
[password] :: ConnectionParams -> Text
[port] :: ConnectionParams -> Int
[sslMode] :: ConnectionParams -> SslMode
[sslRootCert] :: ConnectionParams -> Maybe Text

-- | The fully resolved, INSPECTABLE libpq parameter set built from a
--   <a>ConnectionParams</a>. Every value that <a>toConnectionParams</a>
--   hands to libpq is captured here as plain data so tests can assert each
--   one exactly -- including the four ADR-0037 keepalive entries, guarding
--   against a dropped or mis-mapped field. <a>port</a> is the validated
--   <a>Word16</a> (1..65535).
--   
--   Derives (Eq) only -- Show is dropped for the same password-hygiene
--   reason as <a>ConnectionParams</a> (sec-04-001).
data ResolvedParams
ResolvedParams :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Word16 -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> ResolvedParams
[host] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[databaseName] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[user] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[password] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[port] :: ResolvedParams -> Word16
[keepalives] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[keepalivesIdle] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[keepalivesInterval] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[keepalivesCount] :: ResolvedParams -> Text
[sslMode] :: ResolvedParams -> SslMode
[sslRootCert] :: ResolvedParams -> Maybe Text

-- | Validate a TCP port. <a>port</a> wants a <a>Word16</a>, so a raw
--   <a>fromIntegral</a> would silently wrap an out-of-range <a>Int</a> (-1
--   -&gt; 65535, 65536 -&gt; 0). Fail fast with a clear typed error
--   instead, mirroring the fail-fast <tt>poolSize &gt; 0</tt> validation
--   WI-1 added to the pool-acquire paths.
validatePort :: Int -> Result Text Word16

-- | Map an <a>SslMode</a> to its libpq sslmode token (WI-5 / ADR-0064).
--   Total over the closed enum. <a>SslModeUnset</a> has NO token — returns
--   <a>Nothing</a>, because the unset mode emits no sslmode param at all
--   (ADR-0064 §2). Every other constructor maps to its exact libpq token.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; sslModeToText SslModeRequire
--   Just "require"
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; sslModeToText SslModeVerifyFull
--   Just "verify-full"
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; sslModeToText SslModeUnset
--   Nothing
--   </pre>
sslModeToText :: SslMode -> Maybe Text

-- | Parse an operator-supplied env token into an <a>SslMode</a> (WI-5 /
--   ADR-0064). The empty string and "unset" both map to
--   <a>SslModeUnset</a> (default-off). Every libpq token maps to its
--   constructor; anything else is one clean <a>Result</a> error naming the
--   bad value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; textToSslMode "require"
--   Ok SslModeRequire
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; textToSslMode "unset"
--   Ok SslModeUnset
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; textToSslMode ""
--   Ok SslModeUnset
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; textToSslMode "requre"
--   Err "unknown DB_SSL_MODE \"requre\"; expected one of: unset, disable, allow, prefer, require, verify-ca, verify-full"
--   </pre>
textToSslMode :: Text -> Result Text SslMode

-- | The single validation point for connection inputs (ADR-0062 section
--   2): resolve a <a>ConnectionParams</a> into the inspectable
--   <a>ResolvedParams</a>, validating the port along the way. All three
--   pools route through here (via <a>toConnectionParams</a>), so all three
--   get fail-fast port validation.
resolveParams :: ConnectionParams -> Result Text ResolvedParams

-- | The complete, ORDERED libpq (key, value) parameter set — base params
--   first, then any conditional TLS params — that the single connection
--   <a>Setting</a> is built from (ADR-0062 section 2). Carries the four
--   ADR-0037 keepalives for every pool and fails fast on an out-of-range
--   port (see <a>validatePort</a>).
--   
--   Exposed as plain inspectable data because the hasql
--   <tt>Param</tt>/<a>Setting</a> types are opaque (no Eq/Show) and the
--   modules that render them to a connection string are internal to hasql,
--   so this is the ONLY place a test can assert the exact params —
--   including that <a>host</a> survives a set sslMode (the #694
--   regression) and that no sslcert/sslkey ever leaks in (ADR-0064 §3).
toParamPairs :: ConnectionParams -> Result Text (LinkedList (Text, Text))

-- | The single place libpq connection params are constructed (ADR-0062
--   section 2). Returns a <a>Result</a> so the three pools surface the
--   typed error in their own error channel.
--   
--   Every param — base AND the conditional TLS params — goes into ONE
--   <a>params</a>, i.e. a SINGLE <a>connection</a> (#694). hasql's
--   <a>staticConnectionSettings</a> applies each <a>connection</a> by
--   REPLACING the whole connection string (last-wins, not merge), so
--   emitting the TLS params as a second connection Setting silently
--   dropped host<i>port</i>dbname and libpq fell back to the local Unix
--   socket. Returning a one-element list keeps the 'LinkedList Setting'
--   shape every pool already threads through <a>toPoolConfig</a>.
toConnectionParams :: ConnectionParams -> Result Text (LinkedList Setting)

-- | The single place <a>Config</a> is constructed (ADR-0062 section 3).
--   Parameterised by <tt>poolSize</tt> so each pool keeps its ADR-0060
--   size.
toPoolConfig :: Int -> LinkedList Setting -> Config

-- | Log connection pool lifecycle events for observability. Only logs
--   termination events to avoid overhead under high load. See ADR-0027 for
--   rationale.
logPoolObservation :: Observation -> IO ()
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Infra.Postgres.ConnectionConfig.ConnectionParams
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Infra.Postgres.ConnectionConfig.ResolvedParams
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.Infra.Postgres.ConnectionConfig.ConnectionParams

module Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres

-- | Configuration for the Postgres-backed QueryObjectStore.
data PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig
PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Int -> Int -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig
[host] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Text
[databaseName] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Text
[user] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Text
[password] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Text
[port] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Int

-- | Connection-pool size for the QueryObjectStore pool. Defaults to 4,
--   sized for Azure Flexible Server B1ms (~35 usable connections); see
--   ADR-0060 for the aggregate budget. Raise per deployment tier via this
--   field.
[poolSize] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Int
[sslMode] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> SslMode
[sslRootCert] :: PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Maybe Text

-- | Errors produced by the Postgres-backed QueryObjectStore.
data QueryObjectStoreError

-- | Unable to acquire Postgres connection.
ConnectionFailed :: Text -> QueryObjectStoreError

-- | Hasql statement execution failed.
StatementFailed :: Text -> QueryObjectStoreError

-- | Hasql result decoder failed.
DecodingFailed :: Text -> QueryObjectStoreError

-- | Operations for hash-aware checkpoint management.
--   
--   This is separate from the QueryObjectStore trait (which handles
--   generic per-instance state). Checkpoint operations carry the
--   query_name and query_hash explicitly, which the trait API does not.
--   
--   Created by createCheckpointStore from a live Pool; tests may supply a
--   mock CheckpointStore without a real Postgres connection.
data CheckpointStore
CheckpointStore :: (Text -> Text -> Task QueryObjectStoreError (Maybe Int64)) -> (Text -> Text -> Task QueryObjectStoreError Unit) -> (Text -> Text -> Int64 -> Task QueryObjectStoreError Unit) -> CheckpointStore

-- | Fetch the minimum persisted position for a query whose hash matches.
--   
--   Returns Nothing when no rows for (queryName, queryHash) exist — i.e.,
--   the query has never been persisted or the hash has changed.
[resumeFromCheckpoint] :: CheckpointStore -> Text -> Text -> Task QueryObjectStoreError (Maybe Int64)

-- | Delete all rows for a query whose hash does NOT match queryHash.
--   
--   Called before a full replay when a schema change is detected.
[deleteStaleHash] :: CheckpointStore -> Text -> Text -> Task QueryObjectStoreError Unit

-- | Persist (query_name, query_hash, position) for checkpoint resume.
--   
--   Uses CAS-on-position (ADR-0059 §"Concurrency &amp; persistence",
--   hazard H2): the row is only updated if the incoming position is
--   strictly greater than the stored position. A nil UUID is used as the
--   checkpoint marker row key, keeping checkpoint rows separate from
--   per-instance trait rows.
[writeCheckpoint] :: CheckpointStore -> Text -> Text -> Int64 -> Task QueryObjectStoreError Unit

-- | Create a Postgres-backed QueryObjectStore from the given config.
--   
--   Use qualified at call sites:
--   <tt>PostgresQueryObjectStore.newFromConfig cfg</tt>.
newFromConfig :: (FromJSON query, ToJSON query) => PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig -> Task QueryObjectStoreError (QueryObjectStore query)

-- | Create a CheckpointStore backed by the given Postgres Pool.
createCheckpointStore :: Pool -> CheckpointStore
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.QueryObjectStoreError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.QueryObjectStoreError
instance Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.QueryObjectStoreConfig Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.QueryObjectStore.Postgres.QueryObjectStoreError

module Service.Query.Subscriber

-- | The QuerySubscriber listens to events from the EventStore and
--   dispatches them to registered QueryUpdaters.
data QuerySubscriber
QuerySubscriber :: EventStore Value -> QueryRegistry -> ConcurrentVar (Maybe StreamPosition) -> ConcurrentVar (Maybe SubscriptionId) -> ConcurrentVar (Map Text Readiness) -> Maybe (QueryObjectStore Value) -> Maybe CheckpointStore -> QuerySubscriber
[eventStore] :: QuerySubscriber -> EventStore Value
[registry] :: QuerySubscriber -> QueryRegistry
[lastProcessedPosition] :: QuerySubscriber -> ConcurrentVar (Maybe StreamPosition)
[subscriptionId] :: QuerySubscriber -> ConcurrentVar (Maybe SubscriptionId)

-- | Per-query readiness state, keyed by queryName.
[queryReadiness] :: QuerySubscriber -> ConcurrentVar (Map Text Readiness)

-- | Optional persistent query object store for checkpoint resume. Nothing
--   = in-memory only (tests); Just store = Postgres-backed (production).
[objectStore] :: QuerySubscriber -> Maybe (QueryObjectStore Value)

-- | Optional checkpoint store for hash-aware checkpoint operations.
--   
--   Carries resumeFromCheckpoint and deleteStaleHash helpers (ADR-0059
--   §Internal helpers). Nothing = skip hash checking (in-memory / test
--   path). Just store = Postgres-backed checkpoint management.
[checkpointStore] :: QuerySubscriber -> Maybe CheckpointStore

-- | Readiness state of a query rebuild.
data Readiness
Rebuilding :: Readiness
Ready :: Readiness
Failed :: Text -> Readiness

-- | Options controlling a per-query rebuild.
data RebuildOptions
RebuildOptions :: Int -> Int -> Bool -> Bool -> RebuildOptions

-- | Events per fetch (default: 1000).
[chunkSize] :: RebuildOptions -> Int

-- | Per-query rebuild timeout in seconds (default: 300).
[timeout] :: RebuildOptions -> Int

-- | Emit a log message after each chunk (default: True).
[logProgress] :: RebuildOptions -> Bool

-- | Delete rows with mismatched query_hash before replaying (default:
--   True).
[deleteStaleHashFirst] :: RebuildOptions -> Bool

-- | Errors produced during a query rebuild.
data QueryRebuildError

-- | Rebuild took longer than the configured timeout.
RebuildTimeout :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | QueryUpdater returned Err during replay.
UpdaterException :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | Hash mismatch forced a replay, but the replay itself failed.
HashMismatchReplay :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | Could not read the resume position from the object store.
CheckpointFetchFailed :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | Could not persist the new checkpoint after a successful rebuild.
CheckpointWriteFailed :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | EventStore.readFrom returned Err.
EventStoreFailed :: Text -> QueryRebuildError

-- | Create a new QuerySubscriber.
--   
--   Pass Nothing for objectStore in tests (no checkpoint persistence).
--   Pass Just store in production to enable checkpoint resume via
--   rebuildFrom.
new :: EventStore Value -> QueryRegistry -> Task Text QuerySubscriber

-- | Create a new QuerySubscriber with a persistent QueryObjectStore.
--   
--   Use this when checkpoint persistence is needed (production wiring).
--   Enables rebuildFrom to resume from the last persisted position instead
--   of replaying from position 0 on every restart.
newWithStore :: EventStore Value -> QueryRegistry -> QueryObjectStore Value -> Task Text QuerySubscriber

-- | Create a new QuerySubscriber with a CheckpointStore for hash-aware
--   checkpoint ops.
--   
--   Use this in tests that need to exercise the hash-mismatch path (H5) or
--   in production wiring where both a QueryObjectStore and a
--   CheckpointStore are present. The checkpointStore carries
--   resumeFromCheckpoint and deleteStaleHash helpers.
newWithCheckpointStore :: EventStore Value -> QueryRegistry -> CheckpointStore -> Task Text QuerySubscriber

-- | Start live subscription for query updates. Should be called after
--   rebuildAll completes.
start :: QuerySubscriber -> Task Text Unit

-- | Stop the live subscription. This unsubscribes from the event store,
--   allowing graceful shutdown.
stop :: QuerySubscriber -> Task Text Unit

-- | Rebuild all queries from the beginning of the event store. Called on
--   application startup before starting live subscription.
rebuildAll :: QuerySubscriber -> Task Text Unit

-- | Resumable per-query rebuild from a given StreamPosition.
--   
--   Reads events from the EventStore in chunks, applies them to registered
--   query updaters for the named query, and tracks progress.
--   
--   Error variants: CheckpointFetchFailed — when store.get returns Err
--   (store unavailable) EventStoreFailed — when
--   EventStore.readAllEventsForwardFrom returns Err UpdaterException —
--   when any QueryUpdater.updateQuery returns Err HashMismatchReplay —
--   reserved for hash-mismatch deletion + replay failure RebuildTimeout —
--   when the rebuild exceeds options.timeout seconds
rebuildFrom :: QuerySubscriber -> Text -> StreamPosition -> RebuildOptions -> Task QueryRebuildError Unit

-- | Spawn async rebuild for all registered queries, updating readiness
--   states.
--   
--   Returns Ok Unit when all queries have completed (either Ready or
--   Failed). Per-query failures are recorded in readiness state, not
--   propagated as errors.
rebuildAllAsync :: QuerySubscriber -> RebuildOptions -> Task QueryRebuildError Unit

-- | Fetch the aggregate readiness state of all registered queries.
--   
--   Returns: - Ready if all registered queries are Ready (or none are
--   registered) - Rebuilding if any query is Rebuilding - Failed if any
--   query has Failed (and none are Rebuilding)
readinessOf :: QuerySubscriber -> Task Text Readiness

-- | Fetch the readiness state for a specific named query.
--   
--   Returns Nothing if the query is not registered.
readinessOfQuery :: QuerySubscriber -> Text -> Task Text (Maybe Readiness)

-- | Default rebuild options.
rebuildOptionsDefault :: RebuildOptions

-- | Compute the schema-evolution hash for a query name.
--   
--   Must produce the same value as the compile-time hash emitted by
--   <a>deriveKnownHash</a> (and the equivalent in <a>TH</a>), which both
--   use <a>hash</a> on the query name in its <tt>String</tt> form. We
--   mirror that exact pipeline: Text → Array Char → [Char] → Hashable.hash
--   :: Int → Text.
queryHashFor :: Text -> Text

-- | Handler wrapper for subscription callback. Processes the event and
--   updates lastProcessedPosition, mirroring rebuildAll behavior.
processEventHandler :: QuerySubscriber -> Event Value -> Task Text Unit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Query.Subscriber.QueryRebuildError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Query.Subscriber.Readiness
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.Query.Subscriber.RebuildOptions
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Query.Subscriber.QueryRebuildError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.Query.Subscriber.Readiness
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Query.Subscriber.QueryRebuildError
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Query.Subscriber.Readiness
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.Query.Subscriber.RebuildOptions

module Service.EventStore.Simple
data SimpleEventStore
SimpleEventStore :: Path -> Bool -> SimpleEventStore
[basePath] :: SimpleEventStore -> Path
[persistent] :: SimpleEventStore -> Bool
new :: SimpleEventStore -> Task Text (EventStore Value)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Simple.SimpleEventStore
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Simple.SubscriptionType
instance Service.EventStore.Core.EventStoreConfig Service.EventStore.Simple.SimpleEventStore
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Simple.SimpleEventStore
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Simple.SimpleEventStore
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Simple.SubscriptionType

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.SubscriptionStore
data SubscriptionStore
SubscriptionStore :: ConcurrentVar Subscriptions -> ConcurrentVar (Map StreamId Subscriptions) -> ConcurrentVar (Map EntityName Subscriptions) -> SubscriptionStore
[globalSubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar Subscriptions
[streamSubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar (Map StreamId Subscriptions)
[entitySubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar (Map EntityName Subscriptions)
data Error
UserHandlerError :: Text -> Error
OtherError :: Error
new :: Task Error SubscriptionStore
addGlobalSubscription :: SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addGlobalSubscriptionFromPosition :: Maybe StreamPosition -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addStreamSubscription :: EntityName -> StreamId -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addStreamSubscriptionFromPosition :: EntityName -> StreamId -> Maybe StreamPosition -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addStreamSubscriptionWithCleanup :: EntityName -> StreamId -> Maybe StreamPosition -> Task Text Unit -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addEntitySubscription :: EntityName -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
addEntitySubscriptionFromPosition :: EntityName -> Maybe StreamPosition -> SubscriptionCallback -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error SubscriptionId
getStreamSubscriptions :: StreamId -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error Subscriptions
getEntitySubscriptions :: EntityName -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error Subscriptions
dispatch :: StreamId -> Event Value -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error Unit
removeSubscription :: SubscriptionId -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Error Unit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Postgres.SubscriptionStore.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.SubscriptionStore.Error
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.SubscriptionStore.SubscriptionInfo

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord
data PostgresEventRecord
PostgresEventRecord :: Uuid -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Text -> Text -> Value -> Value -> PostgresEventRecord
[eventId] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Uuid
[globalPosition] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Int64
[localPosition] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Int64
[inlinedStreamId] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Text
[entityName] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Text
[eventData] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Value
[metadata] :: PostgresEventRecord -> Value
rowDecoder :: Row PostgresEventRecord
uuid' :: Value Uuid
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord.PostgresEventRecord
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord.PostgresEventRecord
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord.PostgresEventRecord
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord.PostgresEventRecord
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.PostgresEventRecord.PostgresEventRecord

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core
data PostgresStoreError
SessionError :: UsageError -> PostgresStoreError
ConnectionAcquisitionError :: Text -> PostgresStoreError
ConnectionReleaseError :: Text -> PostgresStoreError
TableInitializationError :: Text -> PostgresStoreError
SubscriptionInitializationError :: Text -> PostgresStoreError
CoreInsertionError :: InsertionFailure -> PostgresStoreError
SubscriptionStoreError :: Text -> Error -> PostgresStoreError
data RelativePosition
Start :: RelativePosition
End :: RelativePosition
FromAndAfter :: StreamPosition -> RelativePosition
Before :: StreamPosition -> RelativePosition
relativePositionToInt64 :: Maybe RelativePosition -> Maybe Int64
data ReadDirection
Forwards :: ReadDirection
Backwards :: ReadDirection
batchSize :: Int
toPostgresDirection :: Maybe RelativePosition -> Maybe ReadDirection -> Text
toPostgresGlobalPositionComparison :: Maybe ReadDirection -> Text
toPostgresLocalPositionComparison :: Maybe ReadDirection -> Text
toPostgresPosition :: Maybe RelativePosition -> Maybe ReadDirection -> Int64
toPostgresEntityFilters :: Maybe (Array EntityName) -> Text

instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.ReadDirection
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.RelativePosition
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.ReadDirection
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.RelativePosition

instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.ReadDirection
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.Core.RelativePosition

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions
data Connection
Connection :: Pool -> Connection
MockConnection :: Connection
data EventInsertionRecord
EventInsertionRecord :: Uuid -> Maybe Int64 -> Int64 -> Text -> Text -> Value -> Value -> EventInsertionRecord
[eventId] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Uuid
[globalPosition] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Maybe Int64
[localPosition] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Int64
[inlinedStreamId] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Text
[entity] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Text
[eventData] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Value
[metadata] :: EventInsertionRecord -> Value
data EventNotificationPayload
EventNotificationPayload :: Uuid -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Text -> Text -> EventNotificationPayload
[eventId] :: EventNotificationPayload -> Uuid
[globalPosition] :: EventNotificationPayload -> Int64
[localPosition] :: EventNotificationPayload -> Int64
[inlinedStreamId] :: EventNotificationPayload -> Text
[entity] :: EventNotificationPayload -> Text
postgresRecordToEvent :: PostgresEventRecord -> Result Text (Event Value)
insertionRecordToEvent :: FromJSON eventType => EventInsertionRecord -> Result Text (Event eventType)
run :: Default result => Connection -> Session result -> Task UsageError result
runConnection :: Default result => Connection -> Session result -> Task SessionError result
selectEventByGlobalPositionSession :: Int64 -> Session (Maybe PostgresEventRecord)

-- | Read at most <tt>limit</tt> events with <tt>GlobalPosition &gt;
--   cursor</tt>, ascending. Used by the LISTEN/NOTIFY reconnect catch-up
--   (ADR-0061) to replay the gap in bounded batches, so a long listener
--   outage cannot pull an unbounded result set into one <a>Array</a> (the
--   caller, <tt>catchUpFromCursor</tt>, loops over batches and advances
--   the cursor between them). Both the cursor (<tt>$1</tt>) and the batch
--   limit (<tt>$2</tt>) are parameterised, never interpolated, mirroring
--   <a>selectEventByGlobalPositionSession</a>. The <tt>&gt;</tt> (strict)
--   makes the read exclusive of the cursor, so the already-dispatched
--   cursor event is not re-read.
selectEventsForwardFromGlobalPositionSession :: Int64 -> Int64 -> Session (Array PostgresEventRecord)
createEventsTableSession :: Session Unit
dropEventsTableSession :: Session Unit
createEventNotificationTriggerFunctionSession :: Session Unit
createEventNotificationTriggerSession :: Session Unit
selectExistingIdsSession :: Array Uuid -> Session (Array Uuid)
toLegacyUuids :: Array Uuid -> Vector UUID
fromLegacyUuids :: Vector UUID -> Array Uuid
selectLatestEventInStream :: EntityName -> StreamId -> Session (Maybe (StreamPosition, StreamPosition))
selectMaxGlobalPosition :: Session (Maybe StreamPosition)
selectInsertedEvent :: Uuid -> Session (Maybe (StreamPosition, StreamPosition))
insertRecordsIntoStream :: Array EventInsertionRecord -> Session Unit
selectEventBatch :: Var Int64 -> Maybe RelativePosition -> Maybe ReadDirection -> Maybe (Array EntityName) -> Task PostgresStoreError (Session (Array PostgresEventRecord))
selectStreamEventBatch :: Var Int64 -> EntityName -> StreamId -> Maybe RelativePosition -> Maybe ReadDirection -> Task PostgresStoreError (Session (Array PostgresEventRecord))
truncateStreamSession :: EntityName -> StreamId -> StreamPosition -> Session Unit
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventInsertionRecord
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventNotificationPayload
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventInsertionRecord
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventNotificationPayload
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventInsertionRecord
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventNotificationPayload
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventInsertionRecord
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventNotificationPayload
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventInsertionRecord
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.EventStore.Postgres.Sessions.EventNotificationPayload

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.Notifications
connectTo :: Task Text (Connection, Connection) -> SubscriptionStore -> Task Text (Task Text Unit)
nextBackoff :: Int -> Int
subscribeToStream :: Connection -> StreamId -> Task Text Unit

-- | Replay every event committed after the last-processed
--   <tt>globalPosition</tt> through the shared <a>SubscriptionStore</a>,
--   advancing the cursor as it goes. Invoked on each listener reconnect,
--   AFTER <tt>LISTEN</tt> is re-established and BEFORE the live wait, so
--   the gap created while the listen socket was down reaches live dispatch
--   (ADR-0061). The dispatch sink is identical to the live handler's, so
--   there is one sink and one read implementation.
--   
--   The read is BOUNDED: rather than pulling every row after the cursor
--   into one <a>Array</a> (which a long outage could blow up), it reads in
--   pages of <a>catchUpBatchSize</a>, dispatches each page, advances the
--   cursor to the last <a>globalPosition</a> in the page (via
--   <a>dispatchCaughtUpRecord</a>), and repeats until a page returns fewer
--   rows than the limit (the gap is drained). Paging is monotonic in
--   <tt>globalPosition</tt>, so at-least-once/idempotency are preserved
--   and ordering is unchanged.
catchUpFromCursor :: Connection -> SubscriptionStore -> Var Int64 -> Task Text Unit

module Service.EventStore.Postgres.Internal

-- | PostgreSQL event store configuration.
--   
--   For config-dependent values, pass a factory function to
--   <a>withEventStore</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.withEventStore makePostgresConfig
--   </pre>
--   
--   The config type is inferred from the factory signature. This avoids
--   the chicken-and-egg problem where Config.get panics before
--   Application.run.
data PostgresEventStore
PostgresEventStore :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Int -> Int -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> PostgresEventStore
[host] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[databaseName] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[user] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[password] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[port] :: PostgresEventStore -> Int

-- | Connection-pool size for the EventStore pool (also shared by the
--   FileUpload state-store pool). Defaults to 6, sized for Azure Flexible
--   Server B1ms (~35 usable connections); see ADR-0060 for the aggregate
--   budget. Raise per deployment tier via this field.
[poolSize] :: PostgresEventStore -> Int
[sslMode] :: PostgresEventStore -> SslMode
[sslRootCert] :: PostgresEventStore -> Maybe Text
data Ops
Ops :: (PostgresEventStore -> Task Text Connection) -> (Connection -> Task Text Unit) -> (Connection -> Task Text Unit) -> (Connection -> SubscriptionStore -> PostgresEventStore -> Task Text (Task Text Unit)) -> Ops
[acquire] :: Ops -> PostgresEventStore -> Task Text Connection
[release] :: Ops -> Connection -> Task Text Unit
[initializeTable] :: Ops -> Connection -> Task Text Unit
[initializeSubscriptions] :: Ops -> Connection -> SubscriptionStore -> PostgresEventStore -> Task Text (Task Text Unit)
new :: Ops -> PostgresEventStore -> Task Text (EventStore Value)
defaultOps :: Ops
data Connection
Connection :: Pool -> Connection
MockConnection :: Connection
data SubscriptionStore
SubscriptionStore :: ConcurrentVar Subscriptions -> ConcurrentVar (Map StreamId Subscriptions) -> ConcurrentVar (Map EntityName Subscriptions) -> SubscriptionStore
[globalSubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar Subscriptions
[streamSubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar (Map StreamId Subscriptions)
[entitySubscriptions] :: SubscriptionStore -> ConcurrentVar (Map EntityName Subscriptions)
withConnection :: PostgresEventStore -> (Connection -> Task PostgresStoreError result) -> Ops -> Task PostgresStoreError result
toConnectionSettings :: PostgresEventStore -> Result Text (LinkedList Setting)
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Service.EventStore.Postgres.Internal.PostgresEventStore
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.EventStore.Postgres.Internal.PostgresEventStore
instance Service.EventStore.Core.EventStoreConfig Service.EventStore.Postgres.Internal.PostgresEventStore
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Service.EventStore.Postgres.Internal.PostgresEventStore

module Service.EventStore.Postgres

-- | PostgreSQL event store configuration.
--   
--   For config-dependent values, pass a factory function to
--   <a>withEventStore</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.withEventStore makePostgresConfig
--   </pre>
--   
--   The config type is inferred from the factory signature. This avoids
--   the chicken-and-egg problem where Config.get panics before
--   Application.run.
data PostgresEventStore
PostgresEventStore :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Int -> Int -> SslMode -> Maybe Text -> PostgresEventStore
[host] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[databaseName] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[user] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[password] :: PostgresEventStore -> Text
[port] :: PostgresEventStore -> Int

-- | Connection-pool size for the EventStore pool (also shared by the
--   FileUpload state-store pool). Defaults to 6, sized for Azure Flexible
--   Server B1ms (~35 usable connections); see ADR-0060 for the aggregate
--   budget. Raise per deployment tier via this field.
[poolSize] :: PostgresEventStore -> Int
[sslMode] :: PostgresEventStore -> SslMode
[sslRootCert] :: PostgresEventStore -> Maybe Text
new :: PostgresEventStore -> Task Text (EventStore Value)


-- | PostgreSQL-backed FileStateStore for production use.
--   
--   This module provides a persistent FileStateStore implementation that
--   survives server restarts, unlike the in-memory store.
--   
--   <h1>Usage</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Service.EventStore.Postgres (PostgresEventStore (..))
--   import Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres qualified as PostgresFileStore
--   
--   -- Use the same config as your event store
--   let dbConfig = PostgresEventStore
--         { host = "localhost"
--         , port = 5432
--         , databaseName = "myapp"
--         , user = "myuser"
--         , password = "mypassword"
--         }
--   
--   stateStore &lt;- PostgresFileStore.new dbConfig
--   
--   -- Use in FileUploadSetup
--   let setup = FileUploadSetup
--         { config = ...
--         , blobStore = ...
--         , stateStore = stateStore
--         }
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Schema</h1>
--   
--   The module creates a <tt>file_upload_state</tt> table if it doesn't
--   exist. See <a>initializeTable</a> for the schema definition.
module Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres

-- | Create a new PostgreSQL-backed FileStateStore.
--   
--   Uses the same connection config as the PostgresEventStore, so you can
--   share database settings across your application.
--   
--   Creates the file_upload_state table if it doesn't exist.
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Service.EventStore.Postgres (PostgresEventStore (..))
--   import Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres qualified as PostgresFileStore
--   
--   let dbConfig = PostgresEventStore { ... }
--   stateStore &lt;- PostgresFileStore.new dbConfig
--   </pre>
new :: PostgresEventStore -> Task Text FileStateStore

-- | Create a PostgreSQL-backed FileStateStore with access to the
--   connection pool.
--   
--   Use this when you need the pool for cleanup operations. The returned
--   pool should be reused for all cleanup calls - never create pools
--   per-call.
--   
--   <pre>
--   (stateStore, pool) &lt;- PostgresFileStore.newWithCleanup dbConfig
--   
--   -- Use stateStore for normal operations
--   let setup = FileUploadSetup { stateStore = stateStore, ... }
--   
--   -- Use pool for cleanup worker (runs periodically)
--   cleanupWorker = do
--     expired &lt;- PostgresFileStore.findExpiredPendingFiles pool currentTime
--     for_ expired $ \(fileRef, blobKey) -&gt; do
--       blobStore.delete blobKey
--       PostgresFileStore.deleteFileState pool fileRef
--   </pre>
newWithCleanup :: PostgresEventStore -> Task Text (FileStateStore, Pool)

-- | Build the shared <a>PostgresEventStore</a> connection config that the
--   FileUpload state-store pool runs on, from the declarative
--   <tt>PostgresStateStore</tt> backend. WI-5 (#684): threads the
--   operator's <tt>pgSslMode</tt>/<tt>pgSslRootCert</tt> through so the
--   FileUpload pool honours DB_SSL_MODE exactly like the EventStore pool,
--   closing the silent-TLS-downgrade gap (ADR-0064).
--   <tt>InMemoryStateStore</tt> has no Postgres connection config, so it
--   maps to <a>Nothing</a>.
toEventStoreConfig :: FileStateStoreBackend -> Maybe PostgresEventStore
data Pool

-- | Errors that can occur when using PostgreSQL FileStateStore
data PostgresFileStoreError
PoolError :: UsageError -> PostgresFileStoreError
DeserializationError :: Text -> PostgresFileStoreError
InvalidPoolSize :: Text -> PostgresFileStoreError
InvalidPort :: Text -> PostgresFileStoreError

-- | Find all pending files that have expired. Returns list of (FileRef,
--   BlobKey) for cleanup.
--   
--   IMPORTANT: Use the pool from <a>newWithCleanup</a>. Do NOT create
--   pools per-call.
--   
--   <pre>
--   (stateStore, pool) &lt;- PostgresFileStore.newWithCleanup dbConfig
--   -- ... later in cleanup worker ...
--   expired &lt;- PostgresFileStore.findExpiredPendingFiles pool currentTime
--   for_ expired $ \(fileRef, blobKey) -&gt; do
--     blobStore.delete blobKey
--     PostgresFileStore.deleteFileState pool fileRef
--   </pre>
findExpiredPendingFiles :: Pool -> Int64 -> Task Text [(FileRef, BlobKey)]

-- | Delete a file state record.
--   
--   IMPORTANT: Use the pool from <a>newWithCleanup</a>. Do NOT create
--   pools per-call.
deleteFileState :: Pool -> FileRef -> Task Text ()
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres.FileStateRow
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres.PostgresFileStoreError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres.FileStateRow
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres.FileStateRow
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore.Postgres.PostgresFileStoreError

module Service.EventStore.InMemory
new :: Task Error (EventStore Value)

module Service.CommandExecutor.TH
command :: Name -> DecsQ

-- | Derives a KnownHash instance for a given string at compile time. This
--   generates: instance KnownHash "yourString" where hashVal _ =
--   <a>hash</a>
deriveKnownHash :: String -> DecsQ
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Service.CommandExecutor.TH.MultiTenancyMode
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Service.CommandExecutor.TH.FunctionInfo
instance GHC.Show.Show Service.CommandExecutor.TH.MultiTenancyMode

module Service.Transport.Web

-- | HTTP<i>JSON transport using WAI</i>Warp.
data WebTransport
WebTransport :: Int -> Int -> Maybe AuthEnabled -> Maybe OAuth2Config -> Maybe FileUploadEnabled -> Maybe ApiInfo -> Maybe CorsConfig -> Maybe HealthCheckConfig -> Maybe IntegrationStatus -> Maybe ReadinessConfig -> Maybe (Task Text Readiness) -> WebTransport
[port] :: WebTransport -> Int
[maxBodySize] :: WebTransport -> Int

-- | Optional JWT authentication. Set via Application.withAuth.
[authEnabled] :: WebTransport -> Maybe AuthEnabled

-- | Optional OAuth2 provider routes. Set via
--   Application.withOAuth2Provider.
[oauth2Config] :: WebTransport -> Maybe OAuth2Config

-- | Optional file upload routes. Set via Application.withFileUpload.
[fileUploadEnabled] :: WebTransport -> Maybe FileUploadEnabled

-- | Optional API metadata for OpenAPI spec generation. Set via
--   Application.withApiInfo.
[apiInfo] :: WebTransport -> Maybe ApiInfo

-- | Optional CORS configuration. Set via Application.withCors.
[corsConfig] :: WebTransport -> Maybe CorsConfig

-- | Optional health check endpoint. Enabled by default at /health. Set via
--   Application.withHealthCheck or disabled via
--   Application.withoutHealthCheck.
[healthCheck] :: WebTransport -> Maybe HealthCheckConfig

-- | Integration selection status for /health reporting. Set via
--   Application.run.
[integrationStatus] :: WebTransport -> Maybe IntegrationStatus

-- | Readiness endpoint configuration. Set via
--   Application.withReadinessProbe. When Nothing, GET /ready returns 404.
[readinessConfig] :: WebTransport -> Maybe ReadinessConfig

-- | Readiness probe closure. Built from the live Subscriber in
--   Application.run. When Nothing, GET /ready returns 404.
[readinessProbe] :: WebTransport -> Maybe (Task Text Readiness)

-- | Auth configuration for WebTransport endpoints. When enabled, all
--   endpoints require a valid JWT. Permission checks should be done in the
--   command's decide method.
data AuthEnabled
AuthEnabled :: JwksManager -> AuthConfig -> AuthEnabled
[jwksManager] :: AuthEnabled -> JwksManager
[authConfig] :: AuthEnabled -> AuthConfig

-- | OAuth2 provider configuration for WebTransport. When set, enables
--   <i>connect</i>{provider}, <i>callback</i>{provider},
--   <i>disconnect</i>{provider} routes.
data OAuth2Config
OAuth2Config :: OAuth2Routes -> (Text -> Task Text Unit) -> OAuth2Config

-- | OAuth2 route handlers
[routes] :: OAuth2Config -> OAuth2Routes

-- | Action dispatcher for OAuth2 callbacks. Called with the JSON-encoded
--   action from onSuccess<i>onFailure</i>onDisconnect. The application
--   layer provides this to dispatch actions to command handlers.
[dispatchAction] :: OAuth2Config -> Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | File upload configuration for WebTransport. When set, enables POST
--   <i>files</i>upload and GET <i>files</i>:fileRef routes.
data FileUploadEnabled
FileUploadEnabled :: FileUploadRoutes -> Int64 -> FileUploadEnabled

-- | File upload route handlers from Service.FileUpload.Web
[fileUploadRoutes] :: FileUploadEnabled -> FileUploadRoutes

-- | Maximum request body size for file uploads. This is used to coordinate
--   WebTransport.maxBodySize with FileUploadConfig.maxFileSizeBytes. See
--   ADR-0019 for details.
[maxRequestBodyBytes] :: FileUploadEnabled -> Int64

-- | CORS configuration for WebTransport. When set, CORS headers are added
--   to all responses and OPTIONS preflight requests are handled
--   automatically.
data CorsConfig
CorsConfig :: Array Text -> Array Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Int -> CorsConfig

-- | Allowed origins. Use ["*"] for any origin, or specific origins.
[allowedOrigins] :: CorsConfig -> Array Text

-- | Allowed HTTP methods (e.g., [<a>GET</a>, <a>POST</a>,
--   <a>OPTIONS</a>]).
[allowedMethods] :: CorsConfig -> Array Text

-- | Allowed request headers (e.g., ["Content-Type",
--   <a>Authorization</a>]).
[allowedHeaders] :: CorsConfig -> Array Text

-- | Preflight cache duration in seconds. Nothing means no max-age header.
[maxAge] :: CorsConfig -> Maybe Int

-- | Health check configuration for WebTransport. When enabled, GET
--   requests to the health path return 200 OK with a JSON body. Enabled by
--   default with path "health". Can be disabled or customized.
data HealthCheckConfig
HealthCheckConfig :: Text -> HealthCheckConfig

-- | URL path segment for the health endpoint (default: "health"). The
--   endpoint will be available at /{healthPath}.
[healthPath] :: HealthCheckConfig -> Text

-- | Integration mode and active fakes for the /health endpoint. Set by
--   Application.run based on the --integrations flag.
data IntegrationStatus
IntegrationStatus :: !Text -> !Array Text -> IntegrationStatus
[mode] :: IntegrationStatus -> !Text
[fakes] :: IntegrationStatus -> !Array Text

-- | Default WebTransport configuration. Port defaults to 8080. Max body
--   size defaults to 1MB (1048576 bytes) to prevent DoS attacks. Auth is
--   disabled by default - use Application.withAuth to enable. OAuth2 is
--   disabled by default - use Application.withOAuth2Provider to enable.
--   File uploads are disabled by default - use Application.withFileUpload
--   to enable. Health check is enabled by default at /health - use
--   Application.withoutHealthCheck to disable.
server :: WebTransport

-- | Check if a path segment matches the configured health check path.
--   Returns False if health check is disabled (healthCheck = Nothing).
isHealthCheckPath :: Text -> WebTransport -> Bool

-- | Build the JSON response body for the health endpoint. When integration
--   status is present it is included in the response.
buildHealthResponse :: WebTransport -> ByteString

-- | Check if a path segment matches the configured readiness path. Returns
--   False if readiness config is absent (readinessConfig = Nothing).
isReadinessPath :: Text -> WebTransport -> Bool

-- | Respond to a GET /ready request using the transport's readiness probe.
--   Returns 404 when no probe is configured, 200 when Ready, 503
--   otherwise.
respondReadiness :: WebTransport -> (Response -> Task Text ResponseReceived) -> Task Text ResponseReceived

-- | Map a Readiness value to an HTTP status and JSON body. Body shapes per
--   ADR-0059's "Readiness endpoint contract": Ready → 200
--   {"status":"ready"} Rebuilding → 503 {"status":"rebuilding"} Failed
--   reason → 503 {"status":"failed","reason":"..."}
renderReadiness :: Readiness -> (Status, ByteString)

-- | Map a per-command auth error to its HTTP status and user-facing
--   message. The message intentionally carries no information about the
--   caller's claims or the permission name — that data stays on the server
--   side and is only visible in the audit log.
unauthorizedResponse :: AccessError -> (Status, Text)

-- | Wrap an auth error message in the {"error": ...} JSON envelope so the
--   response body matches the application/json content-type.
unauthorizedResponseBody :: Text -> Text
instance Data.Record.Anon.Plugin.Internal.Runtime.KnownHash "WebTransport"
instance Service.Transport.Transport Service.Transport.Web.WebTransport


-- | # Service.Application.Transports
--   
--   Transport runtime for the Application.
--   
--   This module handles running transports with their combined endpoints.
module Service.Application.Transports

-- | Run all transports with their combined endpoints.
--   
--   Each transport is started exactly once with all command and query
--   endpoints from all services that use that transport.
--   
--   If <tt>maybeWebAuth</tt> is provided, WebTransport will enforce JWT
--   authentication. If <tt>maybeOAuth2</tt> is provided, WebTransport will
--   enable OAuth2 provider routes. If <tt>maybeFileUpload</tt> is
--   provided, WebTransport will enable file upload/download routes. Other
--   transports ignore these parameters.
runTransports :: Map Text TransportValue -> Map Text (Map Text EndpointHandler) -> Map Text (Map Text EndpointSchema) -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler -> Map Text EndpointSchema -> Maybe AuthEnabled -> Maybe OAuth2Config -> Maybe FileUploadEnabled -> Maybe ApiInfo -> Maybe CorsConfig -> Maybe HealthCheckConfig -> Maybe IntegrationStatus -> Maybe ReadinessConfig -> QuerySubscriber -> Task Text Unit

module Service.Transport.Mcp
data McpTransport
McpTransport :: Text -> Text -> McpTransport
[serverName] :: McpTransport -> Text
[serverVersion] :: McpTransport -> Text
instance Data.Record.Anon.Plugin.Internal.Runtime.KnownHash "McpTransport"
instance Service.Transport.Transport Service.Transport.Mcp.McpTransport

module Service.Transport.Cli
data CliTransport
CliTransport :: Text -> Text -> Text -> CliTransport
[programName] :: CliTransport -> Text
[version] :: CliTransport -> Text
[description] :: CliTransport -> Text

-- | Default CLI transport configuration.
cli :: CliTransport
instance Data.Record.Anon.Plugin.Internal.Runtime.KnownHash "CliTransport"
instance Service.Transport.Transport Service.Transport.Cli.CliTransport

module Service.OutboundIntegration.TH

-- | Generate OutboundIntegration-related instances for a handler type.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   data ReserveStockOnItemAdded = ReserveStockOnItemAdded
--     deriving (Generic, Typeable, Show)
--   
--   type instance EntityOf ReserveStockOnItemAdded = CartEntity
--   
--   handleEvent :: CartEntity -&gt; CartEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   handleEvent cart event = case event of
--     ItemAdded {stockId, quantity} -&gt; Integration.batch [...]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   
--   outboundIntegration ''ReserveStockOnItemAdded
--   </pre>
--   
--   Validates at compile time:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>EntityOf</a> instance exists for the handler</li>
--   <li><a>EventOf</a> resolves from the entity</li>
--   <li><tt>handleEvent</tt> function exists with correct signature:
--   <tt>EntityType -&gt; EventType -&gt; Integration.Outbound</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>instance OutboundIntegration HandlerType</tt> with
--   <a>HandledEvent</a> set to the event ADT type (EventOf entity)</li>
--   <li><pre>type instance NameOf HandlerType =
--   <a>HandlerType</a></pre></li>
--   <li><pre>instance KnownHash <a>HandlerType</a></pre></li>
--   </ul>
outboundIntegration :: Name -> DecsQ


-- | Core types for the Config DSL.
--   
--   This module defines the data types used to represent configuration
--   field definitions before they are compiled into opt-env-conf parsers.
module Config.Core

-- | A single field definition within a config type.
--   
--   Built incrementally using the Config.Builder combinators:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
data FieldDef
FieldDef :: Text -> Type -> [FieldModifier] -> FieldDef

-- | Field name (becomes the record field name)
[fieldName] :: FieldDef -> Text

-- | The Haskell type of this field
[fieldType] :: FieldDef -> Type

-- | Accumulated modifiers
[fieldModifiers] :: FieldDef -> [FieldModifier]

-- | Modifiers that can be applied to a config field.
--   
--   These are accumulated by builder functions and converted to
--   opt-env-conf builders during TH expansion.
data FieldModifier

-- | Documentation string (shown in --help and error messages)
ModDoc :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Environment variable name override
ModEnvVar :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Default value (as a TH Q Exp for splicing)
ModDefault :: Q Exp -> FieldModifier

-- | Field is required (mutually exclusive with ModDefault)
ModRequired :: FieldModifier

-- | Sensitive value - redacted in logs and --help
ModSecret :: FieldModifier

-- | Long CLI option name (e.g., "port" for --port)
ModCliLong :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Short CLI option character (e.g., <tt>p</tt> for -p)
ModCliShort :: Char -> FieldModifier

-- | Environment variable prefix for nested configs
ModEnvPrefix :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Mark field as a nested config (uses subSettings instead of setting)
ModNested :: FieldModifier

-- | Validation errors detected at compile time.
data ConfigError

-- | Field is missing Config.doc
MissingDoc :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Field has neither defaultsTo nor required
MissingDefaultOrRequired :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Field has both defaultsTo and required (invalid)
BothDefaultAndRequired :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Validate a field definition, returning any errors found.
validateFieldDef :: FieldDef -> [ConfigError]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Config.Core.ConfigError
instance GHC.Show.Show Config.Core.ConfigError


-- | Template Haskell macro for the NeoHaskell Config DSL.
--   
--   This module provides the <a>defineConfig</a> macro that generates:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>A data type with the specified fields</li>
--   <li>A <tt>HasParser</tt> instance for opt-env-conf integration</li>
--   <li>A <tt>HasXxxConfig</tt> type alias for implicit parameter
--   access</li>
--   </ol>
module Config.TH

-- | Define a configuration type with the given name and fields.
--   
--   This macro generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>A data type: <tt>data AppConfig = AppConfig { field1 :: Type1, ...
--   }</tt></li>
--   <li>A custom Show instance that redacts secret fields</li>
--   <li>A HasParser instance using opt-env-conf</li>
--   <li>A type alias: <tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config ::
--   AppConfig)</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   All fields must have <a>doc</a> and either <a>defaultsTo</a> or
--   <a>required</a>.
defineConfig :: Text -> [FieldDef] -> Q [Dec]

-- | Convert camelCase field name to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE env var name.
--   
--   Examples: * <tt>port</tt> -&gt; <tt>PORT</tt> * <tt>databaseUrl</tt>
--   -&gt; <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt> * <tt>openRouterKey</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>OPEN_ROUTER_KEY</tt> * <tt>oauth2ClientId</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID</tt> * <tt>http2Enabled</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>HTTP2_ENABLED</tt>
toEnvVarName :: Text -> Text


-- | Builder combinators for the Config DSL.
--   
--   These functions provide a fluent API for defining configuration
--   fields:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>PORT</a>
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>
--   </pre>
module Config.Builder

-- | Start defining a configuration field with the given type and name.
--   
--   The type is captured via TypeApplications:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--   Config.field @(Maybe Int) "optionalPort"
--   </pre>
field :: forall {k} (fieldType :: k). Typeable fieldType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define an enum configuration field.
--   
--   This is semantically equivalent to <a>field</a> but makes the intent
--   clear when defining enum-typed configuration. The enum type must have
--   <tt>Read</tt> and <a>Show</a> instances for parsing from strings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data LogLevel = Debug | Info | Warn | Error
--     deriving (Show, Read, Generic)
--   
--   Config.enum @LogLevel "logLevel"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "Minimum log level"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo Info
--   </pre>
enum :: forall {k} (enumType :: k). Typeable enumType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define a nested configuration field.
--   
--   Nested configs allow composing multiple config types together. The
--   nested type must also have a <tt>HasParser</tt> instance (typically
--   from its own <tt>defineConfig</tt> declaration).
--   
--   Use <a>envPrefix</a> to namespace the nested config's environment
--   variables.
--   
--   <pre>
--   defineConfig <a>DatabaseConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Text "host"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database host"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo "localhost"
--     , Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database port"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 5432
--     ]
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database connection settings"
--         |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   With <tt>envPrefix <a>DB_</a></tt>, the nested fields become:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>DB_HOST</tt> (defaults to "localhost")</li>
--   <li><tt>DB_PORT</tt> (defaults to 5432)</li>
--   </ul>
nested :: forall {k} (nestedType :: k). Typeable nestedType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Add documentation for a field.
--   
--   Documentation is REQUIRED and will cause a compile-time error if
--   missing. It appears in <tt>--help</tt> output and error messages.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--   </pre>
doc :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Specify the environment variable name for a field.
--   
--   If not specified, the field name is converted to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
--   <tt>databaseUrl</tt> becomes <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--   </pre>
envVar :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set a default value for a field.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>required</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   The value must have a Lift instance so it can be spliced into the
--   generated code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
defaultsTo :: Lift value => value -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as required.
--   
--   Required fields must be provided via CLI, environment variable, or
--   config file. Missing required fields cause startup to fail with a
--   helpful error message.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>defaultsTo</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
required :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as containing sensitive data.
--   
--   Secret fields: - Are redacted in <tt>--help</tt> output - Are redacted
--   in error messages - Should use the <tt>Secret</tt> wrapper type for
--   additional protection
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--     |&gt; Config.secret
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
secret :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a long CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"  -- enables --port 8080
--   </pre>
cliLong :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a short CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>  -- enables -p 8080
--   </pre>
cliShort :: Char -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set environment variable prefix for nested configs.
--   
--   When using nested configs, this prefix is prepended to all nested
--   field environment variable names.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--     |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--   -- Nested fields: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, etc.
--   </pre>
envPrefix :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef


-- | Declarative configuration DSL for NeoHaskell applications.
--   
--   This module provides a type-safe, fail-fast configuration system that
--   validates all configuration at startup before the application accepts
--   requests.
--   
--   <h1>Quick Start</h1>
--   
--   Define your config using <a>defineConfig</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE TemplateHaskell #-}
--   module MyApp.Config where
--   
--   import Config (defineConfig)
--   import Config qualified
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--         |&gt; Config.envVar <a>PORT</a>
--         |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"
--         |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>
--   
--     , Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "PostgreSQL connection string"
--         |&gt; Config.required
--         |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><pre>data AppConfig = AppConfig { port :: Int, databaseUrl :: Text
--   }</pre></li>
--   <li><tt>instance HasParser AppConfig</tt> (for opt-env-conf)</li>
--   <li><tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config :: AppConfig)</tt> (for implicit
--   parameter access)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage Patterns</h1>
--   
--   <h2>Via Application Framework (Recommended)</h2>
--   
--   Register config with <a>withConfig</a> for automatic loading and
--   injection:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app :: Application
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withConfig @AppConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = Application.run app |&gt; Task.runOrPanic
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Explicit Access</h2>
--   
--   After <a>run</a>, access config anywhere via <a>get</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   myFunction :: Task err ()
--   myFunction = do
--     let port = (Config.get @AppConfig).port
--     ...
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Direct Loading (Advanced)</h2>
--   
--   For standalone scripts or testing, use <a>load</a> directly:
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config <a>Config.load @AppConfig |</a> Task.runOrPanic
--     let ?config = config
--     runApp
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Accessing Configuration</h1>
--   
--   With the implicit parameter in scope, access config fields directly:
--   
--   <pre>
--   startServer :: (HasAppConfig) =&gt; Task Error ()
--   startServer = do
--     Console.printLine [fmt|Starting on port {?config.port}|]
--     ...
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Field Modifiers</h1>
--   
--   | Modifier | Description | |----------|-------------| | <a>doc</a> |
--   Documentation (REQUIRED) | | <a>envVar</a> | Environment variable name
--   | | <a>defaultsTo</a> | Default value | | <a>required</a> | Must be
--   provided | | <a>secret</a> | Redacted in output | | <a>cliLong</a> |
--   Long CLI flag | | <a>cliShort</a> | Short CLI flag | |
--   <a>envPrefix</a> | Prefix for nested configs |
module Config

-- | Define a configuration type with the given name and fields.
--   
--   This macro generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>A data type: <tt>data AppConfig = AppConfig { field1 :: Type1, ...
--   }</tt></li>
--   <li>A custom Show instance that redacts secret fields</li>
--   <li>A HasParser instance using opt-env-conf</li>
--   <li>A type alias: <tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config ::
--   AppConfig)</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   All fields must have <a>doc</a> and either <a>defaultsTo</a> or
--   <a>required</a>.
defineConfig :: Text -> [FieldDef] -> Q [Dec]

-- | Start defining a configuration field with the given type and name.
--   
--   The type is captured via TypeApplications:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--   Config.field @(Maybe Int) "optionalPort"
--   </pre>
field :: forall {k} (fieldType :: k). Typeable fieldType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define an enum configuration field.
--   
--   This is semantically equivalent to <a>field</a> but makes the intent
--   clear when defining enum-typed configuration. The enum type must have
--   <tt>Read</tt> and <a>Show</a> instances for parsing from strings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data LogLevel = Debug | Info | Warn | Error
--     deriving (Show, Read, Generic)
--   
--   Config.enum @LogLevel "logLevel"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "Minimum log level"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo Info
--   </pre>
enum :: forall {k} (enumType :: k). Typeable enumType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define a nested configuration field.
--   
--   Nested configs allow composing multiple config types together. The
--   nested type must also have a <tt>HasParser</tt> instance (typically
--   from its own <tt>defineConfig</tt> declaration).
--   
--   Use <a>envPrefix</a> to namespace the nested config's environment
--   variables.
--   
--   <pre>
--   defineConfig <a>DatabaseConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Text "host"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database host"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo "localhost"
--     , Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database port"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 5432
--     ]
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database connection settings"
--         |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   With <tt>envPrefix <a>DB_</a></tt>, the nested fields become:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>DB_HOST</tt> (defaults to "localhost")</li>
--   <li><tt>DB_PORT</tt> (defaults to 5432)</li>
--   </ul>
nested :: forall {k} (nestedType :: k). Typeable nestedType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Add documentation for a field.
--   
--   Documentation is REQUIRED and will cause a compile-time error if
--   missing. It appears in <tt>--help</tt> output and error messages.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--   </pre>
doc :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Specify the environment variable name for a field.
--   
--   If not specified, the field name is converted to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
--   <tt>databaseUrl</tt> becomes <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--   </pre>
envVar :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set a default value for a field.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>required</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   The value must have a Lift instance so it can be spliced into the
--   generated code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
defaultsTo :: Lift value => value -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as required.
--   
--   Required fields must be provided via CLI, environment variable, or
--   config file. Missing required fields cause startup to fail with a
--   helpful error message.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>defaultsTo</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
required :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as containing sensitive data.
--   
--   Secret fields: - Are redacted in <tt>--help</tt> output - Are redacted
--   in error messages - Should use the <tt>Secret</tt> wrapper type for
--   additional protection
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--     |&gt; Config.secret
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
secret :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a long CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"  -- enables --port 8080
--   </pre>
cliLong :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a short CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>  -- enables -p 8080
--   </pre>
cliShort :: Char -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set environment variable prefix for nested configs.
--   
--   When using nested configs, this prefix is prepended to all nested
--   field environment variable names.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--     |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--   -- Nested fields: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, etc.
--   </pre>
envPrefix :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Load configuration from CLI args, environment variables, and config
--   files.
--   
--   This parses configuration at startup and fails fast if any required
--   configuration is missing or invalid.
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config &lt;- Config.load @AppConfig
--     let ?config = config
--     runApp
--   </pre>
load :: HasParser config => Task Text config

-- | Load configuration with explicit version and description.
--   
--   The version and description are shown in <tt>--help</tt> output.
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config &lt;- Config.loadWithVersion version "My Application" @MyConfig
--     ...
--   </pre>
loadWithVersion :: HasParser config => Version -> Text -> Task Text config

-- | Get the global config.
--   
--   This function retrieves the application config that was loaded during
--   <a>run</a>. It uses <a>Typeable</a> to recover the concrete type from
--   the type-erased <a>Dynamic</a> storage.
--   
--   <b>IMPORTANT</b>: This function MUST be called after <a>run</a> has
--   loaded the config. Calling it before will result in a runtime error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   myFunction :: Task err ()
--   myFunction = do
--     let port = (Config.get @AppConfig).port
--     Console.print [fmt|Using port: {port}|]
--   </pre>
get :: (Typeable config, HasCallStack) => config

-- | Run an action with a specific configuration for testing.
--   
--   This allows testing code that uses the <tt>HasXxxConfig</tt>
--   constraint without setting up environment variables or CLI arguments.
--   
--   <pre>
--   spec :: Spec
--   spec = describe "OpenRouter integration" do
--     it "sends requests with API key" do
--       let testConfig = def { openRouterKey = Redacted.wrap "test-key" }
--       result &lt;- testWithConfig testConfig do
--         sendToOpenRouter testRequest
--       result.headers `shouldContain` (<a>Authorization</a>, "Bearer test-key")
--   </pre>
testWithConfig :: config -> ((?config :: config) => result) -> result
class HasParser a

module Service.Application
data Application
Application :: Maybe ConfigSpec -> Maybe EventStoreFactory -> Maybe QueryObjectStoreConfigValue -> Array QueryDefinition -> QueryRegistry -> Array ServiceRunner -> Map Text TransportValue -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler -> Array OutboundRunner -> Array OutboundLifecycleRunner -> Array Inbound -> Maybe WebAuthFactory -> Maybe OAuth2Setup -> Maybe FileUploadFactory -> Maybe SecretStore -> Maybe ApiInfo -> Maybe CorsConfig -> Maybe HealthCheckConfig -> Maybe DispatcherConfig -> Maybe CorsFactory -> Maybe ApiInfoFactory -> Maybe HealthCheckFactory -> Maybe DispatcherConfigFactory -> Maybe SecretStoreFactory -> Array DeferredOutboundLifecycleReg -> Array DeferredInboundReg -> Array IntegrationRegistrationEntry -> Maybe ReadinessConfig -> Application
[configSpec] :: Application -> Maybe ConfigSpec

-- | EventStore factory. Resolved during Application.run after config is
--   loaded.
[eventStoreFactory] :: Application -> Maybe EventStoreFactory
[queryObjectStoreConfig] :: Application -> Maybe QueryObjectStoreConfigValue
[queryDefinitions] :: Application -> Array QueryDefinition
[queryRegistry] :: Application -> QueryRegistry
[serviceRunners] :: Application -> Array ServiceRunner
[transports] :: Application -> Map Text TransportValue
[queryEndpoints] :: Application -> Map Text QueryEndpointHandler
[outboundRunners] :: Application -> Array OutboundRunner
[outboundLifecycleRunners] :: Application -> Array OutboundLifecycleRunner
[inboundIntegrations] :: Application -> Array Inbound

-- | WebAuth factory. Resolved during Application.run after config is
--   loaded.
[webAuthFactory] :: Application -> Maybe WebAuthFactory
[oauth2Setup] :: Application -> Maybe OAuth2Setup

-- | FileUpload factory. Resolved during Application.run after config is
--   loaded.
[fileUploadFactory] :: Application -> Maybe FileUploadFactory
[secretStore] :: Application -> Maybe SecretStore
[apiInfo] :: Application -> Maybe ApiInfo
[corsConfig] :: Application -> Maybe CorsConfig

-- | Health check configuration. Enabled by default at /health. Use
--   withHealthCheck to customize the path, or withoutHealthCheck to
--   disable.
[healthCheckConfig] :: Application -> Maybe HealthCheckConfig
[dispatcherConfig] :: Application -> Maybe DispatcherConfig
[corsFactory] :: Application -> Maybe CorsFactory
[apiInfoFactory] :: Application -> Maybe ApiInfoFactory
[healthCheckFactory] :: Application -> Maybe HealthCheckFactory
[dispatcherConfigFactory] :: Application -> Maybe DispatcherConfigFactory
[secretStoreFactory] :: Application -> Maybe SecretStoreFactory

-- | Deferred integration registrations. Resolved during Application.run
--   after config is loaded.
[deferredOutboundLifecycleRegs] :: Application -> Array DeferredOutboundLifecycleReg
[deferredInboundRegs] :: Application -> Array DeferredInboundReg

-- | ADR-0055 Integration registrations. Built at startup into a
--   DispatchRegistry.
[integrationRegistrations] :: Application -> Array IntegrationRegistrationEntry

-- | Readiness endpoint configuration. Enabled by default at /ready. Use
--   useReadinessEndpoint to customize.
[readinessConfig] :: Application -> Maybe ReadinessConfig

-- | Registration entry for an ADR-0055 Integration type. Stores the
--   integration name (for hybrid-mode and ERROR logging) and a function
--   that, given the active Selection, inserts the pre-bound closure into
--   the DispatchRegistry.
data IntegrationRegistrationEntry
IntegrationRegistrationEntry :: !Text -> (Selection -> DispatchRegistry -> DispatchRegistry) -> IntegrationRegistrationEntry
[integrationName] :: IntegrationRegistrationEntry -> !Text
[registerInto] :: IntegrationRegistrationEntry -> Selection -> DispatchRegistry -> DispatchRegistry

-- | A service runner that provides command endpoints grouped by transport.
--   
--   Services no longer run transports directly - instead, Application
--   collects all endpoints from all services and runs each transport once
--   with combined endpoints.
data ServiceRunner
ServiceRunner :: (EventStore Value -> Map Text TransportValue -> Task Text (Map Text (Map Text EndpointHandler), Map Text (Map Text EndpointSchema), Map Text EndpointHandler)) -> ServiceRunner

-- | Get command endpoints and schemas for this service, grouped by
--   transport name. Returns a tuple of (public handlers, public schemas,
--   dispatch handlers). The dispatch handlers are transport-independent
--   command handlers used by integrations.
[getEndpointsByTransport] :: ServiceRunner -> EventStore Value -> Map Text TransportValue -> Task Text (Map Text (Map Text EndpointHandler), Map Text (Map Text EndpointSchema), Map Text EndpointHandler)

-- | Configuration for WebTransport authentication. This is stored in the
--   Application (via factory) and converted to WebTransport.AuthEnabled at
--   runtime.
--   
--   When auth is enabled, all endpoints require a valid JWT by default.
--   Permission checks should be done in the command's decide method, not
--   at the transport layer.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Static URL:
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withTransport WebTransport.server
--     |&gt; Application.withAuth @() (\_ -&gt; "<a>https://auth.example.com")</a>
--   
--   -- Config-dependent URL:
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withConfig @AppConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withAuth (\cfg -&gt; cfg.authServerUrl)
--   </pre>
data WebAuthSetup
WebAuthSetup :: Text -> AuthOverrides -> WebAuthSetup

-- | OAuth provider URL (e.g., "<a>https://auth.example.com")</a>
[authServerUrl] :: WebAuthSetup -> Text

-- | Optional overrides for auth configuration
[authOverrides] :: WebAuthSetup -> AuthOverrides

-- | Configuration for OAuth2 provider integration.
--   
--   This is stored in the Application and converted to
--   WebTransport.OAuth2Config at runtime. OAuth2 routes require JWT
--   authentication to be enabled (via withAuth).
--   
--   SECURITY: The HMAC key is loaded from an environment variable at
--   runtime. This ensures the key is persistent across application
--   restarts.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   let ouraProvider = OAuth2ProviderConfig
--         { provider = Provider { name = "oura", ... }
--         , clientId = ClientId "your-client-id"
--         , ...
--         }
--   
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withTransport WebTransport.server
--     |&gt; Application.withAuth @() (\_ -&gt; "<a>https://auth.example.com")</a>
--     |&gt; Application.withOAuth2StateKey <a>OAUTH2_STATE_KEY</a>
--     |&gt; Application.withOAuth2Provider @() (\_ -&gt; ouraProvider)
--   </pre>
data OAuth2Setup
OAuth2Setup :: Text -> Array OAuth2ProviderFactory -> OAuth2Setup

-- | Environment variable name containing the HMAC secret (min 32 bytes)
[hmacKeyEnvVar] :: OAuth2Setup -> Text

-- | Configured OAuth2 provider factories
[providers] :: OAuth2Setup -> Array OAuth2ProviderFactory

-- | Factory for creating OAuth2 provider configuration.
--   
--   The factory is stored at <a>withOAuth2Provider</a> time and evaluated
--   during <a>run</a> after config is loaded (if needed).
data OAuth2ProviderFactory

-- | OAuth2 provider config evaluated at build time (when config type is
--   ())
[EvaluatedOAuth2Provider] :: !OAuth2ProviderConfig -> OAuth2ProviderFactory

-- | OAuth2 provider config deferred to run time (requires withConfig)
[DeferredOAuth2Provider] :: forall cfg. Typeable cfg => (cfg -> OAuth2ProviderConfig) -> OAuth2ProviderFactory

-- | API metadata for OpenAPI spec generation.
data ApiInfo
ApiInfo :: Text -> Text -> Text -> ApiInfo
[apiTitle] :: ApiInfo -> Text
[apiVersion] :: ApiInfo -> Text
[apiDescription] :: ApiInfo -> Text

-- | Default API info when none is provided.
defaultApiInfo :: ApiInfo

-- | Create a new empty Application.
--   
--   By default, queries use in-memory storage. Use
--   <a>withQueryObjectStore</a> to configure a different storage backend.
new :: Application

-- | Register application config for automatic loading.
--   
--   The config is loaded during <a>run</a> before any services start.
--   Config is parsed from CLI args, environment variables, and config
--   files.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withConfig @AppConfig
